Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: ```python\\n2: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n3: flockdesk.modules.presence.service\\n4: =================================\\n5: \\n6: PresenceService is responsible for tracking the local user\u2019s presence\\n7: status, broadcasting updates to the internal event-bus, and consuming\\n8: presence-events from other peers so that the rest of the desktop suite\\n9: (i.e. chat panes, user lists, dashboards) can present up-to-date\\n10: availability information.\\n11: \\n12: Key responsibilities\\n13: --------------------\\n14: 1. Detect local activity / idleness and derive an appropriate\\n15:    `PresenceState`.\\n16: 2. Expose an explicit `set_status` API so plugins (e.g. \u201cDo-Not-Disturb\\n17:    scheduler\u201d) or GUI controls can override the automatic heuristic.\\n18: 3. Maintain an in-memory registry of *all* known users\u2019 presence data.\\n19: 4. Publish `presence.updated` events and subscribe to the same topic for\\n20:    remote updates\u2014guaranteeing eventual consistency across processes.\\n21: 5. Provide a coroutine-driven lifecycle that can be started / stopped\\n22:    from the application\u2019s service-loader infrastructure.\\n23: \\n24: The implementation purposely avoids any hard dependency on a concrete\\n25: EventBus or IPC layer.  Instead, it relies on a lightweight protocol\\n26: interface (`EventBusProtocol`) so that different deployments (embedded\\n27: in-proc, ZeroMQ, or an HTTP bridge) can supply their own bus adapter.\\n28: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n29: \\n30: from __future__ import annotations\\n31: \\n32: import asyncio\\n33: import enum\\n34: import json\\n35: import logging\\n36: import sys\\n37: from dataclasses import dataclass, field\\n38: from datetime import datetime, timedelta\\n39: from types import TracebackType\\n40: from typing import (\\n41:     Any,\\n42:     Awaitable,\\n43:     Callable,\\n44:     Dict,\\n45:     MutableMapping,\\n46:     Optional,\\n47:     Protocol,\\n48:     Type,\\n49: )\\n50: \\n51: LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)\\n52: \\n53: \\n54: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n55: # Protocols & Data Models\\n56: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n57: class EventBusProtocol(Protocol):\\n58:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n59:     A minimal protocol any event-bus implementation has to satisfy so the\\n60:     PresenceService can remain transport-agnostic.\\n61:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n62: \\n63:     def subscribe(\\n64:         self, topic: str, callback: Callable[[str, Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]]\\n65:     ) -> None:  # pragma: no cover\\n66:         ...\\n67: \\n68:     async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:  # noqa: D401\\n69:         ...\\n70: \\n71: \\n72: class PresenceState(str, enum.Enum):\\n73:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Enumeration of all presence states supported by FlockDesk.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n74: \\n75:     ONLINE = \\\"online\\\"\\n76:     AWAY = \\\"away\\\"\\n77:     DND = \\\"dnd\\\"  # Do-Not-Disturb\\n78:     OFFLINE = \\\"offline\\\"\\n79: \\n80:     def __str__(self) -> str:\\n81:         return self.value\\n82: \\n83: \\n84: @dataclass(slots=True)\\n85: class PresenceInfo:\\n86:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n87:     Canonical presence payload that gets serialized to the event-bus.\\n88:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n89: \\n90:     user_id: str\\n91:     state: PresenceState\\n92:     updated_at: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)\\n93:     # Free-form metadata for plugins (e.g. custom emoji, busy_reason)\\n94:     metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\\n95: \\n96:     def to_json(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n97:         return {\\n98:             \\\"user_id\\\": self.user_id,\\n99:             \\\"state\\\": self.state.value,\\n100:             \\\"updated_at\\\": self.updated_at.isoformat(),\\n101:             \\\"metadata\\\": self.metadata,\\n102:         }\\n103: \\n104:     @classmethod\\n105:     def from_json(cls, raw: Dict[str, Any]) -> \\\"PresenceInfo\\\":\\n106:         return cls(\\n107:             user_id=raw[\\\"user_id\\\"],\\n108:             state=PresenceState(raw[\\\"state\\\"]),\\n109:             updated_at=datetime.fromisoformat(raw[\\\"updated_at\\\"]),\\n110:             metadata=raw.get(\\\"metadata\\\", {}),\\n111:         )\\n112: \\n113: \\n114: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n115: # Helper Utilities\\n116: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n117: def _system_idle_seconds() -> float:\\n118:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n119:     Cross-platform(ish) API for fetching idle time. We degrade gracefully to\\n120:     returning `0` when platform integration is unavailable rather than\\n121:     raising\u2014presence detection will still work albeit less smart.\\n122: \\n123:     Notes\\n124:     -----\\n125:     \u2022 Windows:   GetLastInputInfo via `ctypes`.\\n126:     \u2022 macOS:     IOKit wrapper (\u274c not yet implemented).\\n127:     \u2022 X11/Wayland:  XScreenSaver (\u274c not yet implemented).\\n128:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n129:     try:\\n130:         if sys.platform == \\\"win32\\\":\\n131:             import ctypes  # pylint: disable=import-error\\n132: \\n133:             class LASTINPUTINFO(ctypes.Structure):  # type: ignore\\n134:                 _fields_ = [(\\\"cbSize\\\", ctypes.c_uint), (\\\"dwTime\\\", ctypes.c_uint)]\\n135: \\n136:             plii = LASTINPUTINFO()\\n137:             plii.cbSize = ctypes.sizeof(plii)\\n138:             if ctypes.windll.user32.GetLastInputInfo(ctypes.byref(plii)) == 0:  # type: ignore  # noqa\\n139:                 return 0.0\\n140:             millis = ctypes.windll.kernel32.GetTickCount() - plii.dwTime  # type: ignore\\n141:             return millis / 1000.0\\n142:     except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\\n143:         LOGGER.debug(\\\"Idle time detection failed\\\", exc_info=True)\\n144: \\n145:     # Unsupported OS or error\u2014fallback.\\n146:     return 0.0\\n147: \\n148: \\n149: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n150: # Presence Service\\n151: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n152: class PresenceService:\\n153:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n154:     High-level fa\u00e7ade exposed to the rest of the desktop suite.\\n155: \\n156:     Lifecycle (`async with PresenceService(...):`) is preferred so that the\\n157:     activity watchdog coroutine shuts down cleanly during application exit.\\n158:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n159: \\n160:     HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL = timedelta(seconds=30)\\n161:     IDLE_THRESHOLD = timedelta(minutes=5)\\n162: \\n163:     def __init__(\\n164:         self,\\n165:         *,\\n166:         user_id: str,\\n167:         bus: EventBusProtocol,\\n168:         idle_provider: Callable[[], float] | None = None,\\n169:     ) -> None:\\n170:         self._user_id: str = user_id\\n171:         self._bus = bus\\n172:         self._idle_provider = idle_provider or _system_idle_seconds\\n173: \\n174:         self._registry: MutableMapping[str, PresenceInfo] = {}\\n175:         self._local_state: PresenceState = PresenceState.ONLINE\\n176:         self._override_state: PresenceState | None = None\\n177: \\n178:         # Concurrency\\n179:         self._loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()\\n180:         self._tasks: set[asyncio.Task[Any]] = set()\\n181:         self._stop_event = asyncio.Event()\\n182: \\n183:         # Register bus subscription early so we don't miss events during `await __aenter__`\\n184:         self._bus.subscribe(\\\"presence.updated\\\", self._on_presence_event)\\n185: \\n186:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n187:     # Context Manager Helpers\\n188:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n189:     async def __aenter__(self) -> \\\"PresenceService\\\":\\n190:         self.start()\\n191:         return self\\n192: \\n193:     async def __aexit__(\\n194:         self,\\n195:         exc_type: Type[BaseException] | None,\\n196:         exc: BaseException | None,\\n197:         tb: TracebackType | None,\\n198:     ) -> Optional[bool]:\\n199:         await self.stop()\\n200:         # Don't suppress any exception\\n201:         return None\\n202: \\n203:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n204:     # Public API\\n205:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n206:     def start(self) -> None:\\n207:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Spawn background coroutines (watchdog + heartbeat).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n208:         if self._tasks:\\n209:             return  # Already started\\n210:         LOGGER.debug(\\\"PresenceService starting\\\")\\n211:         self._tasks.add(self._loop.create_task(self._activity_watchdog()))\\n212:         self._tasks.add(self._loop.create_task(self._heartbeat_publisher()))\\n213: \\n214:     async def stop(self) -> None:\\n215:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Signal background tasks to stop and wait for graceful exit.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n216:         LOGGER.debug(\\\"PresenceService stopping\\\")\\n217:         self._stop_event.set()\\n218:         # Wait for tasks to finish with timeout\\n219:         await asyncio.wait(self._tasks, timeout=2.0)\\n220:         self._tasks.clear()\\n221: \\n222:     async def set_status(\\n223:         self,\\n224:         state: PresenceState,\\n225:         *,\\n226:         metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,\\n227:         override: bool = True,\\n228:     ) -> None:\\n229:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n230:         Explicitly set the local user\u2019s status.\\n231: \\n232:         Parameters\\n233:         ----------\\n234:         state:\\n235:             One of PresenceState values.\\n236:         metadata:\\n237:             Optional free-form metadata blob.\\n238:         override:\\n239:             If `True` automatic idle detection is disabled until\\n240:             `clear_manual_override()` is called.  This prevents race\\n241:             conditions when the user explicitly picks *DND* but is\\n242:             technically \u201cactive\u201d according to the heuristic.\\n243:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n244:         self._override_state = state if override else None\\n245:         await self._broadcast_local_state(state, metadata or {})\\n246: \\n247:     async def clear_manual_override(self) -> None:\\n248:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n249:         Re-enable automatic presence detection that may have been\\n250:         suspended via `set_status(..., override=True)`.\\n251:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n252:         LOGGER.debug(\\\"Presence manual override cleared\\\")\\n253:         self._override_state = None\\n254:         # Force immediate reconsideration\\n255:         await self._evaluate_local_state()\\n256: \\n257:     def get_presence(self, user_id: str) -> Optional[PresenceInfo]:\\n258:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Return the latest presence data we have for `user_id`.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n259:         return self._registry.get(user_id)\\n260: \\n261:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n262:     # Internal \u2013 Background Tasks\\n263:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n264:     async def _activity_watchdog(self) -> None:\\n265:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n266:         Periodically check system idle time and update `self._local_state`\\n267:         accordingly.\\n268:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n269:         LOGGER.debug(\\\"Presence watchdog started\\\")\\n270:         while not self._stop_event.is_set():\\n271:             try:\\n272:                 await self._evaluate_local_state()\\n273:             except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\\n274:                 LOGGER.exception(\\\"Presence watchdog error\\\")\\n275:             await asyncio.sleep(5)  # Poll frequency\\n276: \\n277:         LOGGER.debug(\\\"Presence watchdog terminated\\\")\\n278: \\n279:     async def _heartbeat_publisher(self) -> None:\\n280:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n281:         Consolidated heartbeat ensuring that *some* presence update is sent\\n282:         at least every `HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL`, even if the state hasn\u2019t\\n283:         changed.  This aids recovery in case any bus message was missed.\\n284:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n285:         LOGGER.debug(\\\"Presence heartbeat started\\\")\\n286:         while not self._stop_event.is_set():\\n287:             try:\\n288:                 await self._broadcast_local_state(self._local_state, {})\\n289:             except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\\n290:                 LOGGER.exception(\\\"Presence heartbeat error\\\")\\n291:             await asyncio.sleep(self.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL.total_seconds())\\n292: \\n293:         LOGGER.debug(\\\"Presence heartbeat terminated\\\")\\n294: \\n295:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n296:     # Internal \u2013 State Evaluation & Publishing\\n297:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n298:     async def _evaluate_local_state(self) -> None:\\n299:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n300:         Decide which `PresenceState` the local user should be in based on\\n301:         idle time and manual overrides.\\n302:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n303:         # Manual override takes precedence\\n304:         if self._override_state:\\n305:             state = self._override_state\\n306:         else:\\n307:             idle_seconds = self._idle_provider()\\n308:             state = (\\n309:                 PresenceState.AWAY\\n310:                 if idle_seconds >= self.IDLE_THRESHOLD.total_seconds()\\n311:                 else PresenceState.ONLINE\\n312:             )\\n313: \\n314:         if state != self._local_state:\\n315:             LOGGER.debug(\\n316:                 \\\"Presence state transition: %s \u2192 %s (override=%s)\\\",\\n317:                 self._local_state,\\n318:                 state,\\n319:                 bool(self._override_state),\\n320:             )\\n321:             await self._broadcast_local_state(state, {})\\n322: \\n323:     async def _broadcast_local_state(\\n324:         self, state: PresenceState, metadata: Dict[str, Any]\\n325:     ) -> None:\\n326:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n327:         Update internal registry and publish the change.\\n328:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n329:         self._local_state = state\\n330:         payload = PresenceInfo(\\n331:             user_id=self._user_id,\\n332:             state=state,\\n333:             metadata=metadata,\\n334:         )\\n335:         # Update local registry synchronously\\n336:         self._registry[self._user_id] = payload\\n337:         await self._bus.publish(\\\"presence.updated\\\", payload.to_json())\\n338: \\n339:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n340:     # Event-Bus Handlers\\n341:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n342:     async def _on_presence_event(self, topic: str, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\\n343:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n344:         Called for *all* presence.updated events\u2014including the one we just\\n345:         broadcast ourselves.  We update our registry and forward the\\n346:         information to any interested observers (plugins, UI model, etc.).\\n347:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n348:         try:\\n349:             info = PresenceInfo.from_json(data)\\n350:         except (ValueError, KeyError) as exc:\\n351:             LOGGER.warning(\\\"Malformed presence payload: %s (err=%s)\\\", data, exc)\\n352:             return\\n353: \\n354:         # Ignore outdated events \u2013 ensure monotonic update per user\\n355:         current = self._registry.get(info.user_id)\\n356:         if current and info.updated_at <= current.updated_at:\\n357:             return\\n358: \\n359:         self._registry[info.user_id] = info\\n360:         LOGGER.debug(\\n361:             \\\"Presence updated: %s state=%s meta=%s\\\",\\n362:             info.user_id,\\n363:             info.state,\\n364:             json.dumps(info.metadata, ensure_ascii=False),\\n365:         )\\n366: \\n367:         # Fan-out to local observers by publishing an *internal* asyncio.Event.\\n368:         # A more sophisticated implementation might rely on RxPy, signal\\n369:         # dispatchers, or other observer patterns.  For now we stick with the\\n370:         # event-bus only.\\n371:         # NOTE: Nothing to do here; UI layers will subscribe to the same bus\\n372:         # topic, or they can query `get_presence()` directly.\\n373: \\n374: ```\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021@16180b273d43410fcc114af7997ce2d2b28e392e/-/blob/flockdesk/modules/presence/service.py\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: ```python\n2: \"\"\"\n3: flockdesk.modules.presence.service\n4: =================================\n5: \n6: PresenceService is responsible for tracking the local user\u2019s presence\n7: status, broadcasting updates to the internal event-bus, and consuming\n8: presence-events from other peers so that the rest of the desktop suite\n9: (i.e. chat panes, user lists, dashboards) can present up-to-date\n10: availability information.\n11: \n12: Key responsibilities\n13: --------------------\n14: 1. Detect local activity / idleness and derive an appropriate\n15:    `PresenceState`.\n16: 2. Expose an explicit `set_status` API so plugins (e.g. \u201cDo-Not-Disturb\n17:    scheduler\u201d) or GUI controls can override the automatic heuristic.\n18: 3. Maintain an in-memory registry of *all* known users\u2019 presence data.\n19: 4. Publish `presence.updated` events and subscribe to the same topic for\n20:    remote updates\u2014guaranteeing eventual consistency across processes.\n21: 5. Provide a coroutine-driven lifecycle that can be started / stopped\n22:    from the application\u2019s service-loader infrastructure.\n23: \n24: The implementation purposely avoids any hard dependency on a concrete\n25: EventBus or IPC layer.  Instead, it relies on a lightweight protocol\n26: interface (`EventBusProtocol`) so that different deployments (embedded\n27: in-proc, ZeroMQ, or an HTTP bridge) can supply their own bus adapter.\n28: \"\"\"\n29: \n30: from __future__ import annotations\n31: \n32: import asyncio\n33: import enum\n34: import json\n35: import logging\n36: import sys\n37: from dataclasses import dataclass, field\n38: from datetime import datetime, timedelta\n39: from types import TracebackType\n40: from typing import (\n41:     Any,\n42:     Awaitable,\n43:     Callable,\n44:     Dict,\n45:     MutableMapping,\n46:     Optional,\n47:     Protocol,\n48:     Type,\n49: )\n50: \n51: LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n52: \n53: \n54: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n55: # Protocols & Data Models\n56: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n57: class EventBusProtocol(Protocol):\n58:     \"\"\"\n59:     A minimal protocol any event-bus implementation has to satisfy so the\n60:     PresenceService can remain transport-agnostic.\n61:     \"\"\"\n62: \n63:     def subscribe(\n64:         self, topic: str, callback: Callable[[str, Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]]\n65:     ) -> None:  # pragma: no cover\n66:         ...\n67: \n68:     async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:  # noqa: D401\n69:         ...\n70: \n71: \n72: class PresenceState(str, enum.Enum):\n73:     \"\"\"Enumeration of all presence states supported by FlockDesk.\"\"\"\n74: \n75:     ONLINE = \"online\"\n76:     AWAY = \"away\"\n77:     DND = \"dnd\"  # Do-Not-Disturb\n78:     OFFLINE = \"offline\"\n79: \n80:     def __str__(self) -> str:\n81:         return self.value\n82: \n83: \n84: @dataclass(slots=True)\n85: class PresenceInfo:\n86:     \"\"\"\n87:     Canonical presence payload that gets serialized to the event-bus.\n88:     \"\"\"\n89: \n90:     user_id: str\n91:     state: PresenceState\n92:     updated_at: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)\n93:     # Free-form metadata for plugins (e.g. custom emoji, busy_reason)\n94:     metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\n95: \n96:     def to_json(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n97:         return {\n98:             \"user_id\": self.user_id,\n99:             \"state\": self.state.value,\n100:             \"updated_at\": self.updated_at.isoformat(),\n101:             \"metadata\": self.metadata,\n102:         }\n103: \n104:     @classmethod\n105:     def from_json(cls, raw: Dict[str, Any]) -> \"PresenceInfo\":\n106:         return cls(\n107:             user_id=raw[\"user_id\"],\n108:             state=PresenceState(raw[\"state\"]),\n109:             updated_at=datetime.fromisoformat(raw[\"updated_at\"]),\n110:             metadata=raw.get(\"metadata\", {}),\n111:         )\n112: \n113: \n114: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n115: # Helper Utilities\n116: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n117: def _system_idle_seconds() -> float:\n118:     \"\"\"\n119:     Cross-platform(ish) API for fetching idle time. We degrade gracefully to\n120:     returning `0` when platform integration is unavailable rather than\n121:     raising\u2014presence detection will still work albeit less smart.\n122: \n123:     Notes\n124:     -----\n125:     \u2022 Windows:   GetLastInputInfo via `ctypes`.\n126:     \u2022 macOS:     IOKit wrapper (\u274c not yet implemented).\n127:     \u2022 X11/Wayland:  XScreenSaver (\u274c not yet implemented).\n128:     \"\"\"\n129:     try:\n130:         if sys.platform == \"win32\":\n131:             import ctypes  # pylint: disable=import-error\n132: \n133:             class LASTINPUTINFO(ctypes.Structure):  # type: ignore\n134:                 _fields_ = [(\"cbSize\", ctypes.c_uint), (\"dwTime\", ctypes.c_uint)]\n135: \n136:             plii = LASTINPUTINFO()\n137:             plii.cbSize = ctypes.sizeof(plii)\n138:             if ctypes.windll.user32.GetLastInputInfo(ctypes.byref(plii)) == 0:  # type: ignore  # noqa\n139:                 return 0.0\n140:             millis = ctypes.windll.kernel32.GetTickCount() - plii.dwTime  # type: ignore\n141:             return millis / 1000.0\n142:     except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n143:         LOGGER.debug(\"Idle time detection failed\", exc_info=True)\n144: \n145:     # Unsupported OS or error\u2014fallback.\n146:     return 0.0\n147: \n148: \n149: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n150: # Presence Service\n151: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n152: class PresenceService:\n153:     \"\"\"\n154:     High-level fa\u00e7ade exposed to the rest of the desktop suite.\n155: \n156:     Lifecycle (`async with PresenceService(...):`) is preferred so that the\n157:     activity watchdog coroutine shuts down cleanly during application exit.\n158:     \"\"\"\n159: \n160:     HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL = timedelta(seconds=30)\n161:     IDLE_THRESHOLD = timedelta(minutes=5)\n162: \n163:     def __init__(\n164:         self,\n165:         *,\n166:         user_id: str,\n167:         bus: EventBusProtocol,\n168:         idle_provider: Callable[[], float] | None = None,\n169:     ) -> None:\n170:         self._user_id: str = user_id\n171:         self._bus = bus\n172:         self._idle_provider = idle_provider or _system_idle_seconds\n173: \n174:         self._registry: MutableMapping[str, PresenceInfo] = {}\n175:         self._local_state: PresenceState = PresenceState.ONLINE\n176:         self._override_state: PresenceState | None = None\n177: \n178:         # Concurrency\n179:         self._loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()\n180:         self._tasks: set[asyncio.Task[Any]] = set()\n181:         self._stop_event = asyncio.Event()\n182: \n183:         # Register bus subscription early so we don't miss events during `await __aenter__`\n184:         self._bus.subscribe(\"presence.updated\", self._on_presence_event)\n185: \n186:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n187:     # Context Manager Helpers\n188:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n189:     async def __aenter__(self) -> \"PresenceService\":\n190:         self.start()\n191:         return self\n192: \n193:     async def __aexit__(\n194:         self,\n195:         exc_type: Type[BaseException] | None,\n196:         exc: BaseException | None,\n197:         tb: TracebackType | None,\n198:     ) -> Optional[bool]:\n199:         await self.stop()\n200:         # Don't suppress any exception\n201:         return None\n202: \n203:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n204:     # Public API\n205:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n206:     def start(self) -> None:\n207:         \"\"\"Spawn background coroutines (watchdog + heartbeat).\"\"\"\n208:         if self._tasks:\n209:             return  # Already started\n210:         LOGGER.debug(\"PresenceService starting\")\n211:         self._tasks.add(self._loop.create_task(self._activity_watchdog()))\n212:         self._tasks.add(self._loop.create_task(self._heartbeat_publisher()))\n213: \n214:     async def stop(self) -> None:\n215:         \"\"\"Signal background tasks to stop and wait for graceful exit.\"\"\"\n216:         LOGGER.debug(\"PresenceService stopping\")\n217:         self._stop_event.set()\n218:         # Wait for tasks to finish with timeout\n219:         await asyncio.wait(self._tasks, timeout=2.0)\n220:         self._tasks.clear()\n221: \n222:     async def set_status(\n223:         self,\n224:         state: PresenceState,\n225:         *,\n226:         metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,\n227:         override: bool = True,\n228:     ) -> None:\n229:         \"\"\"\n230:         Explicitly set the local user\u2019s status.\n231: \n232:         Parameters\n233:         ----------\n234:         state:\n235:             One of PresenceState values.\n236:         metadata:\n237:             Optional free-form metadata blob.\n238:         override:\n239:             If `True` automatic idle detection is disabled until\n240:             `clear_manual_override()` is called.  This prevents race\n241:             conditions when the user explicitly picks *DND* but is\n242:             technically \u201cactive\u201d according to the heuristic.\n243:         \"\"\"\n244:         self._override_state = state if override else None\n245:         await self._broadcast_local_state(state, metadata or {})\n246: \n247:     async def clear_manual_override(self) -> None:\n248:         \"\"\"\n249:         Re-enable automatic presence detection that may have been\n250:         suspended via `set_status(..., override=True)`.\n251:         \"\"\"\n252:         LOGGER.debug(\"Presence manual override cleared\")\n253:         self._override_state = None\n254:         # Force immediate reconsideration\n255:         await self._evaluate_local_state()\n256: \n257:     def get_presence(self, user_id: str) -> Optional[PresenceInfo]:\n258:         \"\"\"Return the latest presence data we have for `user_id`.\"\"\"\n259:         return self._registry.get(user_id)\n260: \n261:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n262:     # Internal \u2013 Background Tasks\n263:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n264:     async def _activity_watchdog(self) -> None:\n265:         \"\"\"\n266:         Periodically check system idle time and update `self._local_state`\n267:         accordingly.\n268:         \"\"\"\n269:         LOGGER.debug(\"Presence watchdog started\")\n270:         while not self._stop_event.is_set():\n271:             try:\n272:                 await self._evaluate_local_state()\n273:             except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n274:                 LOGGER.exception(\"Presence watchdog error\")\n275:             await asyncio.sleep(5)  # Poll frequency\n276: \n277:         LOGGER.debug(\"Presence watchdog terminated\")\n278: \n279:     async def _heartbeat_publisher(self) -> None:\n280:         \"\"\"\n281:         Consolidated heartbeat ensuring that *some* presence update is sent\n282:         at least every `HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL`, even if the state hasn\u2019t\n283:         changed.  This aids recovery in case any bus message was missed.\n284:         \"\"\"\n285:         LOGGER.debug(\"Presence heartbeat started\")\n286:         while not self._stop_event.is_set():\n287:             try:\n288:                 await self._broadcast_local_state(self._local_state, {})\n289:             except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\n290:                 LOGGER.exception(\"Presence heartbeat error\")\n291:             await asyncio.sleep(self.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL.total_seconds())\n292: \n293:         LOGGER.debug(\"Presence heartbeat terminated\")\n294: \n295:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n296:     # Internal \u2013 State Evaluation & Publishing\n297:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n298:     async def _evaluate_local_state(self) -> None:\n299:         \"\"\"\n300:         Decide which `PresenceState` the local user should be in based on\n301:         idle time and manual overrides.\n302:         \"\"\"\n303:         # Manual override takes precedence\n304:         if self._override_state:\n305:             state = self._override_state\n306:         else:\n307:             idle_seconds = self._idle_provider()\n308:             state = (\n309:                 PresenceState.AWAY\n310:                 if idle_seconds >= self.IDLE_THRESHOLD.total_seconds()\n311:                 else PresenceState.ONLINE\n312:             )\n313: \n314:         if state != self._local_state:\n315:             LOGGER.debug(\n316:                 \"Presence state transition: %s \u2192 %s (override=%s)\",\n317:                 self._local_state,\n318:                 state,\n319:                 bool(self._override_state),\n320:             )\n321:             await self._broadcast_local_state(state, {})\n322: \n323:     async def _broadcast_local_state(\n324:         self, state: PresenceState, metadata: Dict[str, Any]\n325:     ) -> None:\n326:         \"\"\"\n327:         Update internal registry and publish the change.\n328:         \"\"\"\n329:         self._local_state = state\n330:         payload = PresenceInfo(\n331:             user_id=self._user_id,\n332:             state=state,\n333:             metadata=metadata,\n334:         )\n335:         # Update local registry synchronously\n336:         self._registry[self._user_id] = payload\n337:         await self._bus.publish(\"presence.updated\", payload.to_json())\n338: \n339:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n340:     # Event-Bus Handlers\n341:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n342:     async def _on_presence_event(self, topic: str, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n343:         \"\"\"\n344:         Called for *all* presence.updated events\u2014including the one we just\n345:         broadcast ourselves.  We update our registry and forward the\n346:         information to any interested observers (plugins, UI model, etc.).\n347:         \"\"\"\n348:         try:\n349:             info = PresenceInfo.from_json(data)\n350:         except (ValueError, KeyError) as exc:\n351:             LOGGER.warning(\"Malformed presence payload: %s (err=%s)\", data, exc)\n352:             return\n353: \n354:         # Ignore outdated events \u2013 ensure monotonic update per user\n355:         current = self._registry.get(info.user_id)\n356:         if current and info.updated_at <= current.updated_at:\n357:             return\n358: \n359:         self._registry[info.user_id] = info\n360:         LOGGER.debug(\n361:             \"Presence updated: %s state=%s meta=%s\",\n362:             info.user_id,\n363:             info.state,\n364:             json.dumps(info.metadata, ensure_ascii=False),\n365:         )\n366: \n367:         # Fan-out to local observers by publishing an *internal* asyncio.Event.\n368:         # A more sophisticated implementation might rely on RxPy, signal\n369:         # dispatchers, or other observer patterns.  For now we stick with the\n370:         # event-bus only.\n371:         # NOTE: Nothing to do here; UI layers will subscribe to the same bus\n372:         # topic, or they can query `get_presence()` directly.\n373: \n374: ```",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021@16180b273d43410fcc114af7997ce2d2b28e392e/-/blob/flockdesk/modules/presence/service.py"
  }
}

11. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "flockdesk/core/app.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: ```python\\n2: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n3: flockdesk.core.app\\n4: ==================\\n5: \\n6: The central orchestration runtime for the FlockDesk desktop suite.\\n7: Responsible for:\\n8: \\n9: * Application-wide lifecycle & graceful shutdown\\n10: * Global event-bus for intra-service communication\\n11: * Plugin discovery, dependency wiring and hot-reload hooks\\n12: * Persisted user settings & dynamic preference updates\\n13: * Crash-reporting funnel to Sentry ( sandboxed per-service )\\n14: * Asyncio <-> Qt/GUI message-pump coherency\\n15: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n16: \\n17: from __future__ import annotations\\n18: \\n19: import asyncio\\n20: import importlib\\n21: import importlib.util\\n22: import json\\n23: import logging\\n24: import os\\n25: import signal\\n26: import sys\\n27: import traceback\\n28: from pathlib import Path\\n29: from types import ModuleType\\n30: from typing import (\\n31:     Any,\\n32:     Awaitable,\\n33:     Callable,\\n34:     Coroutine,\\n35:     Dict,\\n36:     Iterable,\\n37:     List,\\n38:     Optional,\\n39:     Protocol,\\n40:     Set,\\n41:     Tuple,\\n42:     Union,\\n43: )\\n44: \\n45: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n46: # Optional / soft dependencies                                               #\\n47: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n48: try:\\n49:     from PySide6.QtCore import QCoreApplication, QObject, QTimer  # type: ignore\\n50: except ModuleNotFoundError:  # Headless / non-GUI environments\\n51:     QCoreApplication = None  # type: ignore\\n52:     QObject = object  # type: ignore\\n53:     QTimer = None  # type: ignore\\n54: \\n55: try:\\n56:     import appdirs  # type: ignore\\n57: except ModuleNotFoundError:\\n58:     appdirs = None\\n59: \\n60: try:\\n61:     import sentry_sdk  # type: ignore\\n62: except ModuleNotFoundError:\\n63:     sentry_sdk = None  # noqa: N816\\n64: \\n65: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n66: # Logging setup                                                              #\\n67: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n68: logger = logging.getLogger(\\\"flockdesk.core\\\")\\n69: logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)\\n70: \\n71: _handler = logging.StreamHandler(stream=sys.stdout)\\n72: _handler.setFormatter(\\n73:     logging.Formatter(\\\"%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(name)s: %(message)s\\\")\\n74: )\\n75: logger.addHandler(_handler)\\n76: \\n77: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n78: # Typing                                                                     #\\n79: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n80: JSON_T = Union[str, int, float, bool, None, Dict[str, \\\"JSON_T\\\"], List[\\\"JSON_T\\\"]]\\n81: \\n82: \\n83: class Event:\\n84:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n85:     A lightweight event envelope exchanged over the FlockDesk event bus.\\n86:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n87: \\n88:     __slots__ = (\\\"topic\\\", \\\"payload\\\", \\\"meta\\\")\\n89: \\n90:     def __init__(self, topic: str, payload: Any = None, **meta: Any) -> None:\\n91:         self.topic: str = topic\\n92:         self.payload: Any = payload\\n93:         self.meta: Dict[str, Any] = meta\\n94: \\n95:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n96:     # Convenience helpers                                                   #\\n97:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n98:     def __repr__(self) -> str:  # pragma: no cover\\n99:         return f\\\"Event({self.topic!r}, payload={self.payload!r}, meta={self.meta!r})\\\"\\n100: \\n101: \\n102: Listener = Callable[[Event], Union[None, Awaitable[None]]]\\n103: \\n104: \\n105: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n106: # Event Bus Implementation                                                   #\\n107: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n108: class EventBus:\\n109:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n110:     A minimal in-process publish/subscribe mechanism.\\n111: \\n112:     The bus is intentionally *na\u00efve* but good enough for inter-plugin traffic.\\n113:     More advanced requirements (wildcards, QoS, type routing) can be layered\\n114:     on top by dedicated broker services.\\n115:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n116: \\n117:     def __init__(self) -> None:\\n118:         self._subscribers: Dict[str, Set[Listener]] = {}\\n119:         self._loop: asyncio.AbstractEventLoop = asyncio.get_event_loop()\\n120: \\n121:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n122:     # Subscription                                                          #\\n123:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n124:     def subscribe(self, topic: str, listener: Listener) -> None:\\n125:         listeners = self._subscribers.setdefault(topic, set())\\n126:         listeners.add(listener)\\n127:         logger.debug(\\\"Listener %s subscribed to topic %s\\\", listener, topic)\\n128: \\n129:     def unsubscribe(self, topic: str, listener: Listener) -> None:\\n130:         try:\\n131:             self._subscribers[topic].discard(listener)\\n132:             logger.debug(\\\"Listener %s unsubscribed from topic %s\\\", listener, topic)\\n133:         except KeyError:\\n134:             logger.warning(\\\"Attempted to unsubscribe unknown topic %s\\\", topic)\\n135: \\n136:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n137:     # Publishing                                                            #\\n138:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n139:     def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Any = None, **meta: Any) -> None:\\n140:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n141:         Schedule the dispatch of an event to all interested listeners.\\n142:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n143:         event = Event(topic, payload, **meta)\\n144:         logger.debug(\\\"Publishing event %s\\\", event)\\n145:         listeners = self._subscribers.get(topic, set()).copy()\\n146: \\n147:         for listener in listeners:\\n148:             try:\\n149:                 result = listener(event)\\n150:                 if asyncio.iscoroutine(result):\\n151:                     # Fire-and-forget, but capture exceptions.\\n152:                     task = self._loop.create_task(result)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n153:                     task.add_done_callback(self._capture_async_exception)\\n154:             except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\\n155:                 logger.exception(\\n156:                     \\\"Synchronous listener %s crashed while handling %s: %s\\\",\\n157:                     listener,\\n158:                     topic,\\n159:                     exc,\\n160:                 )\\n161: \\n162:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n163:     # Helpers                                                               #\\n164:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n165:     def _capture_async_exception(self, task: asyncio.Task[Any]) -> None:  # noqa: D401\\n166:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n167:         Funnel unhandled exceptions from background tasks into the logger.\\n168:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n169:         if task.cancelled():\\n170:             return\\n171:         if exc := task.exception():\\n172:             logger.exception(\\\"Async listener raised: %s\\\", exc)\\n173: \\n174: \\n175: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n176: # Plugin System                                                              #\\n177: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n178: class Plugin(Protocol):\\n179:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n180:     Public interface that all plugins must comply with.\\n181:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n182: \\n183:     name: str\\n184:     version: str\\n185: \\n186:     def setup(self, bus: EventBus) -> None: ...\\n187:     async def start(self) -> None: ...\\n188:     async def shutdown(self) -> None: ...\\n189: \\n190: \\n191: class PluginLoader:\\n192:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n193:     Discover & instantiate plugins.\\n194: \\n195:     *User* plugins are loaded from the following search order:\\n196: \\n197:     1. `$FLOCKDESK_PLUGINS` (semicolon separated list of paths)\\n198:     2. `~/.flockdesk/plugins`\\n199:     3. `<install-root>/flockdesk/plugins`\\n200:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n201: \\n202:     SEARCH_LOCATIONS: Tuple[Path, ...] = (\\n203:         Path(os.getenv(\\\"FLOCKDESK_PLUGINS\\\", \\\"\\\")),\\n204:         Path.home() / \\\".flockdesk\\\" / \\\"plugins\\\",\\n205:         Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / \\\"plugins\\\",\\n206:     )\\n207: \\n208:     def __init__(self, bus: EventBus) -> None:\\n209:         self._bus = bus\\n210:         self._plugins: List[Plugin] = []\\n211: \\n212:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n213:     # Discovery                                                             #\\n214:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n215:     def discover(self) -> List[Plugin]:\\n216:         logger.info(\\\"Discovering plugins \u2026\\\")\\n217:         for location in self.SEARCH_LOCATIONS:\\n218:             if not location or not location.exists():\\n219:                 continue\\n220:             for path in location.glob(\\\"*/__init__.py\\\"):\\n221:                 try:\\n222:                     plugin = self._load_module(path)\\n223:                     self._plugins.append(plugin)\\n224:                     logger.info(\\\"Loaded plugin: %s %s\\\", plugin.name, plugin.version)\\n225:                 except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\\n226:                     logger.error(\\n227:                         \\\"Failed to load plugin at %s:\\\\n%s\\\",\\n228:                         path,\\n229:                         traceback.format_exc(),\\n230:                     )\\n231:         return self._plugins\\n232: \\n233:     def _load_module(self, init_file: Path) -> Plugin:\\n234:         module_name = f\\\"flockdesk.plugin.{init_file.parent.name}\\\"\\n235:         spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(module_name, init_file)\\n236:         if not spec or not spec.loader:\\n237:             raise ImportError(f\\\"Cannot build spec for {init_file}\\\")\\n238:         module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)\\n239:         sys.modules[module_name] = module\\n240:         spec.loader.exec_module(module)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n241: \\n242:         if not hasattr(module, \\\"Plugin\\\"):\\n243:             raise AttributeError(\\n244:                 f\\\"{module_name} does not expose a 'Plugin' class\\\"\\n245:             )  # noqa: TRY003\\n246: \\n247:         plugin_cls = getattr(module, \\\"Plugin\\\")\\n248:         plugin: Plugin = plugin_cls()  # type: ignore[call-arg]\\n249: \\n250:         plugin.setup(self._bus)\\n251:         return plugin\\n252: \\n253:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n254:     # Lifecycle                                                             #\\n255:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n256:     async def start_all(self) -> None:\\n257:         for plugin in self._plugins:\\n258:             try:\\n259:                 await plugin.start()\\n260:                 logger.info(\\\"Plugin started: %s\\\", plugin.name)\\n261:             except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\\n262:                 logger.exception(\\\"Plugin %s failed to start\\\", plugin.name)\\n263: \\n264:     async def shutdown_all(self) -> None:\\n265:         for plugin in reversed(self._plugins):\\n266:             try:\\n267:                 await plugin.shutdown()\\n268:                 logger.info(\\\"Plugin stopped: %s\\\", plugin.name)\\n269:             except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\\n270:                 logger.exception(\\\"Plugin %s failed to shutdown\\\", plugin.name)\\n271: \\n272: \\n273: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n274: # Settings / Configuration                                                   #\\n275: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n276: class SettingsManager:\\n277:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n278:     Persisted key/value store backed by a JSON blob in the user's config dir.\\n279:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n280: \\n281:     FILE_NAME = \\\"settings.json\\\"\\n282: \\n283:     def __init__(self) -> None:\\n284:         self._data: Dict[str, JSON_T] = {}\\n285:         self._path = self._resolve_path()\\n286:         self._load()\\n287: \\n288:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n289:     # Public API                                                            #\\n290:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n291:     def get(self, key: str, default: JSON_T | None = None) -> JSON_T | None:\\n292:         return self._data.get(key, default)\\n293: \\n294:     def set(self, key: str, value: JSON_T) -> None:\\n295:         self._data[key] = value\\n296:         self._save_async()\\n297: \\n298:     def all(self) -> Dict[str, JSON_T]:\\n299:         return dict(self._data)\\n300: \\n301:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n302:     # Persistence                                                           #\\n303:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n304:     def _load(self) -> None:\\n305:         try:\\n306:             if self._path.exists():\\n307:                 self._data = json.loads(self._path.read_text(encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\"))\\n308:                 logger.debug(\\\"Settings loaded from %s\\\", self._path)\\n309:         except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError) as exc:\\n310:             logger.warning(\\\"Failed to load settings (%s), starting fresh\\\", exc)\\n311:             self._data = {}\\n312: \\n313:     def _save_async(self) -> None:\\n314:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n315:         Off-load writing to disk to avoid blocking the GUI / event-loop.\\n316:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n317: \\n318:         async def _writer() -> None:\\n319:             try:\\n320:                 self._path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n321:                 self._path.write_text(json.dumps(self._data, indent=2), encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n322:                 logger.debug(\\\"Settings persisted to %s\\\", self._path)\\n323:             except OSError as exc:\\n324:                 logger.error(\\\"Could not persist settings: %s\\\", exc)\\n325: \\n326:         asyncio.get_event_loop().create_task(_writer())\\n327: \\n328:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n329:     # Helpers                                                               #\\n330:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n331:     @staticmethod\\n332:     def _resolve_path() -> Path:\\n333:         if appdirs:\\n334:             cfg_dir = Path(appdirs.user_config_dir(\\\"FlockDesk\\\", \\\"Flockware\\\"))\\n335:         else:\\n336:             cfg_dir = Path.home() / \\\".config\\\" / \\\"flockdesk\\\"\\n337:         return cfg_dir / SettingsManager.FILE_NAME\\n338: \\n339: \\n340: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n341: # Crash / Diagnostics                                                        #\\n342: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n343: class CrashReporter:\\n344:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n345:     Thin wrapper around Sentry so we can easily replace/disable later on.\\n346:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n347: \\n348:     def __init__(self, dsn: Optional[str] = None) -> None:\\n349:         self._enabled: bool = False\\n350:         if dsn and sentry_sdk:\\n351:             sentry_sdk.init(dsn=dsn, release=\\\"flockdesk@1.0.0\\\")\\n352:             self._enabled = True\\n353:             logger.info(\\\"Crash reporting enabled\\\")\\n354:         elif dsn:\\n355:             logger.warning(\\\"sentry-sdk not installed, crash reporting disabled\\\")\\n356: \\n357:     async def capture_exception(self, exc: BaseException) -> None:\\n358:         if self._enabled:\\n359:             sentry_sdk.capture_exception(exc)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n360: \\n361: \\n362: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n363: # Core Application                                                           #\\n364: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n365: class FlockDeskCoreApp(QObject if QObject is not object else object):  # type: ignore[misc]\\n366:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n367:     Central bootstrap for FlockDesk.\\n368: \\n369:     Spins up the event-bus, plugin runtime, setting manager, and bridges\\n370:     between the asyncio reactor and Qt's message-pump if available.\\n371:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n372: \\n373:     def __init__(self, *, headless: bool = False) -> None:\\n374:         if not headless and QCoreApplication is None:\\n375:             raise RuntimeError(\\n376:                 \\\"PySide6 not installed \u2011 cannot run in GUI mode. \\\"\\n377:                 \\\"Pass `headless=True` to run without Qt.\\\"\\n378:             )\\n379: \\n380:         super().__init__()  # type: ignore[misc]\\n381:         self._headless = headless\\n382:         self._loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()\\n383:         asyncio.set_event_loop(self._loop)\\n384: \\n385:         self.bus = EventBus()\\n386:         self.settings = SettingsManager()\\n387:         self.crash = CrashReporter(\\n388:             dsn=self.settings.get(\\\"sentry_dsn\\\")  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n389:         )\\n390:         self._plugin_loader = PluginLoader(self.bus)\\n391: \\n392:         self._closing = asyncio.Event()\\n393: \\n394:         # Connect SIGINT / SIGTERM\\n395:         for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):\\n396:             signal.signal(sig, self._signal_handler)\\n397: \\n398:         if not self._headless:\\n399:             self._qt_app: QCoreApplication = QCoreApplication(sys.argv)  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n400:             # Pump asyncio via Qt's event loop every 15ms\\n401:             QTimer.singleShot(0, self._qt_asyncio_integration_tick)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n402: \\n403:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n404:     # Bootstrap                                                             #\\n405:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n406:     def run(self) -> None:\\n407:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n408:         Synchronous entry-point; block until shutdown completes.\\n409:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n410:         logger.info(\\\"Starting FlockDesk \u2026\\\")\\n411:         try:\\n412:             self._loop.run_until_complete(self._async_main())\\n413:         finally:\\n414:             self._loop.run_until_complete(self._loop.shutdown_asyncgens())\\n415:             self._loop.close()\\n416:             logger.info(\\\"FlockDesk stopped\\\")\\n417: \\n418:     async def _async_main(self) -> None:\\n419:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n420:         Coroutine that manages the asynchronous lifecycle.\\n421:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n422:         # Plugin discovery/startup\\n423:         self._plugin_loader.discover()\\n424:         await self._plugin_loader.start_all()\\n425: \\n426:         # Run until a shutdown signal is received\\n427:         await self._closing.wait()\\n428: \\n429:         # Tear-down\\n430:         await self._plugin_loader.shutdown_all()\\n431: \\n432:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n433:     # Shutdown & Signals                                                    #\\n434:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n435:     def _signal_handler(self, signum: int, _frame: Any | None = None) -> None:\\n436:         logger.info(\\\"Received signal %s, shutting down \u2026\\\", signum)\\n437:         self._loop.call_soon_threadsafe(self._closing.set)\\n438: \\n439:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n440:     # Qt / Async Integration                                                #\\n441:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n442:     def _qt_asyncio_integration_tick(self) -> None:  # pragma: no cover\\n443:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n444:         Execute all asyncio tasks waiting on the loop & re-schedule next tick.\\n445:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n446:         try:\\n447:             self._loop.call_soon(self._loop.stop)\\n448:             self._loop.run_forever()\\n449:         except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\\n450:             logger.exception(\\\"Error during asyncio-Qt integration tick: %s\\\", exc)\\n451:             self._loop.create_task(self.crash.capture_exception(exc))\\n452:         finally:\\n453:             # Re-schedule as long as the application is alive\\n454:             if not self._closing.is_set():\\n455:                 QTimer.singleShot(15, self._qt_asyncio_integration_tick)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n456: \\n457:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n458:     # Convenience helpers                                                   #\\n459:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n460:     def quit(self) -> None:\\n461:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n462:         Can be called by plugins/UI to request a graceful shutdown.\\n463:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n464:         self._closing.set()\\n465: \\n466: \\n467: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n468: # Entrypoint                                                                 #\\n469: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n470: def _main() -> None:  # pragma: no cover\\n471:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n472:     CLI bootstrap mainly for development/testing purposes.\\n473:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n474:     import argparse\\n475: \\n476:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\\\"Launch FlockDesk core service\\\")\\n477:     parser.add_argument(\\n478:         \\\"--headless\\\",\\n479:         action=\\\"store_true\\\",\\n480:         help=\\\"Run without Qt (no GUI) \u2013 suitable for CI or server mode.\\\",\\n481:     )\\n482:     args = parser.parse_args()\\n483: \\n484:     app = FlockDeskCoreApp(headless=args.headless)\\n485:     app.run()\\n486: \\n487: \\n488: if __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n489:     _main()\\n490: ```\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021@16180b273d43410fcc114af7997ce2d2b28e392e/-/blob/flockdesk/core/app.py\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: ```python\n2: \"\"\"\n3: flockdesk.core.app\n4: ==================\n5: \n6: The central orchestration runtime for the FlockDesk desktop suite.\n7: Responsible for:\n8: \n9: * Application-wide lifecycle & graceful shutdown\n10: * Global event-bus for intra-service communication\n11: * Plugin discovery, dependency wiring and hot-reload hooks\n12: * Persisted user settings & dynamic preference updates\n13: * Crash-reporting funnel to Sentry ( sandboxed per-service )\n14: * Asyncio <-> Qt/GUI message-pump coherency\n15: \"\"\"\n16: \n17: from __future__ import annotations\n18: \n19: import asyncio\n20: import importlib\n21: import importlib.util\n22: import json\n23: import logging\n24: import os\n25: import signal\n26: import sys\n27: import traceback\n28: from pathlib import Path\n29: from types import ModuleType\n30: from typing import (\n31:     Any,\n32:     Awaitable,\n33:     Callable,\n34:     Coroutine,\n35:     Dict,\n36:     Iterable,\n37:     List,\n38:     Optional,\n39:     Protocol,\n40:     Set,\n41:     Tuple,\n42:     Union,\n43: )\n44: \n45: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n46: # Optional / soft dependencies                                               #\n47: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n48: try:\n49:     from PySide6.QtCore import QCoreApplication, QObject, QTimer  # type: ignore\n50: except ModuleNotFoundError:  # Headless / non-GUI environments\n51:     QCoreApplication = None  # type: ignore\n52:     QObject = object  # type: ignore\n53:     QTimer = None  # type: ignore\n54: \n55: try:\n56:     import appdirs  # type: ignore\n57: except ModuleNotFoundError:\n58:     appdirs = None\n59: \n60: try:\n61:     import sentry_sdk  # type: ignore\n62: except ModuleNotFoundError:\n63:     sentry_sdk = None  # noqa: N816\n64: \n65: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n66: # Logging setup                                                              #\n67: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n68: logger = logging.getLogger(\"flockdesk.core\")\n69: logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)\n70: \n71: _handler = logging.StreamHandler(stream=sys.stdout)\n72: _handler.setFormatter(\n73:     logging.Formatter(\"%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(name)s: %(message)s\")\n74: )\n75: logger.addHandler(_handler)\n76: \n77: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n78: # Typing                                                                     #\n79: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n80: JSON_T = Union[str, int, float, bool, None, Dict[str, \"JSON_T\"], List[\"JSON_T\"]]\n81: \n82: \n83: class Event:\n84:     \"\"\"\n85:     A lightweight event envelope exchanged over the FlockDesk event bus.\n86:     \"\"\"\n87: \n88:     __slots__ = (\"topic\", \"payload\", \"meta\")\n89: \n90:     def __init__(self, topic: str, payload: Any = None, **meta: Any) -> None:\n91:         self.topic: str = topic\n92:         self.payload: Any = payload\n93:         self.meta: Dict[str, Any] = meta\n94: \n95:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n96:     # Convenience helpers                                                   #\n97:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n98:     def __repr__(self) -> str:  # pragma: no cover\n99:         return f\"Event({self.topic!r}, payload={self.payload!r}, meta={self.meta!r})\"\n100: \n101: \n102: Listener = Callable[[Event], Union[None, Awaitable[None]]]\n103: \n104: \n105: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n106: # Event Bus Implementation                                                   #\n107: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n108: class EventBus:\n109:     \"\"\"\n110:     A minimal in-process publish/subscribe mechanism.\n111: \n112:     The bus is intentionally *na\u00efve* but good enough for inter-plugin traffic.\n113:     More advanced requirements (wildcards, QoS, type routing) can be layered\n114:     on top by dedicated broker services.\n115:     \"\"\"\n116: \n117:     def __init__(self) -> None:\n118:         self._subscribers: Dict[str, Set[Listener]] = {}\n119:         self._loop: asyncio.AbstractEventLoop = asyncio.get_event_loop()\n120: \n121:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n122:     # Subscription                                                          #\n123:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n124:     def subscribe(self, topic: str, listener: Listener) -> None:\n125:         listeners = self._subscribers.setdefault(topic, set())\n126:         listeners.add(listener)\n127:         logger.debug(\"Listener %s subscribed to topic %s\", listener, topic)\n128: \n129:     def unsubscribe(self, topic: str, listener: Listener) -> None:\n130:         try:\n131:             self._subscribers[topic].discard(listener)\n132:             logger.debug(\"Listener %s unsubscribed from topic %s\", listener, topic)\n133:         except KeyError:\n134:             logger.warning(\"Attempted to unsubscribe unknown topic %s\", topic)\n135: \n136:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n137:     # Publishing                                                            #\n138:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n139:     def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Any = None, **meta: Any) -> None:\n140:         \"\"\"\n141:         Schedule the dispatch of an event to all interested listeners.\n142:         \"\"\"\n143:         event = Event(topic, payload, **meta)\n144:         logger.debug(\"Publishing event %s\", event)\n145:         listeners = self._subscribers.get(topic, set()).copy()\n146: \n147:         for listener in listeners:\n148:             try:\n149:                 result = listener(event)\n150:                 if asyncio.iscoroutine(result):\n151:                     # Fire-and-forget, but capture exceptions.\n152:                     task = self._loop.create_task(result)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n153:                     task.add_done_callback(self._capture_async_exception)\n154:             except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n155:                 logger.exception(\n156:                     \"Synchronous listener %s crashed while handling %s: %s\",\n157:                     listener,\n158:                     topic,\n159:                     exc,\n160:                 )\n161: \n162:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n163:     # Helpers                                                               #\n164:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n165:     def _capture_async_exception(self, task: asyncio.Task[Any]) -> None:  # noqa: D401\n166:         \"\"\"\n167:         Funnel unhandled exceptions from background tasks into the logger.\n168:         \"\"\"\n169:         if task.cancelled():\n170:             return\n171:         if exc := task.exception():\n172:             logger.exception(\"Async listener raised: %s\", exc)\n173: \n174: \n175: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n176: # Plugin System                                                              #\n177: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n178: class Plugin(Protocol):\n179:     \"\"\"\n180:     Public interface that all plugins must comply with.\n181:     \"\"\"\n182: \n183:     name: str\n184:     version: str\n185: \n186:     def setup(self, bus: EventBus) -> None: ...\n187:     async def start(self) -> None: ...\n188:     async def shutdown(self) -> None: ...\n189: \n190: \n191: class PluginLoader:\n192:     \"\"\"\n193:     Discover & instantiate plugins.\n194: \n195:     *User* plugins are loaded from the following search order:\n196: \n197:     1. `$FLOCKDESK_PLUGINS` (semicolon separated list of paths)\n198:     2. `~/.flockdesk/plugins`\n199:     3. `<install-root>/flockdesk/plugins`\n200:     \"\"\"\n201: \n202:     SEARCH_LOCATIONS: Tuple[Path, ...] = (\n203:         Path(os.getenv(\"FLOCKDESK_PLUGINS\", \"\")),\n204:         Path.home() / \".flockdesk\" / \"plugins\",\n205:         Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / \"plugins\",\n206:     )\n207: \n208:     def __init__(self, bus: EventBus) -> None:\n209:         self._bus = bus\n210:         self._plugins: List[Plugin] = []\n211: \n212:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n213:     # Discovery                                                             #\n214:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n215:     def discover(self) -> List[Plugin]:\n216:         logger.info(\"Discovering plugins \u2026\")\n217:         for location in self.SEARCH_LOCATIONS:\n218:             if not location or not location.exists():\n219:                 continue\n220:             for path in location.glob(\"*/__init__.py\"):\n221:                 try:\n222:                     plugin = self._load_module(path)\n223:                     self._plugins.append(plugin)\n224:                     logger.info(\"Loaded plugin: %s %s\", plugin.name, plugin.version)\n225:                 except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n226:                     logger.error(\n227:                         \"Failed to load plugin at %s:\\n%s\",\n228:                         path,\n229:                         traceback.format_exc(),\n230:                     )\n231:         return self._plugins\n232: \n233:     def _load_module(self, init_file: Path) -> Plugin:\n234:         module_name = f\"flockdesk.plugin.{init_file.parent.name}\"\n235:         spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(module_name, init_file)\n236:         if not spec or not spec.loader:\n237:             raise ImportError(f\"Cannot build spec for {init_file}\")\n238:         module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)\n239:         sys.modules[module_name] = module\n240:         spec.loader.exec_module(module)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n241: \n242:         if not hasattr(module, \"Plugin\"):\n243:             raise AttributeError(\n244:                 f\"{module_name} does not expose a 'Plugin' class\"\n245:             )  # noqa: TRY003\n246: \n247:         plugin_cls = getattr(module, \"Plugin\")\n248:         plugin: Plugin = plugin_cls()  # type: ignore[call-arg]\n249: \n250:         plugin.setup(self._bus)\n251:         return plugin\n252: \n253:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n254:     # Lifecycle                                                             #\n255:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n256:     async def start_all(self) -> None:\n257:         for plugin in self._plugins:\n258:             try:\n259:                 await plugin.start()\n260:                 logger.info(\"Plugin started: %s\", plugin.name)\n261:             except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n262:                 logger.exception(\"Plugin %s failed to start\", plugin.name)\n263: \n264:     async def shutdown_all(self) -> None:\n265:         for plugin in reversed(self._plugins):\n266:             try:\n267:                 await plugin.shutdown()\n268:                 logger.info(\"Plugin stopped: %s\", plugin.name)\n269:             except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n270:                 logger.exception(\"Plugin %s failed to shutdown\", plugin.name)\n271: \n272: \n273: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n274: # Settings / Configuration                                                   #\n275: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n276: class SettingsManager:\n277:     \"\"\"\n278:     Persisted key/value store backed by a JSON blob in the user's config dir.\n279:     \"\"\"\n280: \n281:     FILE_NAME = \"settings.json\"\n282: \n283:     def __init__(self) -> None:\n284:         self._data: Dict[str, JSON_T] = {}\n285:         self._path = self._resolve_path()\n286:         self._load()\n287: \n288:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n289:     # Public API                                                            #\n290:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n291:     def get(self, key: str, default: JSON_T | None = None) -> JSON_T | None:\n292:         return self._data.get(key, default)\n293: \n294:     def set(self, key: str, value: JSON_T) -> None:\n295:         self._data[key] = value\n296:         self._save_async()\n297: \n298:     def all(self) -> Dict[str, JSON_T]:\n299:         return dict(self._data)\n300: \n301:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n302:     # Persistence                                                           #\n303:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n304:     def _load(self) -> None:\n305:         try:\n306:             if self._path.exists():\n307:                 self._data = json.loads(self._path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n308:                 logger.debug(\"Settings loaded from %s\", self._path)\n309:         except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError) as exc:\n310:             logger.warning(\"Failed to load settings (%s), starting fresh\", exc)\n311:             self._data = {}\n312: \n313:     def _save_async(self) -> None:\n314:         \"\"\"\n315:         Off-load writing to disk to avoid blocking the GUI / event-loop.\n316:         \"\"\"\n317: \n318:         async def _writer() -> None:\n319:             try:\n320:                 self._path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n321:                 self._path.write_text(json.dumps(self._data, indent=2), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n322:                 logger.debug(\"Settings persisted to %s\", self._path)\n323:             except OSError as exc:\n324:                 logger.error(\"Could not persist settings: %s\", exc)\n325: \n326:         asyncio.get_event_loop().create_task(_writer())\n327: \n328:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n329:     # Helpers                                                               #\n330:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n331:     @staticmethod\n332:     def _resolve_path() -> Path:\n333:         if appdirs:\n334:             cfg_dir = Path(appdirs.user_config_dir(\"FlockDesk\", \"Flockware\"))\n335:         else:\n336:             cfg_dir = Path.home() / \".config\" / \"flockdesk\"\n337:         return cfg_dir / SettingsManager.FILE_NAME\n338: \n339: \n340: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n341: # Crash / Diagnostics                                                        #\n342: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n343: class CrashReporter:\n344:     \"\"\"\n345:     Thin wrapper around Sentry so we can easily replace/disable later on.\n346:     \"\"\"\n347: \n348:     def __init__(self, dsn: Optional[str] = None) -> None:\n349:         self._enabled: bool = False\n350:         if dsn and sentry_sdk:\n351:             sentry_sdk.init(dsn=dsn, release=\"flockdesk@1.0.0\")\n352:             self._enabled = True\n353:             logger.info(\"Crash reporting enabled\")\n354:         elif dsn:\n355:             logger.warning(\"sentry-sdk not installed, crash reporting disabled\")\n356: \n357:     async def capture_exception(self, exc: BaseException) -> None:\n358:         if self._enabled:\n359:             sentry_sdk.capture_exception(exc)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n360: \n361: \n362: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n363: # Core Application                                                           #\n364: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n365: class FlockDeskCoreApp(QObject if QObject is not object else object):  # type: ignore[misc]\n366:     \"\"\"\n367:     Central bootstrap for FlockDesk.\n368: \n369:     Spins up the event-bus, plugin runtime, setting manager, and bridges\n370:     between the asyncio reactor and Qt's message-pump if available.\n371:     \"\"\"\n372: \n373:     def __init__(self, *, headless: bool = False) -> None:\n374:         if not headless and QCoreApplication is None:\n375:             raise RuntimeError(\n376:                 \"PySide6 not installed \u2011 cannot run in GUI mode. \"\n377:                 \"Pass `headless=True` to run without Qt.\"\n378:             )\n379: \n380:         super().__init__()  # type: ignore[misc]\n381:         self._headless = headless\n382:         self._loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()\n383:         asyncio.set_event_loop(self._loop)\n384: \n385:         self.bus = EventBus()\n386:         self.settings = SettingsManager()\n387:         self.crash = CrashReporter(\n388:             dsn=self.settings.get(\"sentry_dsn\")  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n389:         )\n390:         self._plugin_loader = PluginLoader(self.bus)\n391: \n392:         self._closing = asyncio.Event()\n393: \n394:         # Connect SIGINT / SIGTERM\n395:         for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):\n396:             signal.signal(sig, self._signal_handler)\n397: \n398:         if not self._headless:\n399:             self._qt_app: QCoreApplication = QCoreApplication(sys.argv)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n400:             # Pump asyncio via Qt's event loop every 15ms\n401:             QTimer.singleShot(0, self._qt_asyncio_integration_tick)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n402: \n403:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n404:     # Bootstrap                                                             #\n405:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n406:     def run(self) -> None:\n407:         \"\"\"\n408:         Synchronous entry-point; block until shutdown completes.\n409:         \"\"\"\n410:         logger.info(\"Starting FlockDesk \u2026\")\n411:         try:\n412:             self._loop.run_until_complete(self._async_main())\n413:         finally:\n414:             self._loop.run_until_complete(self._loop.shutdown_asyncgens())\n415:             self._loop.close()\n416:             logger.info(\"FlockDesk stopped\")\n417: \n418:     async def _async_main(self) -> None:\n419:         \"\"\"\n420:         Coroutine that manages the asynchronous lifecycle.\n421:         \"\"\"\n422:         # Plugin discovery/startup\n423:         self._plugin_loader.discover()\n424:         await self._plugin_loader.start_all()\n425: \n426:         # Run until a shutdown signal is received\n427:         await self._closing.wait()\n428: \n429:         # Tear-down\n430:         await self._plugin_loader.shutdown_all()\n431: \n432:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n433:     # Shutdown & Signals                                                    #\n434:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n435:     def _signal_handler(self, signum: int, _frame: Any | None = None) -> None:\n436:         logger.info(\"Received signal %s, shutting down \u2026\", signum)\n437:         self._loop.call_soon_threadsafe(self._closing.set)\n438: \n439:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n440:     # Qt / Async Integration                                                #\n441:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n442:     def _qt_asyncio_integration_tick(self) -> None:  # pragma: no cover\n443:         \"\"\"\n444:         Execute all asyncio tasks waiting on the loop & re-schedule next tick.\n445:         \"\"\"\n446:         try:\n447:             self._loop.call_soon(self._loop.stop)\n448:             self._loop.run_forever()\n449:         except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n450:             logger.exception(\"Error during asyncio-Qt integration tick: %s\", exc)\n451:             self._loop.create_task(self.crash.capture_exception(exc))\n452:         finally:\n453:             # Re-schedule as long as the application is alive\n454:             if not self._closing.is_set():\n455:                 QTimer.singleShot(15, self._qt_asyncio_integration_tick)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n456: \n457:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n458:     # Convenience helpers                                                   #\n459:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n460:     def quit(self) -> None:\n461:         \"\"\"\n462:         Can be called by plugins/UI to request a graceful shutdown.\n463:         \"\"\"\n464:         self._closing.set()\n465: \n466: \n467: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n468: # Entrypoint                                                                 #\n469: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n470: def _main() -> None:  # pragma: no cover\n471:     \"\"\"\n472:     CLI bootstrap mainly for development/testing purposes.\n473:     \"\"\"\n474:     import argparse\n475: \n476:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Launch FlockDesk core service\")\n477:     parser.add_argument(\n478:         \"--headless\",\n479:         action=\"store_true\",\n480:         help=\"Run without Qt (no GUI) \u2013 suitable for CI or server mode.\",\n481:     )\n482:     args = parser.parse_args()\n483: \n484:     app = FlockDeskCoreApp(headless=args.headless)\n485:     app.run()\n486: \n487: \n488: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n489:     _main()\n490: ```",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021@16180b273d43410fcc114af7997ce2d2b28e392e/-/blob/flockdesk/core/app.py"
  }
}

13. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input

  "path": "flockdesk/modules/chat/viewmodel/chat_vm.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021"

Output
{
