  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: ```python\\n2: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n3: FlockDesk \u2013 Chat Module\\n4: View-Model (MVVM) for the Chat micro-front-end.\\n5: \\n6: This module owns the presentation-ready state of a single chat channel.\\n7: It keeps the Qt View in sync with the domain layer through an internal\\n8: event-bus, handles optimistic UI updates, provides command objects for\\n9: the Controller, and persists transient UI data such as unsent drafts.\\n10: \\n11: Author: FlockDesk Core Team\\n12: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n13: \\n14: from __future__ import annotations\\n15: \\n16: import json\\n17: import logging\\n18: import threading\\n19: from dataclasses import dataclass, field\\n20: from datetime import datetime, timezone\\n21: from pathlib import Path\\n22: from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional\\n23: \\n24: from PySide6 import QtCore\\n25: \\n26: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n27: # External / Project-wide Dependencies\\n28: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n29: try:\\n30:     from flockdesk.core.events import EventBus, Event  # type: ignore\\n31:     from flockdesk.core.settings import settings  # type: ignore\\n32:     from flockdesk.core.exceptions import ChatSendError  # type: ignore\\n33: except ModuleNotFoundError:  # Fallbacks for static type checkers / docs\\n34:     EventBus = Any  # pragma: no cover\\n35:     Event = Any  # pragma: no cover\\n36:     settings = {}  # pragma: no cover\\n37:     ChatSendError = Exception  # pragma: no cover\\n38: \\n39: \\n40: _log = logging.getLogger(__name__)\\n41: \\n42: \\n43: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n44: # Data Models\\n45: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n46: @dataclass(slots=True, frozen=True)\\n47: class ChatMessage:\\n48:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Immutably represents a message entity in the chat session.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n49: \\n50:     msg_id: str\\n51:     channel_id: str\\n52:     sender_id: str\\n53:     sender_display: str\\n54:     content: str\\n55:     timestamp: datetime\\n56:     edited: bool = False\\n57:     is_outgoing: bool = False\\n58:     attachments: List[Path] = field(default_factory=list)\\n59: \\n60:     def serialize(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n61:         return {\\n62:             \\\"msg_id\\\": self.msg_id,\\n63:             \\\"channel_id\\\": self.channel_id,\\n64:             \\\"sender_id\\\": self.sender_id,\\n65:             \\\"sender_display\\\": self.sender_display,\\n66:             \\\"content\\\": self.content,\\n67:             \\\"timestamp\\\": self.timestamp.isoformat(),\\n68:             \\\"edited\\\": self.edited,\\n69:             \\\"is_outgoing\\\": self.is_outgoing,\\n70:             \\\"attachments\\\": [str(p) for p in self.attachments],\\n71:         }\\n72: \\n73: \\n74: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n75: # Chat View-Model\\n76: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n77: class ChatViewModel(QtCore.QObject):\\n78:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n79:     Acts as the View-Model for a single chat channel.\\n80: \\n81:     Responsibilities\\n82:     ----------------\\n83:     \u2022 Keep a local in-memory list of ChatMessage objects\\n84:     \u2022 Subscribe to the domain event-bus for incoming/outgoing messages\\n85:     \u2022 Expose Qt signals so Views can bind via Qt\u2019s signal/slot system\\n86:     \u2022 Persist unsent drafts & restore them on reboot\\n87:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n88: \\n89:     # -------- Qt Signals ----------------------------------------------------#\\n90:     messageAdded = QtCore.Signal(ChatMessage)  # new message appended\\n91:     messageReplaced = QtCore.Signal(ChatMessage)  # edited or updated\\n92:     typingStatusChanged = QtCore.Signal(str, bool)  # user_id, is_typing\\n93:     errorOccurred = QtCore.Signal(str)  # human-readable error string\\n94:     historyLoaded = QtCore.Signal(list)  # list[ChatMessage]\\n95: \\n96:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n97:     def __init__(self, channel_id: str, event_bus: EventBus, parent=None) -> None:\\n98:         super().__init__(parent)\\n99:         self._channel_id: str = channel_id\\n100:         self._event_bus: EventBus = event_bus\\n101:         self._messages: list[ChatMessage] = []\\n102: \\n103:         # drafts/<user>/<channel>.json\\n104:         self._draft_path: Path = (\\n105:             Path(settings.get(\\\"user_data_dir\\\", \\\"~/.flockdesk\\\")).expanduser()\\n106:             / \\\"drafts\\\"\\n107:             / f\\\"{settings.get('user_id', 'anonymous')}\\\"\\n108:             / f\\\"{channel_id}.json\\\"\\n109:         )\\n110: \\n111:         self._typing_users: Dict[str, QtCore.QTimer] = {}\\n112:         self._lock = threading.RLock()\\n113: \\n114:         self._register_event_subscriptions()\\n115:         self._restore_draft_async()\\n116: \\n117:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n118:     # API surface for Controllers\\n119:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n120:     def history(self) -> list[ChatMessage]:\\n121:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Return a copy of the current message history.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n122:         with self._lock:\\n123:             return list(self._messages)\\n124: \\n125:     def send_message(self, content: str, attachments: Optional[List[Path]] = None) -> None:\\n126:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n127:         Send a message to the current channel.\\n128: \\n129:         The actual network interaction is delegated to the domain layer\\n130:         via the event-bus. We optimistically append the message locally\\n131:         and mark it as `is_outgoing=True`; once the server acknowledges\\n132:         (or rejects) the message we will replace/update it.\\n133:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n134:         if not content and not attachments:\\n135:             _log.debug(\\\"Empty message suppressed\\\")\\n136:             return\\n137: \\n138:         msg_id = f\\\"tmp-{datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc).timestamp()}\\\"\\n139:         attachments = attachments or []\\n140:         outgoing = ChatMessage(\\n141:             msg_id=msg_id,\\n142:             channel_id=self._channel_id,\\n143:             sender_id=settings.get(\\\"user_id\\\", \\\"anonymous\\\"),\\n144:             sender_display=settings.get(\\\"user_display\\\", \\\"Me\\\"),\\n145:             content=content,\\n146:             timestamp=datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc),\\n147:             is_outgoing=True,\\n148:             attachments=attachments,\\n149:         )\\n150: \\n151:         self._append_message(outgoing)\\n152: \\n153:         # Publish to domain event-bus (fire-and-forget)\\n154:         _log.debug(\\\"Publishing OutgoingMessage event: %s\\\", outgoing.msg_id)\\n155:         self._event_bus.publish(\\n156:             Event(\\n157:                 topic=\\\"chat.outgoing\\\",\\n158:                 payload=outgoing.serialize(),\\n159:                 replayable=False,\\n160:             )\\n161:         )\\n162: \\n163:         # Clear persisted draft for this channel\\n164:         self._clear_draft_file()\\n165: \\n166:     def start_typing(self) -> None:\\n167:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Publish typing indicator event when user starts typing.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n168:         self._event_bus.publish(\\n169:             Event(\\n170:                 topic=\\\"chat.typing\\\",\\n171:                 payload={\\\"channel_id\\\": self._channel_id, \\\"user_id\\\": settings.get(\\\"user_id\\\")},\\n172:             )\\n173:         )\\n174: \\n175:     def persist_draft(self, draft_text: str) -> None:\\n176:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n177:         Persist unsent draft on disk.\\n178: \\n179:         Called from the Controller on text-changed events.\\n180:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n181:         if not draft_text.strip():\\n182:             self._clear_draft_file()\\n183:             return\\n184: \\n185:         self._draft_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n186:         try:\\n187:             self._draft_path.write_text(\\n188:                 json.dumps({\\\"draft\\\": draft_text, \\\"timestamp\\\": datetime.now().isoformat()}),\\n189:                 encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\",\\n190:             )\\n191:             _log.debug(\\\"Draft persisted to %s\\\", self._draft_path)\\n192:         except OSError as exc:\\n193:             _log.warning(\\\"Failed to write draft file: %s\\\", exc)\\n194: \\n195:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n196:     # Internal helpers\\n197:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n198:     def _register_event_subscriptions(self) -> None:\\n199:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n200:         Subscribe to the global EventBus for chat-related events.\\n201: \\n202:         We keep the subscription tokens so we can unsubscribe\\n203:         when the View-Model is deleted / the window is closed.\\n204:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n205: \\n206:         # Incoming messages\\n207:         self._event_bus.subscribe(\\n208:             \\\"chat.incoming\\\",\\n209:             self._on_incoming_message,\\n210:             group=f\\\"vm_{self._channel_id}\\\",\\n211:         )\\n212: \\n213:         # Delivery acknowledgements for optimistic updates\\n214:         self._event_bus.subscribe(\\n215:             \\\"chat.outgoing.confirmation\\\",\\n216:             self._on_delivery_confirmation,\\n217:             group=f\\\"vm_{self._channel_id}\\\",\\n218:         )\\n219: \\n220:         # Typing indicators\\n221:         self._event_bus.subscribe(\\n222:             \\\"chat.typing\\\",\\n223:             self._on_typing_indicator,\\n224:             group=f\\\"vm_{self._channel_id}\\\",\\n225:         )\\n226: \\n227:         # Synchronised edits / deletions\\n228:         self._event_bus.subscribe(\\n229:             \\\"chat.message.updated\\\",\\n230:             self._on_remote_update,\\n231:             group=f\\\"vm_{self._channel_id}\\\",\\n232:         )\\n233: \\n234:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n235:     def _append_message(self, message: ChatMessage) -> None:\\n236:         with self._lock:\\n237:             self._messages.append(message)\\n238:         self.messageAdded.emit(message)\\n239: \\n240:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n241:     # Event-bus callback handlers\\n242:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n243:     def _on_incoming_message(self, event: Event) -> None:\\n244:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Handle new message events from other users.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n245:         payload = event.payload\\n246:         if payload[\\\"channel_id\\\"] != self._channel_id:\\n247:             return\\n248: \\n249:         msg = ChatMessage(\\n250:             msg_id=payload[\\\"msg_id\\\"],\\n251:             channel_id=payload[\\\"channel_id\\\"],\\n252:             sender_id=payload[\\\"sender_id\\\"],\\n253:             sender_display=payload[\\\"sender_display\\\"],\\n254:             content=payload[\\\"content\\\"],\\n255:             timestamp=datetime.fromisoformat(payload[\\\"timestamp\\\"]),\\n256:             attachments=[Path(p) for p in payload.get(\\\"attachments\\\", [])],\\n257:         )\\n258:         self._append_message(msg)\\n259: \\n260:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n261:     def _on_delivery_confirmation(self, event: Event) -> None:\\n262:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Replace optimistic message with definitive server copy.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n263:         payload = event.payload\\n264:         if payload[\\\"channel_id\\\"] != self._channel_id:\\n265:             return\\n266: \\n267:         tmp_id = payload[\\\"tmp_id\\\"]\\n268:         real_id = payload[\\\"msg_id\\\"]\\n269: \\n270:         with self._lock:\\n271:             for idx, m in enumerate(self._messages):\\n272:                 if m.msg_id == tmp_id:\\n273:                     updated = ChatMessage(\\n274:                         msg_id=real_id,\\n275:                         channel_id=m.channel_id,\\n276:                         sender_id=m.sender_id,\\n277:                         sender_display=m.sender_display,\\n278:                         content=m.content,\\n279:                         timestamp=datetime.fromisoformat(payload[\\\"timestamp\\\"]),\\n280:                         is_outgoing=False,\\n281:                         attachments=m.attachments,\\n282:                     )\\n283:                     self._messages[idx] = updated\\n284:                     _log.debug(\\\"Optimistic message %s replaced by %s\\\", tmp_id, real_id)\\n285:                     self.messageReplaced.emit(updated)\\n286:                     break\\n287: \\n288:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n289:     def _on_typing_indicator(self, event: Event) -> None:\\n290:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Update typing indicator map & emit signal.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n291:         payload = event.payload\\n292:         if payload[\\\"channel_id\\\"] != self._channel_id:\\n293:             return\\n294: \\n295:         user_id = payload[\\\"user_id\\\"]\\n296:         if user_id == settings.get(\\\"user_id\\\"):\\n297:             return  # ignore self typing\\n298: \\n299:         # Reset/Start debounce timer for this user\\n300:         timer = self._typing_users.get(user_id)\\n301:         if timer is None:\\n302:             timer = QtCore.QTimer(self)\\n303:             timer.setSingleShot(True)\\n304:             timer.timeout.connect(lambda uid=user_id: self._emit_typing_end(uid))\\n305:             self._typing_users[user_id] = timer\\n306:             self.typingStatusChanged.emit(user_id, True)\\n307: \\n308:         timer.start(3000)  # 3s window\\n309: \\n310:     def _emit_typing_end(self, user_id: str) -> None:\\n311:         self.typingStatusChanged.emit(user_id, False)\\n312:         self._typing_users.pop(user_id, None)\\n313: \\n314:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n315:     def _on_remote_update(self, event: Event) -> None:\\n316:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Handle remote edits or deletions.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n317:         payload = event.payload\\n318:         if payload[\\\"channel_id\\\"] != self._channel_id:\\n319:             return\\n320: \\n321:         with self._lock:\\n322:             for idx, m in enumerate(self._messages):\\n323:                 if m.msg_id == payload[\\\"msg_id\\\"]:\\n324:                     updated = ChatMessage(\\n325:                         msg_id=m.msg_id,\\n326:                         channel_id=m.channel_id,\\n327:                         sender_id=m.sender_id,\\n328:                         sender_display=m.sender_display,\\n329:                         content=payload.get(\\\"content\\\", m.content),\\n330:                         timestamp=m.timestamp,\\n331:                         edited=True,\\n332:                         attachments=[Path(p) for p in payload.get(\\\"attachments\\\", [])],\\n333:                     )\\n334:                     self._messages[idx] = updated\\n335:                     self.messageReplaced.emit(updated)\\n336:                     _log.debug(\\\"Message %s updated from remote\\\", m.msg_id)\\n337:                     break\\n338: \\n339:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n340:     # Draft persistence\\n341:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n342:     def _restore_draft_async(self) -> None:\\n343:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Restore draft in a background thread to keep UI responsive.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n344: \\n345:         def _worker(vm: \\\"ChatViewModel\\\") -> None:\\n346:             if vm._draft_path.exists():\\n347:                 try:\\n348:                     data = json.loads(vm._draft_path.read_text(encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\"))\\n349:                     draft = data.get(\\\"draft\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n350:                     _log.debug(\\\"Draft restored for channel %s\\\", vm._channel_id)\\n351:                     # Emit via Qt to avoid cross-thread issues\\n352:                     QtCore.QMetaObject.invokeMethod(\\n353:                         vm,\\n354:                         \\\"_emit_draft_restored\\\",\\n355:                         QtCore.Qt.QueuedConnection,\\n356:                         QtCore.Q_ARG(str, draft),\\n357:                     )\\n358:                 except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:\\n359:                     _log.warning(\\\"Failed to restore draft: %s\\\", exc)\\n360: \\n361:         threading.Thread(target=_worker, args=(self,), daemon=True).start()\\n362: \\n363:     @QtCore.Slot(str)\\n364:     def _emit_draft_restored(self, text: str) -> None:\\n365:         self.historyLoaded.emit([])  # just to notify view to attach draft text\\n366: \\n367:     def _clear_draft_file(self) -> None:\\n368:         try:\\n369:             if self._draft_path.exists():\\n370:                 self._draft_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)\\n371:                 _log.debug(\\\"Draft cleared for channel %s\\\", self._channel_id)\\n372:         except OSError as exc:\\n373:             _log.warning(\\\"Failed to clear draft file: %s\\\", exc)\\n374: \\n375:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n376:     # Qt Object Life-Cycle\\n377:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n378:     def __del__(self) -> None:\\n379:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Ensure we cleanly unsubscribe from the EventBus.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n380:         try:\\n381:             self._event_bus.unsubscribe_group(f\\\"vm_{self._channel_id}\\\")\\n382:         except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\\n383:             pass  # During interpreter shutdown objects may be gone\\n384: \\n385: \\n386: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n387: # Factory (Dependency Injection ready)\\n388: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n389: def create_chat_viewmodel(channel_id: str, event_bus: EventBus) -> ChatViewModel:\\n390:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n391:     Factory helper so the app\u2019s DI container can easily inject dependencies.\\n392: \\n393:     Returns\\n394:     -------\\n395:     ChatViewModel\\n396:         Ready-to-use instance with subscriptions in place.\\n397:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n398:     vm = ChatViewModel(channel_id=channel_id, event_bus=event_bus)\\n399:     return vm\\n400: ```\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021@16180b273d43410fcc114af7997ce2d2b28e392e/-/blob/flockdesk/modules/chat/viewmodel/chat_vm.py\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: ```python\n2: \"\"\"\n3: FlockDesk \u2013 Chat Module\n4: View-Model (MVVM) for the Chat micro-front-end.\n5: \n6: This module owns the presentation-ready state of a single chat channel.\n7: It keeps the Qt View in sync with the domain layer through an internal\n8: event-bus, handles optimistic UI updates, provides command objects for\n9: the Controller, and persists transient UI data such as unsent drafts.\n10: \n11: Author: FlockDesk Core Team\n12: \"\"\"\n13: \n14: from __future__ import annotations\n15: \n16: import json\n17: import logging\n18: import threading\n19: from dataclasses import dataclass, field\n20: from datetime import datetime, timezone\n21: from pathlib import Path\n22: from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional\n23: \n24: from PySide6 import QtCore\n25: \n26: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n27: # External / Project-wide Dependencies\n28: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n29: try:\n30:     from flockdesk.core.events import EventBus, Event  # type: ignore\n31:     from flockdesk.core.settings import settings  # type: ignore\n32:     from flockdesk.core.exceptions import ChatSendError  # type: ignore\n33: except ModuleNotFoundError:  # Fallbacks for static type checkers / docs\n34:     EventBus = Any  # pragma: no cover\n35:     Event = Any  # pragma: no cover\n36:     settings = {}  # pragma: no cover\n37:     ChatSendError = Exception  # pragma: no cover\n38: \n39: \n40: _log = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n41: \n42: \n43: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n44: # Data Models\n45: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n46: @dataclass(slots=True, frozen=True)\n47: class ChatMessage:\n48:     \"\"\"Immutably represents a message entity in the chat session.\"\"\"\n49: \n50:     msg_id: str\n51:     channel_id: str\n52:     sender_id: str\n53:     sender_display: str\n54:     content: str\n55:     timestamp: datetime\n56:     edited: bool = False\n57:     is_outgoing: bool = False\n58:     attachments: List[Path] = field(default_factory=list)\n59: \n60:     def serialize(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n61:         return {\n62:             \"msg_id\": self.msg_id,\n63:             \"channel_id\": self.channel_id,\n64:             \"sender_id\": self.sender_id,\n65:             \"sender_display\": self.sender_display,\n66:             \"content\": self.content,\n67:             \"timestamp\": self.timestamp.isoformat(),\n68:             \"edited\": self.edited,\n69:             \"is_outgoing\": self.is_outgoing,\n70:             \"attachments\": [str(p) for p in self.attachments],\n71:         }\n72: \n73: \n74: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n75: # Chat View-Model\n76: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n77: class ChatViewModel(QtCore.QObject):\n78:     \"\"\"\n79:     Acts as the View-Model for a single chat channel.\n80: \n81:     Responsibilities\n82:     ----------------\n83:     \u2022 Keep a local in-memory list of ChatMessage objects\n84:     \u2022 Subscribe to the domain event-bus for incoming/outgoing messages\n85:     \u2022 Expose Qt signals so Views can bind via Qt\u2019s signal/slot system\n86:     \u2022 Persist unsent drafts & restore them on reboot\n87:     \"\"\"\n88: \n89:     # -------- Qt Signals ----------------------------------------------------#\n90:     messageAdded = QtCore.Signal(ChatMessage)  # new message appended\n91:     messageReplaced = QtCore.Signal(ChatMessage)  # edited or updated\n92:     typingStatusChanged = QtCore.Signal(str, bool)  # user_id, is_typing\n93:     errorOccurred = QtCore.Signal(str)  # human-readable error string\n94:     historyLoaded = QtCore.Signal(list)  # list[ChatMessage]\n95: \n96:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n97:     def __init__(self, channel_id: str, event_bus: EventBus, parent=None) -> None:\n98:         super().__init__(parent)\n99:         self._channel_id: str = channel_id\n100:         self._event_bus: EventBus = event_bus\n101:         self._messages: list[ChatMessage] = []\n102: \n103:         # drafts/<user>/<channel>.json\n104:         self._draft_path: Path = (\n105:             Path(settings.get(\"user_data_dir\", \"~/.flockdesk\")).expanduser()\n106:             / \"drafts\"\n107:             / f\"{settings.get('user_id', 'anonymous')}\"\n108:             / f\"{channel_id}.json\"\n109:         )\n110: \n111:         self._typing_users: Dict[str, QtCore.QTimer] = {}\n112:         self._lock = threading.RLock()\n113: \n114:         self._register_event_subscriptions()\n115:         self._restore_draft_async()\n116: \n117:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n118:     # API surface for Controllers\n119:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n120:     def history(self) -> list[ChatMessage]:\n121:         \"\"\"Return a copy of the current message history.\"\"\"\n122:         with self._lock:\n123:             return list(self._messages)\n124: \n125:     def send_message(self, content: str, attachments: Optional[List[Path]] = None) -> None:\n126:         \"\"\"\n127:         Send a message to the current channel.\n128: \n129:         The actual network interaction is delegated to the domain layer\n130:         via the event-bus. We optimistically append the message locally\n131:         and mark it as `is_outgoing=True`; once the server acknowledges\n132:         (or rejects) the message we will replace/update it.\n133:         \"\"\"\n134:         if not content and not attachments:\n135:             _log.debug(\"Empty message suppressed\")\n136:             return\n137: \n138:         msg_id = f\"tmp-{datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc).timestamp()}\"\n139:         attachments = attachments or []\n140:         outgoing = ChatMessage(\n141:             msg_id=msg_id,\n142:             channel_id=self._channel_id,\n143:             sender_id=settings.get(\"user_id\", \"anonymous\"),\n144:             sender_display=settings.get(\"user_display\", \"Me\"),\n145:             content=content,\n146:             timestamp=datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc),\n147:             is_outgoing=True,\n148:             attachments=attachments,\n149:         )\n150: \n151:         self._append_message(outgoing)\n152: \n153:         # Publish to domain event-bus (fire-and-forget)\n154:         _log.debug(\"Publishing OutgoingMessage event: %s\", outgoing.msg_id)\n155:         self._event_bus.publish(\n156:             Event(\n157:                 topic=\"chat.outgoing\",\n158:                 payload=outgoing.serialize(),\n159:                 replayable=False,\n160:             )\n161:         )\n162: \n163:         # Clear persisted draft for this channel\n164:         self._clear_draft_file()\n165: \n166:     def start_typing(self) -> None:\n167:         \"\"\"Publish typing indicator event when user starts typing.\"\"\"\n168:         self._event_bus.publish(\n169:             Event(\n170:                 topic=\"chat.typing\",\n171:                 payload={\"channel_id\": self._channel_id, \"user_id\": settings.get(\"user_id\")},\n172:             )\n173:         )\n174: \n175:     def persist_draft(self, draft_text: str) -> None:\n176:         \"\"\"\n177:         Persist unsent draft on disk.\n178: \n179:         Called from the Controller on text-changed events.\n180:         \"\"\"\n181:         if not draft_text.strip():\n182:             self._clear_draft_file()\n183:             return\n184: \n185:         self._draft_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n186:         try:\n187:             self._draft_path.write_text(\n188:                 json.dumps({\"draft\": draft_text, \"timestamp\": datetime.now().isoformat()}),\n189:                 encoding=\"utf-8\",\n190:             )\n191:             _log.debug(\"Draft persisted to %s\", self._draft_path)\n192:         except OSError as exc:\n193:             _log.warning(\"Failed to write draft file: %s\", exc)\n194: \n195:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n196:     # Internal helpers\n197:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n198:     def _register_event_subscriptions(self) -> None:\n199:         \"\"\"\n200:         Subscribe to the global EventBus for chat-related events.\n201: \n202:         We keep the subscription tokens so we can unsubscribe\n203:         when the View-Model is deleted / the window is closed.\n204:         \"\"\"\n205: \n206:         # Incoming messages\n207:         self._event_bus.subscribe(\n208:             \"chat.incoming\",\n209:             self._on_incoming_message,\n210:             group=f\"vm_{self._channel_id}\",\n211:         )\n212: \n213:         # Delivery acknowledgements for optimistic updates\n214:         self._event_bus.subscribe(\n215:             \"chat.outgoing.confirmation\",\n216:             self._on_delivery_confirmation,\n217:             group=f\"vm_{self._channel_id}\",\n218:         )\n219: \n220:         # Typing indicators\n221:         self._event_bus.subscribe(\n222:             \"chat.typing\",\n223:             self._on_typing_indicator,\n224:             group=f\"vm_{self._channel_id}\",\n225:         )\n226: \n227:         # Synchronised edits / deletions\n228:         self._event_bus.subscribe(\n229:             \"chat.message.updated\",\n230:             self._on_remote_update,\n231:             group=f\"vm_{self._channel_id}\",\n232:         )\n233: \n234:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n235:     def _append_message(self, message: ChatMessage) -> None:\n236:         with self._lock:\n237:             self._messages.append(message)\n238:         self.messageAdded.emit(message)\n239: \n240:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n241:     # Event-bus callback handlers\n242:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n243:     def _on_incoming_message(self, event: Event) -> None:\n244:         \"\"\"Handle new message events from other users.\"\"\"\n245:         payload = event.payload\n246:         if payload[\"channel_id\"] != self._channel_id:\n247:             return\n248: \n249:         msg = ChatMessage(\n250:             msg_id=payload[\"msg_id\"],\n251:             channel_id=payload[\"channel_id\"],\n252:             sender_id=payload[\"sender_id\"],\n253:             sender_display=payload[\"sender_display\"],\n254:             content=payload[\"content\"],\n255:             timestamp=datetime.fromisoformat(payload[\"timestamp\"]),\n256:             attachments=[Path(p) for p in payload.get(\"attachments\", [])],\n257:         )\n258:         self._append_message(msg)\n259: \n260:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n261:     def _on_delivery_confirmation(self, event: Event) -> None:\n262:         \"\"\"Replace optimistic message with definitive server copy.\"\"\"\n263:         payload = event.payload\n264:         if payload[\"channel_id\"] != self._channel_id:\n265:             return\n266: \n267:         tmp_id = payload[\"tmp_id\"]\n268:         real_id = payload[\"msg_id\"]\n269: \n270:         with self._lock:\n271:             for idx, m in enumerate(self._messages):\n272:                 if m.msg_id == tmp_id:\n273:                     updated = ChatMessage(\n274:                         msg_id=real_id,\n275:                         channel_id=m.channel_id,\n276:                         sender_id=m.sender_id,\n277:                         sender_display=m.sender_display,\n278:                         content=m.content,\n279:                         timestamp=datetime.fromisoformat(payload[\"timestamp\"]),\n280:                         is_outgoing=False,\n281:                         attachments=m.attachments,\n282:                     )\n283:                     self._messages[idx] = updated\n284:                     _log.debug(\"Optimistic message %s replaced by %s\", tmp_id, real_id)\n285:                     self.messageReplaced.emit(updated)\n286:                     break\n287: \n288:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n289:     def _on_typing_indicator(self, event: Event) -> None:\n290:         \"\"\"Update typing indicator map & emit signal.\"\"\"\n291:         payload = event.payload\n292:         if payload[\"channel_id\"] != self._channel_id:\n293:             return\n294: \n295:         user_id = payload[\"user_id\"]\n296:         if user_id == settings.get(\"user_id\"):\n297:             return  # ignore self typing\n298: \n299:         # Reset/Start debounce timer for this user\n300:         timer = self._typing_users.get(user_id)\n301:         if timer is None:\n302:             timer = QtCore.QTimer(self)\n303:             timer.setSingleShot(True)\n304:             timer.timeout.connect(lambda uid=user_id: self._emit_typing_end(uid))\n305:             self._typing_users[user_id] = timer\n306:             self.typingStatusChanged.emit(user_id, True)\n307: \n308:         timer.start(3000)  # 3s window\n309: \n310:     def _emit_typing_end(self, user_id: str) -> None:\n311:         self.typingStatusChanged.emit(user_id, False)\n312:         self._typing_users.pop(user_id, None)\n313: \n314:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n315:     def _on_remote_update(self, event: Event) -> None:\n316:         \"\"\"Handle remote edits or deletions.\"\"\"\n317:         payload = event.payload\n318:         if payload[\"channel_id\"] != self._channel_id:\n319:             return\n320: \n321:         with self._lock:\n322:             for idx, m in enumerate(self._messages):\n323:                 if m.msg_id == payload[\"msg_id\"]:\n324:                     updated = ChatMessage(\n325:                         msg_id=m.msg_id,\n326:                         channel_id=m.channel_id,\n327:                         sender_id=m.sender_id,\n328:                         sender_display=m.sender_display,\n329:                         content=payload.get(\"content\", m.content),\n330:                         timestamp=m.timestamp,\n331:                         edited=True,\n332:                         attachments=[Path(p) for p in payload.get(\"attachments\", [])],\n333:                     )\n334:                     self._messages[idx] = updated\n335:                     self.messageReplaced.emit(updated)\n336:                     _log.debug(\"Message %s updated from remote\", m.msg_id)\n337:                     break\n338: \n339:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n340:     # Draft persistence\n341:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n342:     def _restore_draft_async(self) -> None:\n343:         \"\"\"Restore draft in a background thread to keep UI responsive.\"\"\"\n344: \n345:         def _worker(vm: \"ChatViewModel\") -> None:\n346:             if vm._draft_path.exists():\n347:                 try:\n348:                     data = json.loads(vm._draft_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n349:                     draft = data.get(\"draft\", \"\")\n350:                     _log.debug(\"Draft restored for channel %s\", vm._channel_id)\n351:                     # Emit via Qt to avoid cross-thread issues\n352:                     QtCore.QMetaObject.invokeMethod(\n353:                         vm,\n354:                         \"_emit_draft_restored\",\n355:                         QtCore.Qt.QueuedConnection,\n356:                         QtCore.Q_ARG(str, draft),\n357:                     )\n358:                 except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:\n359:                     _log.warning(\"Failed to restore draft: %s\", exc)\n360: \n361:         threading.Thread(target=_worker, args=(self,), daemon=True).start()\n362: \n363:     @QtCore.Slot(str)\n364:     def _emit_draft_restored(self, text: str) -> None:\n365:         self.historyLoaded.emit([])  # just to notify view to attach draft text\n366: \n367:     def _clear_draft_file(self) -> None:\n368:         try:\n369:             if self._draft_path.exists():\n370:                 self._draft_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)\n371:                 _log.debug(\"Draft cleared for channel %s\", self._channel_id)\n372:         except OSError as exc:\n373:             _log.warning(\"Failed to clear draft file: %s\", exc)\n374: \n375:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n376:     # Qt Object Life-Cycle\n377:     # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n378:     def __del__(self) -> None:\n379:         \"\"\"Ensure we cleanly unsubscribe from the EventBus.\"\"\"\n380:         try:\n381:             self._event_bus.unsubscribe_group(f\"vm_{self._channel_id}\")\n382:         except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n383:             pass  # During interpreter shutdown objects may be gone\n384: \n385: \n386: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n387: # Factory (Dependency Injection ready)\n388: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n389: def create_chat_viewmodel(channel_id: str, event_bus: EventBus) -> ChatViewModel:\n390:     \"\"\"\n391:     Factory helper so the app\u2019s DI container can easily inject dependencies.\n392: \n393:     Returns\n394:     -------\n395:     ChatViewModel\n396:         Ready-to-use instance with subscriptions in place.\n397:     \"\"\"\n398:     vm = ChatViewModel(channel_id=channel_id, event_bus=event_bus)\n399:     return vm\n400: ```",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021@16180b273d43410fcc114af7997ce2d2b28e392e/-/blob/flockdesk/modules/chat/viewmodel/chat_vm.py"
  }
}
14. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "flockdesk/modules/presence/viewmodel/presence_vm.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: ```python\\n2: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n3: FlockDesk \u2013 Presence Module: View-Model Layer\\n4: ============================================\\n5: \\n6: This file holds the *view-model* that mediates between the presence\\n7: backend-service (listening on the internal event-bus) and the Qt views /\\n8: QML components that render the roster.\\n9: \\n10: The module is 100 % standalone and can be imported by tests without the\\n11: full desktop runtime.  It exposes one public symbol:\\n12: \\n13:     PresenceViewModel  \u2013 A Qt QObject that wraps a\\n14:                          QAbstractListModel with presence data and\\n15:                          exposes commands for the UI.\\n16: \\n17: Author  : FlockDesk Core Team\\n18: License : MIT (see project root)\\n19: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n20: from __future__ import annotations\\n21: \\n22: import asyncio\\n23: import datetime as _dt\\n24: import logging\\n25: import types\\n26: from dataclasses import dataclass, field\\n27: from enum import Enum, auto\\n28: from typing import Dict, List, Optional\\n29: \\n30: from PySide6 import QtCore\\n31: \\n32: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n33: # 3rd-party / infrastructure stubs\\n34: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n35: # NOTE:\\n36: # The real application injects an actual event-bus implementation that\\n37: # resembles the minimal API below.  We ship a tiny duck-type fallback so\\n38: # the module does not crash when imported in isolation.\\n39: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n40: \\n41: \\n42: class _DummySubscription:  # pragma: no cover \u2013 testing helper\\n43:     def __init__(self, topic: str, cb):\\n44:         self._topic, self._cb = topic, cb\\n45: \\n46:     def dispose(self):\\n47:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Mimic an un-subscribe operation.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n48:         logging.getLogger(__name__).debug(\\\"Unsubscribed from topic %s\\\", self._topic)\\n49: \\n50: \\n51: class EventBus:  # pragma: no cover \u2013 placeholder\\n52:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Very small subset of the real bus used in production.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n53: \\n54:     def __init__(self):\\n55:         self._topics: Dict[str, List] = {}\\n56: \\n57:     def publish(self, topic: str, payload: object) -> None:\\n58:         for cb in self._topics.get(topic, []):\\n59:             try:\\n60:                 cb(payload)  # noqa: B023 \u2013 sync callback in fake bus\\n61:             except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\\n62:                 logging.exception(\\\"Unhandled error in bus subscriber for topic %s\\\", topic)\\n63: \\n64:     def subscribe(self, topic: str, cb) -> _DummySubscription:\\n65:         self._topics.setdefault(topic, []).append(cb)\\n66:         return _DummySubscription(topic, cb)\\n67: \\n68: \\n69: # When FlockDesk boots, the DI-container injects a **shared** bus\\n70: # instance.  Until then, we fall back to a local private one.\\n71: _default_bus = EventBus()\\n72: \\n73: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n74: # Domain model\\n75: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n76: \\n77: \\n78: class PresenceStatus(Enum):\\n79:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Canonical presence states understood by the desktop suite.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n80: \\n81:     ONLINE = auto()\\n82:     AWAY = auto()\\n83:     BUSY = auto()\\n84:     OFFLINE = auto()\\n85: \\n86:     def __str__(self) -> str:  # Readable for QML\\n87:         return self.name.lower()\\n88: \\n89: \\n90: @dataclass(slots=True)\\n91: class PresenceEntry:\\n92:     user_id: str\\n93:     display_name: str\\n94:     status: PresenceStatus = PresenceStatus.OFFLINE\\n95:     last_active: _dt.datetime = field(default_factory=_dt.datetime.utcnow)\\n96: \\n97:     def to_qvariant_map(self) -> Dict[str, object]:\\n98:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Return values convertible by Qt for use in QML/JS.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n99:         return {\\n100:             \\\"userId\\\": self.user_id,\\n101:             \\\"displayName\\\": self.display_name,\\n102:             \\\"status\\\": str(self.status),\\n103:             \\\"lastActive\\\": self.last_active.isoformat(),\\n104:         }\\n105: \\n106: \\n107: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n108: # QAbstractListModel representing the roster\\n109: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n110: \\n111: \\n112: class PresenceListModel(QtCore.QAbstractListModel):\\n113:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n114:     Thin wrapper between a list[PresenceEntry] and Qt's model/view API.\\n115:     Emits the usual model signals, hence updates appear instantaneously\\n116:     on bound QML ListViews or QtWidgets views.\\n117:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n118: \\n119:     # Qt Role numeric IDs\\n120:     USER_ID_ROLE = QtCore.Qt.UserRole + 1\\n121:     DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE = USER_ID_ROLE + 1\\n122:     STATUS_ROLE = DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE + 1\\n123:     LAST_ACTIVE_ROLE = STATUS_ROLE + 1\\n124: \\n125:     _ROLE_NAMES = {\\n126:         USER_ID_ROLE: b\\\"userId\\\",\\n127:         DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE: b\\\"displayName\\\",\\n128:         STATUS_ROLE: b\\\"status\\\",\\n129:         LAST_ACTIVE_ROLE: b\\\"lastActive\\\",\\n130:     }\\n131: \\n132:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n133:     # Qt mandatory overrides\\n134:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n135: \\n136:     def __init__(self, parent: Optional[QtCore.QObject] = None):\\n137:         super().__init__(parent)\\n138:         self._roster: List[PresenceEntry] = []\\n139: \\n140:     # Qt interface \u2013 meta\\n141:     def roleNames(self) -> Dict[int, bytes]:  # noqa: N802 \u2013 Qt API\\n142:         return self._ROLE_NAMES\\n143: \\n144:     # Qt interface \u2013 data\\n145:     def rowCount(self, parent=QtCore.QModelIndex()) -> int:  # noqa: N802\\n146:         return 0 if parent.isValid() else len(self._roster)\\n147: \\n148:     def data(self, index: QtCore.QModelIndex, role: int = QtCore.Qt.DisplayRole):  # noqa: N802\\n149:         if not index.isValid() or not (0 <= index.row() < len(self._roster)):\\n150:             return None\\n151: \\n152:         entry = self._roster[index.row()]\\n153: \\n154:         if role == self.USER_ID_ROLE:\\n155:             return entry.user_id\\n156:         if role == self.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE:\\n157:             return entry.display_name\\n158:         if role == self.STATUS_ROLE:\\n159:             return str(entry.status)\\n160:         if role == self.LAST_ACTIVE_ROLE:\\n161:             return entry.last_active.isoformat()\\n162: \\n163:         return None\\n164: \\n165:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n166:     # Mutating helpers exposed to the VM\\n167:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n168: \\n169:     def upsert_entries(self, entries: List[PresenceEntry]) -> None:\\n170:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n171:         Insert or update one or many presence rows.\\n172: \\n173:         The algorithm is O(N*M) but roster lists are typically short\\n174:         (max ~200 members).  Optimise later if needed.\\n175:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n176:         for entry in entries:\\n177:             try:\\n178:                 idx = next(i for i, e in enumerate(self._roster) if e.user_id == entry.user_id)\\n179:                 if self._roster[idx] != entry:\\n180:                     self._roster[idx] = entry\\n181:                     top_left = self.index(idx, 0)\\n182:                     self.dataChanged.emit(top_left, top_left, self._ROLE_NAMES.keys())\\n183:             except StopIteration:\\n184:                 # New participant \u2013 append\\n185:                 self.beginInsertRows(QtCore.QModelIndex(), len(self._roster), len(self._roster))\\n186:                 self._roster.append(entry)\\n187:                 self.endInsertRows()\\n188: \\n189:     def remove_user(self, user_id: str) -> None:\\n190:         try:\\n191:             idx = next(i for i, e in enumerate(self._roster) if e.user_id == user_id)\\n192:         except StopIteration:\\n193:             return  # nothing to do\\n194:         self.beginRemoveRows(QtCore.QModelIndex(), idx, idx)\\n195:         del self._roster[idx]\\n196:         self.endRemoveRows()\\n197: \\n198:     # convenience for QML\\n199:     @QtCore.Slot(str, result=int)\\n200:     def row_for_user(self, user_id: str) -> int:  # noqa: D401\\n201:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Return row index for a given user or \u20111.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n202:         try:\\n203:             return next(i for i, entry in enumerate(self._roster) if entry.user_id == user_id)\\n204:         except StopIteration:\\n205:             return -1\\n206: \\n207: \\n208: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n209: # Presence View-Model\\n210: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n211: \\n212: \\n213: class PresenceViewModel(QtCore.QObject):\\n214:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n215:     Qt QObject acting as the binding layer between presence model\\n216:     changes and the GUI.  It subscribes to an *EventBus*, receives\\n217:     events like ``presence.updated`` or ``presence.left``, and updates\\n218:     an internal QAbstractListModel exposed to QML.\\n219:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n220: \\n221:     presenceListChanged = QtCore.Signal()  # emitted when the model pointer itself changes\\n222:     errorOccurred = QtCore.Signal(str)\\n223: \\n224:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n225:     # Construction / DI\\n226:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n227: \\n228:     def __init__(\\n229:         self,\\n230:         bus: EventBus | None = None,\\n231:         *,\\n232:         current_user_id: Optional[str] = None,\\n233:         parent: Optional[QtCore.QObject] = None,\\n234:     ):\\n235:         super().__init__(parent)\\n236: \\n237:         self._log = logging.getLogger(self.__class__.__name__)\\n238:         self._bus: EventBus = bus or _default_bus\\n239:         self._current_user_id = current_user_id\\n240: \\n241:         # Our roster list-model\\n242:         self._model = PresenceListModel(self)\\n243: \\n244:         # Keep references to bus subscriptions so we can dispose later.\\n245:         self._subscriptions: List[_DummySubscription] = []\\n246: \\n247:         # Async tasks we spawn (cancel on destruction)\\n248:         self._tasks: List[asyncio.Task] = []\\n249: \\n250:         self._wire_bus()\\n251:         self._bootstrap_roster()\\n252: \\n253:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n254:     # Qt property for QML\\n255:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n256: \\n257:     @QtCore.Property(QtCore.QObject, notify=presenceListChanged)\\n258:     def presenceModel(self) -> PresenceListModel:  # noqa: D401\\n259:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Return the QAbstractListModel consumed by the view.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n260:         return self._model\\n261: \\n262:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n263:     # Public API\\n264:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n265: \\n266:     @QtCore.Slot(result=str)\\n267:     def currentUserId(self) -> str:  # noqa: D401, N802 \u2013 match Qt naming\\n268:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Expose the logged-in user's ID to the view.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n269:         return self._current_user_id or \\\"\\\"\\n270: \\n271:     @QtCore.Slot(str, str)\\n272:     def setStatus(self, user_id: str, raw_status: str) -> None:  # noqa: N802\\n273:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n274:         Command from the UI to update somebody's presence manually.\\n275:         Usually only called for *self*.  Publishes an event so the\\n276:         backend broadcasts to everybody else.\\n277:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n278:         try:\\n279:             status = PresenceStatus[raw_status.upper()]\\n280:         except KeyError as exc:\\n281:             self.errorOccurred.emit(f\\\"Invalid status: {raw_status}\\\")\\n282:             self._log.warning(\\\"Rejected invalid presence status %s\\\", raw_status)\\n283:             return\\n284: \\n285:         payload = {\\n286:             \\\"user_id\\\": user_id,\\n287:             \\\"status\\\": status.name,\\n288:             \\\"timestamp\\\": _dt.datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\\n289:         }\\n290:         self._bus.publish(\\\"presence.set_status\\\", payload)\\n291: \\n292:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n293:     # Private helpers \u2013 bus / async\\n294:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n295: \\n296:     def _wire_bus(self) -> None:\\n297:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Subscribe to presence events on the internal event-bus.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n298:         self._subscriptions.append(self._bus.subscribe(\\\"presence.snapshot\\\", self._on_snapshot))\\n299:         self._subscriptions.append(self._bus.subscribe(\\\"presence.updated\\\", self._on_presence_updated))\\n300:         self._subscriptions.append(self._bus.subscribe(\\\"presence.left\\\", self._on_presence_left))\\n301: \\n302:     def _bootstrap_roster(self) -> None:\\n303:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n304:         Ask the backend for the initial roster list via RPC-style\\n305:         request/response.  We use asyncio so as not to block the Qt loop.\\n306:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n307: \\n308:         async def request_snapshot():\\n309:             try:\\n310:                 # fire-and-wait pattern: publish request, listen for snapshot\\n311:                 # The backend replies on the same `presence.snapshot` topic\\n312:                 self._bus.publish(\\\"presence.request_snapshot\\\", {\\\"requester\\\": self._current_user_id})\\n313:                 # DONE: nothing, listener will receive snapshot asynchronously\\n314:             except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\\n315:                 self._log.exception(\\\"Failed to request presence snapshot\\\")\\n316:                 self.errorOccurred.emit(\\\"Presence initialisation failed\\\")\\n317: \\n318:         task = asyncio.create_task(request_snapshot(), name=\\\"presence.bootstrap\\\")\\n319:         self._tasks.append(task)\\n320: \\n321:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n322:     # Bus event callbacks \u2013 sync context (fake bus).\\n323:     # In production they run on a background thread; we therefore use\\n324:     # Qt's `invokeMethod` to marshal into the main thread.\\n325:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n326: \\n327:     # The real bus dispatches events on worker threads.  Qt objects are\\n328:     # not thread-safe; we hop back to the main thread via single-shot\\n329:     # queued connections.  `QtCore.QMetaObject.invokeMethod` is used\\n330:     # instead of Signals for performance / testability.\\n331:     def _sync_to_qt(self, fn: types.FunctionType, /, *args):\\n332:         QtCore.QMetaObject.invokeMethod(\\n333:             self,\\n334:             lambda: fn(*args),  # type: ignore[arg-type]  # pyqt signature magic\\n335:             QtCore.Qt.QueuedConnection,\\n336:         )\\n337: \\n338:     # Snapshot \u2013 full presence list\\n339:     def _on_snapshot(self, payload):\\n340:         self._log.debug(\\\"Received presence.snapshot %s\\\", payload)\\n341:         entries = [\\n342:             PresenceEntry(\\n343:                 user_id=e[\\\"user_id\\\"],\\n344:                 display_name=e[\\\"display_name\\\"],\\n345:                 status=PresenceStatus[e[\\\"status\\\"].upper()],\\n346:                 last_active=_dt.datetime.fromisoformat(e[\\\"last_active\\\"]),\\n347:             )\\n348:             for e in payload.get(\\\"members\\\", [])\\n349:             if \\\"user_id\\\" in e\\n350:         ]\\n351:         self._sync_to_qt(self._model.upsert_entries, entries)\\n352: \\n353:     # Incremental updates\\n354:     def _on_presence_updated(self, payload):\\n355:         self._log.debug(\\\"presence.updated %s\\\", payload)\\n356:         entry = PresenceEntry(\\n357:             user_id=payload[\\\"user_id\\\"],\\n358:             display_name=payload.get(\\\"display_name\\\", payload[\\\"user_id\\\"]),\\n359:             status=PresenceStatus[payload[\\\"status\\\"].upper()],\\n360:             last_active=_dt.datetime.utcnow(),\\n361:         )\\n362:         self._sync_to_qt(self._model.upsert_entries, [entry])\\n363: \\n364:     # User left \u2013 remove from list\\n365:     def _on_presence_left(self, payload):\\n366:         self._log.debug(\\\"presence.left %s\\\", payload)\\n367:         self._sync_to_qt(self._model.remove_user, payload[\\\"user_id\\\"])\\n368: \\n369:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n370:     # Cleanup\\n371:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\\n372: \\n373:     def __del__(self):\\n374:         for sub in self._subscriptions:\\n375:             try:\\n376:                 sub.dispose()\\n377:             except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\\n378:                 self._log.warning(\\\"Failed to dispose subscription %s\\\", sub, exc_info=True)\\n379:         for task in self._tasks:\\n380:             task.cancel()\\n381: ```\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021@16180b273d43410fcc114af7997ce2d2b28e392e/-/blob/flockdesk/modules/presence/viewmodel/presence_vm.py\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: ```python\n2: \"\"\"\n3: FlockDesk \u2013 Presence Module: View-Model Layer\n4: ============================================\n5: \n6: This file holds the *view-model* that mediates between the presence\n7: backend-service (listening on the internal event-bus) and the Qt views /\n8: QML components that render the roster.\n9: \n10: The module is 100 % standalone and can be imported by tests without the\n11: full desktop runtime.  It exposes one public symbol:\n12: \n13:     PresenceViewModel  \u2013 A Qt QObject that wraps a\n14:                          QAbstractListModel with presence data and\n15:                          exposes commands for the UI.\n16: \n17: Author  : FlockDesk Core Team\n18: License : MIT (see project root)\n19: \"\"\"\n20: from __future__ import annotations\n21: \n22: import asyncio\n23: import datetime as _dt\n24: import logging\n25: import types\n26: from dataclasses import dataclass, field\n27: from enum import Enum, auto\n28: from typing import Dict, List, Optional\n29: \n30: from PySide6 import QtCore\n31: \n32: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n33: # 3rd-party / infrastructure stubs\n34: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n35: # NOTE:\n36: # The real application injects an actual event-bus implementation that\n37: # resembles the minimal API below.  We ship a tiny duck-type fallback so\n38: # the module does not crash when imported in isolation.\n39: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n40: \n41: \n42: class _DummySubscription:  # pragma: no cover \u2013 testing helper\n43:     def __init__(self, topic: str, cb):\n44:         self._topic, self._cb = topic, cb\n45: \n46:     def dispose(self):\n47:         \"\"\"Mimic an un-subscribe operation.\"\"\"\n48:         logging.getLogger(__name__).debug(\"Unsubscribed from topic %s\", self._topic)\n49: \n50: \n51: class EventBus:  # pragma: no cover \u2013 placeholder\n52:     \"\"\"Very small subset of the real bus used in production.\"\"\"\n53: \n54:     def __init__(self):\n55:         self._topics: Dict[str, List] = {}\n56: \n57:     def publish(self, topic: str, payload: object) -> None:\n58:         for cb in self._topics.get(topic, []):\n59:             try:\n60:                 cb(payload)  # noqa: B023 \u2013 sync callback in fake bus\n61:             except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n62:                 logging.exception(\"Unhandled error in bus subscriber for topic %s\", topic)\n63: \n64:     def subscribe(self, topic: str, cb) -> _DummySubscription:\n65:         self._topics.setdefault(topic, []).append(cb)\n66:         return _DummySubscription(topic, cb)\n67: \n68: \n69: # When FlockDesk boots, the DI-container injects a **shared** bus\n70: # instance.  Until then, we fall back to a local private one.\n71: _default_bus = EventBus()\n72: \n73: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n74: # Domain model\n75: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n76: \n77: \n78: class PresenceStatus(Enum):\n79:     \"\"\"Canonical presence states understood by the desktop suite.\"\"\"\n80: \n81:     ONLINE = auto()\n82:     AWAY = auto()\n83:     BUSY = auto()\n84:     OFFLINE = auto()\n85: \n86:     def __str__(self) -> str:  # Readable for QML\n87:         return self.name.lower()\n88: \n89: \n90: @dataclass(slots=True)\n91: class PresenceEntry:\n92:     user_id: str\n93:     display_name: str\n94:     status: PresenceStatus = PresenceStatus.OFFLINE\n95:     last_active: _dt.datetime = field(default_factory=_dt.datetime.utcnow)\n96: \n97:     def to_qvariant_map(self) -> Dict[str, object]:\n98:         \"\"\"Return values convertible by Qt for use in QML/JS.\"\"\"\n99:         return {\n100:             \"userId\": self.user_id,\n101:             \"displayName\": self.display_name,\n102:             \"status\": str(self.status),\n103:             \"lastActive\": self.last_active.isoformat(),\n104:         }\n105: \n106: \n107: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n108: # QAbstractListModel representing the roster\n109: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n110: \n111: \n112: class PresenceListModel(QtCore.QAbstractListModel):\n113:     \"\"\"\n114:     Thin wrapper between a list[PresenceEntry] and Qt's model/view API.\n115:     Emits the usual model signals, hence updates appear instantaneously\n116:     on bound QML ListViews or QtWidgets views.\n117:     \"\"\"\n118: \n119:     # Qt Role numeric IDs\n120:     USER_ID_ROLE = QtCore.Qt.UserRole + 1\n121:     DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE = USER_ID_ROLE + 1\n122:     STATUS_ROLE = DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE + 1\n123:     LAST_ACTIVE_ROLE = STATUS_ROLE + 1\n124: \n125:     _ROLE_NAMES = {\n126:         USER_ID_ROLE: b\"userId\",\n127:         DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE: b\"displayName\",\n128:         STATUS_ROLE: b\"status\",\n129:         LAST_ACTIVE_ROLE: b\"lastActive\",\n130:     }\n131: \n132:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------------#\n133:     # Qt mandatory overrides\n134:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------------#\n135: \n136:     def __init__(self, parent: Optional[QtCore.QObject] = None):\n137:         super().__init__(parent)\n138:         self._roster: List[PresenceEntry] = []\n139: \n140:     # Qt interface \u2013 meta\n141:     def roleNames(self) -> Dict[int, bytes]:  # noqa: N802 \u2013 Qt API\n142:         return self._ROLE_NAMES\n143: \n144:     # Qt interface \u2013 data\n145:     def rowCount(self, parent=QtCore.QModelIndex()) -> int:  # noqa: N802\n146:         return 0 if parent.isValid() else len(self._roster)\n147: \n148:     def data(self, index: QtCore.QModelIndex, role: int = QtCore.Qt.DisplayRole):  # noqa: N802\n149:         if not index.isValid() or not (0 <= index.row() < len(self._roster)):\n150:             return None\n151: \n152:         entry = self._roster[index.row()]\n153: \n154:         if role == self.USER_ID_ROLE:\n155:             return entry.user_id\n156:         if role == self.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE:\n157:             return entry.display_name\n158:         if role == self.STATUS_ROLE:\n159:             return str(entry.status)\n160:         if role == self.LAST_ACTIVE_ROLE:\n161:             return entry.last_active.isoformat()\n162: \n163:         return None\n164: \n165:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n166:     # Mutating helpers exposed to the VM\n167:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n168: \n169:     def upsert_entries(self, entries: List[PresenceEntry]) -> None:\n170:         \"\"\"\n171:         Insert or update one or many presence rows.\n172: \n173:         The algorithm is O(N*M) but roster lists are typically short\n174:         (max ~200 members).  Optimise later if needed.\n175:         \"\"\"\n176:         for entry in entries:\n177:             try:\n178:                 idx = next(i for i, e in enumerate(self._roster) if e.user_id == entry.user_id)\n179:                 if self._roster[idx] != entry:\n180:                     self._roster[idx] = entry\n181:                     top_left = self.index(idx, 0)\n182:                     self.dataChanged.emit(top_left, top_left, self._ROLE_NAMES.keys())\n183:             except StopIteration:\n184:                 # New participant \u2013 append\n185:                 self.beginInsertRows(QtCore.QModelIndex(), len(self._roster), len(self._roster))\n186:                 self._roster.append(entry)\n187:                 self.endInsertRows()\n188: \n189:     def remove_user(self, user_id: str) -> None:\n190:         try:\n191:             idx = next(i for i, e in enumerate(self._roster) if e.user_id == user_id)\n192:         except StopIteration:\n193:             return  # nothing to do\n194:         self.beginRemoveRows(QtCore.QModelIndex(), idx, idx)\n195:         del self._roster[idx]\n196:         self.endRemoveRows()\n197: \n198:     # convenience for QML\n199:     @QtCore.Slot(str, result=int)\n200:     def row_for_user(self, user_id: str) -> int:  # noqa: D401\n201:         \"\"\"Return row index for a given user or \u20111.\"\"\"\n202:         try:\n203:             return next(i for i, entry in enumerate(self._roster) if entry.user_id == user_id)\n204:         except StopIteration:\n205:             return -1\n206: \n207: \n208: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n209: # Presence View-Model\n210: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n211: \n212: \n213: class PresenceViewModel(QtCore.QObject):\n214:     \"\"\"\n215:     Qt QObject acting as the binding layer between presence model\n216:     changes and the GUI.  It subscribes to an *EventBus*, receives\n217:     events like ``presence.updated`` or ``presence.left``, and updates\n218:     an internal QAbstractListModel exposed to QML.\n219:     \"\"\"\n220: \n221:     presenceListChanged = QtCore.Signal()  # emitted when the model pointer itself changes\n222:     errorOccurred = QtCore.Signal(str)\n223: \n224:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n225:     # Construction / DI\n226:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n227: \n228:     def __init__(\n229:         self,\n230:         bus: EventBus | None = None,\n231:         *,\n232:         current_user_id: Optional[str] = None,\n233:         parent: Optional[QtCore.QObject] = None,\n234:     ):\n235:         super().__init__(parent)\n236: \n237:         self._log = logging.getLogger(self.__class__.__name__)\n238:         self._bus: EventBus = bus or _default_bus\n239:         self._current_user_id = current_user_id\n240: \n241:         # Our roster list-model\n242:         self._model = PresenceListModel(self)\n243: \n244:         # Keep references to bus subscriptions so we can dispose later.\n245:         self._subscriptions: List[_DummySubscription] = []\n246: \n247:         # Async tasks we spawn (cancel on destruction)\n248:         self._tasks: List[asyncio.Task] = []\n249: \n250:         self._wire_bus()\n251:         self._bootstrap_roster()\n252: \n253:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n254:     # Qt property for QML\n255:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n256: \n257:     @QtCore.Property(QtCore.QObject, notify=presenceListChanged)\n258:     def presenceModel(self) -> PresenceListModel:  # noqa: D401\n259:         \"\"\"Return the QAbstractListModel consumed by the view.\"\"\"\n260:         return self._model\n261: \n262:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n263:     # Public API\n264:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n265: \n266:     @QtCore.Slot(result=str)\n267:     def currentUserId(self) -> str:  # noqa: D401, N802 \u2013 match Qt naming\n268:         \"\"\"Expose the logged-in user's ID to the view.\"\"\"\n269:         return self._current_user_id or \"\"\n270: \n271:     @QtCore.Slot(str, str)\n272:     def setStatus(self, user_id: str, raw_status: str) -> None:  # noqa: N802\n273:         \"\"\"\n274:         Command from the UI to update somebody's presence manually.\n275:         Usually only called for *self*.  Publishes an event so the\n276:         backend broadcasts to everybody else.\n277:         \"\"\"\n278:         try:\n279:             status = PresenceStatus[raw_status.upper()]\n280:         except KeyError as exc:\n281:             self.errorOccurred.emit(f\"Invalid status: {raw_status}\")\n282:             self._log.warning(\"Rejected invalid presence status %s\", raw_status)\n283:             return\n284: \n285:         payload = {\n286:             \"user_id\": user_id,\n287:             \"status\": status.name,\n288:             \"timestamp\": _dt.datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n289:         }\n290:         self._bus.publish(\"presence.set_status\", payload)\n291: \n292:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n293:     # Private helpers \u2013 bus / async\n294:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n295: \n296:     def _wire_bus(self) -> None:\n297:         \"\"\"Subscribe to presence events on the internal event-bus.\"\"\"\n298:         self._subscriptions.append(self._bus.subscribe(\"presence.snapshot\", self._on_snapshot))\n299:         self._subscriptions.append(self._bus.subscribe(\"presence.updated\", self._on_presence_updated))\n300:         self._subscriptions.append(self._bus.subscribe(\"presence.left\", self._on_presence_left))\n301: \n302:     def _bootstrap_roster(self) -> None:\n303:         \"\"\"\n304:         Ask the backend for the initial roster list via RPC-style\n305:         request/response.  We use asyncio so as not to block the Qt loop.\n306:         \"\"\"\n307: \n308:         async def request_snapshot():\n309:             try:\n310:                 # fire-and-wait pattern: publish request, listen for snapshot\n311:                 # The backend replies on the same `presence.snapshot` topic\n312:                 self._bus.publish(\"presence.request_snapshot\", {\"requester\": self._current_user_id})\n313:                 # DONE: nothing, listener will receive snapshot asynchronously\n314:             except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n315:                 self._log.exception(\"Failed to request presence snapshot\")\n316:                 self.errorOccurred.emit(\"Presence initialisation failed\")\n317: \n318:         task = asyncio.create_task(request_snapshot(), name=\"presence.bootstrap\")\n319:         self._tasks.append(task)\n320: \n321:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n322:     # Bus event callbacks \u2013 sync context (fake bus).\n323:     # In production they run on a background thread; we therefore use\n324:     # Qt's `invokeMethod` to marshal into the main thread.\n325:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n326: \n327:     # The real bus dispatches events on worker threads.  Qt objects are\n328:     # not thread-safe; we hop back to the main thread via single-shot\n329:     # queued connections.  `QtCore.QMetaObject.invokeMethod` is used\n330:     # instead of Signals for performance / testability.\n331:     def _sync_to_qt(self, fn: types.FunctionType, /, *args):\n332:         QtCore.QMetaObject.invokeMethod(\n333:             self,\n334:             lambda: fn(*args),  # type: ignore[arg-type]  # pyqt signature magic\n335:             QtCore.Qt.QueuedConnection,\n336:         )\n337: \n338:     # Snapshot \u2013 full presence list\n339:     def _on_snapshot(self, payload):\n340:         self._log.debug(\"Received presence.snapshot %s\", payload)\n341:         entries = [\n342:             PresenceEntry(\n343:                 user_id=e[\"user_id\"],\n344:                 display_name=e[\"display_name\"],\n345:                 status=PresenceStatus[e[\"status\"].upper()],\n346:                 last_active=_dt.datetime.fromisoformat(e[\"last_active\"]),\n347:             )\n348:             for e in payload.get(\"members\", [])\n349:             if \"user_id\" in e\n350:         ]\n351:         self._sync_to_qt(self._model.upsert_entries, entries)\n352: \n353:     # Incremental updates\n354:     def _on_presence_updated(self, payload):\n355:         self._log.debug(\"presence.updated %s\", payload)\n356:         entry = PresenceEntry(\n357:             user_id=payload[\"user_id\"],\n358:             display_name=payload.get(\"display_name\", payload[\"user_id\"]),\n359:             status=PresenceStatus[payload[\"status\"].upper()],\n360:             last_active=_dt.datetime.utcnow(),\n361:         )\n362:         self._sync_to_qt(self._model.upsert_entries, [entry])\n363: \n364:     # User left \u2013 remove from list\n365:     def _on_presence_left(self, payload):\n366:         self._log.debug(\"presence.left %s\", payload)\n367:         self._sync_to_qt(self._model.remove_user, payload[\"user_id\"])\n368: \n369:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n370:     # Cleanup\n371:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n372: \n373:     def __del__(self):\n374:         for sub in self._subscriptions:\n375:             try:\n376:                 sub.dispose()\n377:             except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n378:                 self._log.warning(\"Failed to dispose subscription %s\", sub, exc_info=True)\n379:         for task in self._tasks:\n380:             task.cancel()\n381: ```",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021@16180b273d43410fcc114af7997ce2d2b28e392e/-/blob/flockdesk/modules/presence/viewmodel/presence_vm.py"
  }
}
15. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "flockdesk/modules/presence/view/presence_widget.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: ```python\\n2: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n3: flockdesk.modules.presence.view.presence_widget\\n4: ------------------------------------------------\\n5: Qt / PySide6 visual component that renders presence information for teammates\\n6: inside the FlockDesk desktop suite.\\n7: \\n8: This widget plays the \uf0b7V (View) part in the MVVM triad.  It is intentionally\\n9: *thin*: all business-logic lives in the ViewModel and/or EventBus handlers,\\n10: allowing us to hot-swap or unit-test the UI without touching domain code.\\n11: \\n12: The widget is:\\n13: \\n14:     \u2022 Subscribing to the global EventBus for presence/theme updates.\\n15:     \u2022 Displaying colleagues in a QListView with custom item-delegates so we\\n16:       can paint avatars, names and activity indicators.\\n17:     \u2022 Drag-and-drop aware \u2013 users can drag a colleague into another micro-UI\\n18:       (e.g. chat or whiteboard) to open a contextual collaboration pane.\\n19:     \u2022 Lightweight \u2013 starts instantly and survives theme changes without leaks.\\n20: \\n21: Author:  The FlockDesk team\\n22: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n23: from __future__ import annotations\\n24: \\n25: import logging\\n26: import sys\\n27: from dataclasses import dataclass\\n28: from typing import List, Optional\\n29: \\n30: from PySide6.QtCore import (QAbstractListModel, QByteArray, QModelIndex,\\n31:                             QObject, QPoint, Qt, Signal, Slot)\\n32: from PySide6.QtGui import (QColor, QDrag, QIcon, QPainter, QPixmap,\\n33:                            QStandardItemModel, QTextOption)\\n34: from PySide6.QtWidgets import (QApplication, QHBoxLayout, QLabel, QListView,\\n35:                                QPushButton, QSpacerItem, QStyle, QStyleOptionViewItem,\\n36:                                QStyledItemDelegate, QVBoxLayout, QWidget)\\n37: \\n38: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n39: # Optional project-local imports                                              #\\n40: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n41: \\n42: try:\\n43:     from flockdesk.core.event_bus import EventBus, Topic  # type: ignore\\n44:     from flockdesk.modules.presence.model import PresenceState  # type: ignore\\n45:     from flockdesk.shared.theming import ThemeManager  # type: ignore\\n46: except (ModuleNotFoundError, ImportError):\\n47:     # Fallback stubs to keep linters and RT preview alive\\n48:     class EventBus:  # noqa: D401\\n49:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Very small stub when running in isolation.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n50:         @staticmethod\\n51:         def subscribe(topic: str, listener: QObject, slot: str) -> None: ...\\n52:         @staticmethod\\n53:         def publish(topic: str, payload) -> None: ...\\n54: \\n55:     class PresenceState:  # noqa: D401\\n56:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Enum stub for online/offline/etc.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n57:         ONLINE = \\\"online\\\"\\n58:         AWAY = \\\"away\\\"\\n59:         OFFLINE = \\\"offline\\\"\\n60: \\n61:     class ThemeManager:  # noqa: D401\\n62:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Simplistic theme stub.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n63:         theme_changed = Signal(dict)\\n64: \\n65: logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\\n66: \\n67: \\n68: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n69: # Dataclasses & Models                                                        #\\n70: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n71: \\n72: @dataclass(slots=True)\\n73: class Colleague:\\n74:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Lightweight data container for a colleague row.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n75:     uid: str\\n76:     display_name: str\\n77:     avatar_path: str\\n78:     presence: PresenceState\\n79: \\n80: \\n81: class _PresenceListModel(QAbstractListModel):\\n82:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n83:     A trivial QAbstractListModel that exposes colleagues to the QListView.\\n84: \\n85:     This model is intentionally 100 % dumb: all mutations come from the\\n86:     PresenceViewModel which will feed new lists in one go.\\n87:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n88: \\n89:     # Roles used by the delegate for painting\\n90:     UID_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 1\\n91:     DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 2\\n92:     AVATAR_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 3\\n93:     PRESENCE_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 4\\n94: \\n95:     _ROLE_NAMES = {\\n96:         UID_ROLE: QByteArray(b\\\"uid\\\"),\\n97:         DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE: QByteArray(b\\\"display_name\\\"),\\n98:         AVATAR_ROLE: QByteArray(b\\\"avatar\\\"),\\n99:         PRESENCE_ROLE: QByteArray(b\\\"presence\\\"),\\n100:     }\\n101: \\n102:     def __init__(self, colleagues: Optional[List[Colleague]] = None, parent: QObject | None = None) -> None:\\n103:         super().__init__(parent)\\n104:         self._colleagues: List[Colleague] = colleagues or []\\n105: \\n106:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n107:     # Required overrides                                                    #\\n108:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n109: \\n110:     def rowCount(self, parent: QModelIndex = QModelIndex()) -> int:  # noqa: N802\\n111:         return 0 if parent.isValid() else len(self._colleagues)\\n112: \\n113:     def data(self, index: QModelIndex, role: int = Qt.DisplayRole):  # noqa: N802\\n114:         if not index.isValid():\\n115:             return None\\n116: \\n117:         try:\\n118:             colleague = self._colleagues[index.row()]\\n119:         except IndexError:\\n120:             return None\\n121: \\n122:         if role == Qt.DisplayRole:\\n123:             return colleague.display_name\\n124:         if role == self.UID_ROLE:\\n125:             return colleague.uid\\n126:         if role == self.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE:\\n127:             return colleague.display_name\\n128:         if role == self.AVATAR_ROLE:\\n129:             return colleague.avatar_path\\n130:         if role == self.PRESENCE_ROLE:\\n131:             return colleague.presence\\n132:         return None\\n133: \\n134:     def roleNames(self) -> dict[int, QByteArray]:  # noqa: N802\\n135:         return self._ROLE_NAMES\\n136: \\n137:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n138:     # Public API                                                            #\\n139:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n140: \\n141:     def update_content(self, colleagues: List[Colleague]) -> None:\\n142:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Replace the entire model dataset in one shot.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n143:         self.beginResetModel()\\n144:         self._colleagues = colleagues\\n145:         self.endResetModel()\\n146: \\n147: \\n148: #
