--------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n149: # Custom Delegate                                                             #\\n150: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n151: \\n152: class PresenceItemDelegate(QStyledItemDelegate):\\n153:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Paints each list item with avatar, name and presence indicator icon.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n154: \\n155:     AVATAR_SIZE = 32\\n156:     PADDING = 6\\n157: \\n158:     _PRESENCE_ICONS = {\\n159:         PresenceState.ONLINE: QIcon(\\\":/presence/online.svg\\\"),\\n160:         PresenceState.AWAY: QIcon(\\\":/presence/away.svg\\\"),\\n161:         PresenceState.OFFLINE: QIcon(\\\":/presence/offline.svg\\\"),\\n162:     }\\n163: \\n164:     def paint(self, painter: QPainter, option: QStyleOptionViewItem, index: QModelIndex) -> None:\\n165:         painter.save()\\n166:         try:\\n167:             # Draw selection highlight\\n168:             if option.state & QStyle.State_Selected:\\n169:                 painter.fillRect(option.rect, option.palette.highlight())\\n170: \\n171:             # Fetch data\\n172:             name: str = index.data(_PresenceListModel.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE)\\n173:             avatar_path: str = index.data(_PresenceListModel.AVATAR_ROLE)\\n174:             presence: PresenceState = index.data(_PresenceListModel.PRESENCE_ROLE)\\n175: \\n176:             # Avatar\\n177:             avatar_rect = option.rect.adjusted(self.PADDING, self.PADDING,\\n178:                                                -(option.rect.width() - self.AVATAR_SIZE - self.PADDING),\\n179:                                                -self.PADDING)\\n180:             avatar_pix = QPixmap(avatar_path).scaled(self.AVATAR_SIZE, self.AVATAR_SIZE,\\n181:                                                      Qt.KeepAspectRatio, Qt.SmoothTransformation)\\n182:             painter.drawPixmap(avatar_rect.topLeft(), avatar_pix)\\n183: \\n184:             # Presence indicator bottom-right of avatar\\n185:             presence_icon: QIcon = self._PRESENCE_ICONS.get(presence, QIcon())\\n186:             indicator_sz = 10\\n187:             indicator_rect = avatar_rect.adjusted(self.AVATAR_SIZE - indicator_sz,\\n188:                                                   self.AVATAR_SIZE - indicator_sz,\\n189:                                                   0, 0)\\n190:             presence_pix = presence_icon.pixmap(indicator_sz, indicator_sz)\\n191:             painter.drawPixmap(indicator_rect.topLeft(), presence_pix)\\n192: \\n193:             # User name\\n194:             text_rect = option.rect.adjusted(self.AVATAR_SIZE + 2 * self.PADDING,\\n195:                                              0, -self.PADDING, 0)\\n196:             painter.setPen(option.palette.text().color())\\n197:             text_option = QTextOption()\\n198:             text_option.setAlignment(Qt.AlignVCenter | Qt.AlignLeft)\\n199:             painter.drawText(text_rect, name, text_option)\\n200: \\n201:         except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\\n202:             logger.exception(\\\"Failed to paint presence delegate.\\\")\\n203:         finally:\\n204:             painter.restore()\\n205: \\n206:     def sizeHint(self, option: QStyleOptionViewItem, index: QModelIndex):  # noqa: N802\\n207:         base = super().sizeHint(option, index)\\n208:         return base.expandedTo(QSize(0, self.AVATAR_SIZE + 2 * self.PADDING))\\n209: \\n210: \\n211: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n212: # PresenceWidget                                                              #\\n213: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n214: \\n215: class PresenceWidget(QWidget):\\n216:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n217:     QWidget exposing team presence.\\n218: \\n219:     Responsibilities\\n220:     ----------------\\n221:     1. Build UI (list-view, refresh button)\\n222:     2. Bind ViewModel (subscribe to signals / event-bus)\\n223:     3. Bridge drag-and-drop to external micro-front-ends\\n224:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n225: \\n226:     # Emitted when the user drags a colleague over somewhere else\\n227:     colleagueDragStarted = Signal(str)  # uid\\n228: \\n229:     def __init__(self, view_model: \\\"PresenceViewModel\\\", parent: QWidget | None = None) -> None:\\n230:         super().__init__(parent)\\n231:         self._vm = view_model\\n232: \\n233:         self._build_ui()\\n234:         self._bind_view_model()\\n235:         self._connect_theme_changes()\\n236: \\n237:         # Enable drag source\\n238:         self._list_view.setDragEnabled(True)\\n239:         self._list_view.viewport().setAcceptDrops(False)\\n240:         self._list_view.setDefaultDropAction(Qt.IgnoreAction)\\n241: \\n242:         logger.debug(\\\"PresenceWidget initialised.\\\")\\n243: \\n244:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\\n245:     # UI                                                                  #\\n246:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\\n247: \\n248:     def _build_ui(self) -> None:\\n249:         self._list_view = QListView(self, objectName=\\\"presenceListView\\\")\\n250:         self._list_view.setSpacing(4)\\n251:         self._list_view.setUniformItemSizes(True)\\n252:         self._list_view.setSelectionMode(QListView.NoSelection)\\n253:         self._list_view.setItemDelegate(PresenceItemDelegate(self._list_view))\\n254: \\n255:         self._refresh_btn = QPushButton(self.tr(\\\"Refresh\\\"))\\n256:         self._refresh_btn.setToolTip(self.tr(\\\"Force a manual presence refresh\\\"))\\n257: \\n258:         # Top bar\\n259:         top_layout = QHBoxLayout()\\n260:         top_layout.addWidget(QLabel(self.tr(\\\"Presence\\\")))\\n261:         top_layout.addItem(QSpacerItem(0, 0, QSizePolicy.Expanding, QSizePolicy.Minimum))\\n262:         top_layout.addWidget(self._refresh_btn)\\n263: \\n264:         # Main layout\\n265:         layout = QVBoxLayout(self)\\n266:         layout.addLayout(top_layout)\\n267:         layout.addWidget(self._list_view)\\n268:         self.setLayout(layout)\\n269: \\n270:         # Model\\n271:         self._model = _PresenceListModel([])\\n272:         self._list_view.setModel(self._model)\\n273: \\n274:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\\n275:     # Data Binding                                                       #\\n276:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\\n277: \\n278:     def _bind_view_model(self) -> None:\\n279:         self._refresh_btn.clicked.connect(self._vm.refresh)\\n280:         self._vm.presence_changed.connect(self._on_presence_changed)\\n281: \\n282:         # For initial data\\n283:         self._on_presence_changed(self._vm.snapshot())\\n284: \\n285:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\\n286:     # Theme                                                              #\\n287:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\\n288: \\n289:     def _connect_theme_changes(self) -> None:\\n290:         if hasattr(ThemeManager, \\\"theme_changed\\\"):\\n291:             ThemeManager.theme_changed.connect(self._on_theme_changed)\\n292: \\n293:     @Slot(dict)\\n294:     def _on_theme_changed(self, theme_meta: dict) -> None:\\n295:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n296:         Triggered when the global theme changed.\\n297: \\n298:         For the sake of demo we only update the list spacing/palette; more\\n299:         thorough implementation would adapt colors in the delegate, etc.\\n300:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n301:         palette = self.palette()\\n302:         palette.setColor(self.backgroundRole(), QColor(theme_meta.get(\\\"window\\\", \\\"#FFFFFF\\\")))\\n303:         self.setPalette(palette)\\n304: \\n305:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\\n306:     # ViewModel slot                                                     #\\n307:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\\n308: \\n309:     @Slot(list)\\n310:     def _on_presence_changed(self, colleagues: List[Colleague]) -> None:\\n311:         logger.debug(\\\"PresenceWidget received %d colleagues.\\\", len(colleagues))\\n312:         self._model.update_content(colleagues)\\n313: \\n314:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\\n315:     # Drag-and-drop                                                      #\\n316:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\\n317: \\n318:     def startDrag(self, supportedActions: Qt.DropActions) -> None:  # noqa: N802\\n319:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Override QListView drag start behaviour.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n320:         index = self._list_view.currentIndex()\\n321:         if not index.isValid():\\n322:             return\\n323: \\n324:         uid = index.data(_PresenceListModel.UID_ROLE)\\n325:         display_name = index.data(_PresenceListModel.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE)\\n326:         avatar_path = index.data(_PresenceListModel.AVATAR_ROLE)\\n327: \\n328:         logger.debug(\\\"Start drag for user %s\\\", display_name)\\n329:         drag = QDrag(self)\\n330:         mime_data = self._vm.build_mime_data(uid)\\n331:         drag.setMimeData(mime_data)\\n332: \\n333:         # Pixmap for drag feedback\\n334:         pixmap = QPixmap(avatar_path).scaled(48, 48, Qt.KeepAspectRatio, Qt.SmoothTransformation)\\n335:         drag.setPixmap(pixmap)\\n336:         drag.setHotSpot(QPoint(pixmap.width() // 2, pixmap.height() // 2))\\n337: \\n338:         drag.exec_(Qt.MoveAction)\\n339:         self.colleagueDragStarted.emit(uid)\\n340: \\n341: \\n342: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n343: # ViewModel interface placeholder                                             #\\n344: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n345: \\n346: class PresenceViewModel(QObject):\\n347:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n348:     This interface is implemented elsewhere in the presence module.\\n349: \\n350:     PresenceWidget only relies on the signals described below.\\n351:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n352: \\n353:     presence_changed = Signal(list)  # List[Colleague]\\n354: \\n355:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\\n356:     # API expected by the widget                                         #\\n357:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\\n358: \\n359:     def refresh(self) -> None: ...  # noqa: D400\\n360: \\n361:     def snapshot(self) -> List[Colleague]:\\n362:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Return current presence list synchronously.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n363:         return []\\n364: \\n365:     def build_mime_data(self, uid: str) -> \\\"QMimeData\\\": ...  # noqa: D401\\n366: \\n367: \\n368: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n369: # Debug entry-point (can be stripped for prod)                                #\\n370: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n371: \\n372: if __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":  # pragma: no cover\\n373:     # Minimal harness to showcase the widget in isolation.\\n374:     logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)\\n375: \\n376:     class _DummyVM(PresenceViewModel):\\n377: \\n378:         def __init__(self) -> None:\\n379:             super().__init__()\\n380:             self._colleagues = [\\n381:                 Colleague(\\\"1\\\", \\\"Alice\\\", \\\":/avatars/alice.png\\\", PresenceState.ONLINE),\\n382:                 Colleague(\\\"2\\\", \\\"Bob\\\", \\\":/avatars/bob.png\\\", PresenceState.AWAY),\\n383:                 Colleague(\\\"3\\\", \\\"Charlie\\\", \\\":/avatars/charlie.png\\\", PresenceState.OFFLINE),\\n384:             ]\\n385: \\n386:         def refresh(self) -> None:\\n387:             logger.info(\\\"Refresh called from UI\\\")\\n388:             # In real case we might ask server; here we just toggle Bob\\n389:             import random\\n390:             self._colleagues[1].presence = random.choice(\\n391:                 [PresenceState.ONLINE, PresenceState.AWAY, PresenceState.OFFLINE])\\n392:             self.presence_changed.emit(self._colleagues)\\n393: \\n394:         def snapshot(self) -> List[Colleague]:\\n395:             return self._colleagues\\n396: \\n397:         def build_mime_data(self, uid: str):\\n398:             from PySide6.QtCore import QMimeData\\n399:             md = QMimeData()\\n400:             md.setText(uid)\\n401:             return md\\n402: \\n403:     app = QApplication(sys.argv)\\n404:     w = PresenceWidget(_DummyVM())\\n405:     w.resize(240, 320)\\n406:     w.show()\\n407:     sys.exit(app.exec())\\n408: ```\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021@16180b273d43410fcc114af7997ce2d2b28e392e/-/blob/flockdesk/modules/presence/view/presence_widget.py\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: ```python\n2: \"\"\"\n3: flockdesk.modules.presence.view.presence_widget\n4: ------------------------------------------------\n5: Qt / PySide6 visual component that renders presence information for teammates\n6: inside the FlockDesk desktop suite.\n7: \n8: This widget plays the \uf0b7V (View) part in the MVVM triad.  It is intentionally\n9: *thin*: all business-logic lives in the ViewModel and/or EventBus handlers,\n10: allowing us to hot-swap or unit-test the UI without touching domain code.\n11: \n12: The widget is:\n13: \n14:     \u2022 Subscribing to the global EventBus for presence/theme updates.\n15:     \u2022 Displaying colleagues in a QListView with custom item-delegates so we\n16:       can paint avatars, names and activity indicators.\n17:     \u2022 Drag-and-drop aware \u2013 users can drag a colleague into another micro-UI\n18:       (e.g. chat or whiteboard) to open a contextual collaboration pane.\n19:     \u2022 Lightweight \u2013 starts instantly and survives theme changes without leaks.\n20: \n21: Author:  The FlockDesk team\n22: \"\"\"\n23: from __future__ import annotations\n24: \n25: import logging\n26: import sys\n27: from dataclasses import dataclass\n28: from typing import List, Optional\n29: \n30: from PySide6.QtCore import (QAbstractListModel, QByteArray, QModelIndex,\n31:                             QObject, QPoint, Qt, Signal, Slot)\n32: from PySide6.QtGui import (QColor, QDrag, QIcon, QPainter, QPixmap,\n33:                            QStandardItemModel, QTextOption)\n34: from PySide6.QtWidgets import (QApplication, QHBoxLayout, QLabel, QListView,\n35:                                QPushButton, QSpacerItem, QStyle, QStyleOptionViewItem,\n36:                                QStyledItemDelegate, QVBoxLayout, QWidget)\n37: \n38: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n39: # Optional project-local imports                                              #\n40: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n41: \n42: try:\n43:     from flockdesk.core.event_bus import EventBus, Topic  # type: ignore\n44:     from flockdesk.modules.presence.model import PresenceState  # type: ignore\n45:     from flockdesk.shared.theming import ThemeManager  # type: ignore\n46: except (ModuleNotFoundError, ImportError):\n47:     # Fallback stubs to keep linters and RT preview alive\n48:     class EventBus:  # noqa: D401\n49:         \"\"\"Very small stub when running in isolation.\"\"\"\n50:         @staticmethod\n51:         def subscribe(topic: str, listener: QObject, slot: str) -> None: ...\n52:         @staticmethod\n53:         def publish(topic: str, payload) -> None: ...\n54: \n55:     class PresenceState:  # noqa: D401\n56:         \"\"\"Enum stub for online/offline/etc.\"\"\"\n57:         ONLINE = \"online\"\n58:         AWAY = \"away\"\n59:         OFFLINE = \"offline\"\n60: \n61:     class ThemeManager:  # noqa: D401\n62:         \"\"\"Simplistic theme stub.\"\"\"\n63:         theme_changed = Signal(dict)\n64: \n65: logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n66: \n67: \n68: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n69: # Dataclasses & Models                                                        #\n70: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n71: \n72: @dataclass(slots=True)\n73: class Colleague:\n74:     \"\"\"Lightweight data container for a colleague row.\"\"\"\n75:     uid: str\n76:     display_name: str\n77:     avatar_path: str\n78:     presence: PresenceState\n79: \n80: \n81: class _PresenceListModel(QAbstractListModel):\n82:     \"\"\"\n83:     A trivial QAbstractListModel that exposes colleagues to the QListView.\n84: \n85:     This model is intentionally 100 % dumb: all mutations come from the\n86:     PresenceViewModel which will feed new lists in one go.\n87:     \"\"\"\n88: \n89:     # Roles used by the delegate for painting\n90:     UID_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 1\n91:     DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 2\n92:     AVATAR_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 3\n93:     PRESENCE_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 4\n94: \n95:     _ROLE_NAMES = {\n96:         UID_ROLE: QByteArray(b\"uid\"),\n97:         DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE: QByteArray(b\"display_name\"),\n98:         AVATAR_ROLE: QByteArray(b\"avatar\"),\n99:         PRESENCE_ROLE: QByteArray(b\"presence\"),\n100:     }\n101: \n102:     def __init__(self, colleagues: Optional[List[Colleague]] = None, parent: QObject | None = None) -> None:\n103:         super().__init__(parent)\n104:         self._colleagues: List[Colleague] = colleagues or []\n105: \n106:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n107:     # Required overrides                                                    #\n108:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n109: \n110:     def rowCount(self, parent: QModelIndex = QModelIndex()) -> int:  # noqa: N802\n111:         return 0 if parent.isValid() else len(self._colleagues)\n112: \n113:     def data(self, index: QModelIndex, role: int = Qt.DisplayRole):  # noqa: N802\n114:         if not index.isValid():\n115:             return None\n116: \n117:         try:\n118:             colleague = self._colleagues[index.row()]\n119:         except IndexError:\n120:             return None\n121: \n122:         if role == Qt.DisplayRole:\n123:             return colleague.display_name\n124:         if role == self.UID_ROLE:\n125:             return colleague.uid\n126:         if role == self.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE:\n127:             return colleague.display_name\n128:         if role == self.AVATAR_ROLE:\n129:             return colleague.avatar_path\n130:         if role == self.PRESENCE_ROLE:\n131:             return colleague.presence\n132:         return None\n133: \n134:     def roleNames(self) -> dict[int, QByteArray]:  # noqa: N802\n135:         return self._ROLE_NAMES\n136: \n137:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n138:     # Public API                                                            #\n139:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n140: \n141:     def update_content(self, colleagues: List[Colleague]) -> None:\n142:         \"\"\"Replace the entire model dataset in one shot.\"\"\"\n143:         self.beginResetModel()\n144:         self._colleagues = colleagues\n145:         self.endResetModel()\n146: \n147: \n148: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n149: # Custom Delegate                                                             #\n150: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n151: \n152: class PresenceItemDelegate(QStyledItemDelegate):\n153:     \"\"\"Paints each list item with avatar, name and presence indicator icon.\"\"\"\n154: \n155:     AVATAR_SIZE = 32\n156:     PADDING = 6\n157: \n158:     _PRESENCE_ICONS = {\n159:         PresenceState.ONLINE: QIcon(\":/presence/online.svg\"),\n160:         PresenceState.AWAY: QIcon(\":/presence/away.svg\"),\n161:         PresenceState.OFFLINE: QIcon(\":/presence/offline.svg\"),\n162:     }\n163: \n164:     def paint(self, painter: QPainter, option: QStyleOptionViewItem, index: QModelIndex) -> None:\n165:         painter.save()\n166:         try:\n167:             # Draw selection highlight\n168:             if option.state & QStyle.State_Selected:\n169:                 painter.fillRect(option.rect, option.palette.highlight())\n170: \n171:             # Fetch data\n172:             name: str = index.data(_PresenceListModel.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE)\n173:             avatar_path: str = index.data(_PresenceListModel.AVATAR_ROLE)\n174:             presence: PresenceState = index.data(_PresenceListModel.PRESENCE_ROLE)\n175: \n176:             # Avatar\n177:             avatar_rect = option.rect.adjusted(self.PADDING, self.PADDING,\n178:                                                -(option.rect.width() - self.AVATAR_SIZE - self.PADDING),\n179:                                                -self.PADDING)\n180:             avatar_pix = QPixmap(avatar_path).scaled(self.AVATAR_SIZE, self.AVATAR_SIZE,\n181:                                                      Qt.KeepAspectRatio, Qt.SmoothTransformation)\n182:             painter.drawPixmap(avatar_rect.topLeft(), avatar_pix)\n183: \n184:             # Presence indicator bottom-right of avatar\n185:             presence_icon: QIcon = self._PRESENCE_ICONS.get(presence, QIcon())\n186:             indicator_sz = 10\n187:             indicator_rect = avatar_rect.adjusted(self.AVATAR_SIZE - indicator_sz,\n188:                                                   self.AVATAR_SIZE - indicator_sz,\n189:                                                   0, 0)\n190:             presence_pix = presence_icon.pixmap(indicator_sz, indicator_sz)\n191:             painter.drawPixmap(indicator_rect.topLeft(), presence_pix)\n192: \n193:             # User name\n194:             text_rect = option.rect.adjusted(self.AVATAR_SIZE + 2 * self.PADDING,\n195:                                              0, -self.PADDING, 0)\n196:             painter.setPen(option.palette.text().color())\n197:             text_option = QTextOption()\n198:             text_option.setAlignment(Qt.AlignVCenter | Qt.AlignLeft)\n199:             painter.drawText(text_rect, name, text_option)\n200: \n201:         except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n202:             logger.exception(\"Failed to paint presence delegate.\")\n203:         finally:\n204:             painter.restore()\n205: \n206:     def sizeHint(self, option: QStyleOptionViewItem, index: QModelIndex):  # noqa: N802\n207:         base = super().sizeHint(option, index)\n208:         return base.expandedTo(QSize(0, self.AVATAR_SIZE + 2 * self.PADDING))\n209: \n210: \n211: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n212: # PresenceWidget                                                              #\n213: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n214: \n215: class PresenceWidget(QWidget):\n216:     \"\"\"\n217:     QWidget exposing team presence.\n218: \n219:     Responsibilities\n220:     ----------------\n221:     1. Build UI (list-view, refresh button)\n222:     2. Bind ViewModel (subscribe to signals / event-bus)\n223:     3. Bridge drag-and-drop to external micro-front-ends\n224:     \"\"\"\n225: \n226:     # Emitted when the user drags a colleague over somewhere else\n227:     colleagueDragStarted = Signal(str)  # uid\n228: \n229:     def __init__(self, view_model: \"PresenceViewModel\", parent: QWidget | None = None) -> None:\n230:         super().__init__(parent)\n231:         self._vm = view_model\n232: \n233:         self._build_ui()\n234:         self._bind_view_model()\n235:         self._connect_theme_changes()\n236: \n237:         # Enable drag source\n238:         self._list_view.setDragEnabled(True)\n239:         self._list_view.viewport().setAcceptDrops(False)\n240:         self._list_view.setDefaultDropAction(Qt.IgnoreAction)\n241: \n242:         logger.debug(\"PresenceWidget initialised.\")\n243: \n244:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n245:     # UI                                                                  #\n246:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n247: \n248:     def _build_ui(self) -> None:\n249:         self._list_view = QListView(self, objectName=\"presenceListView\")\n250:         self._list_view.setSpacing(4)\n251:         self._list_view.setUniformItemSizes(True)\n252:         self._list_view.setSelectionMode(QListView.NoSelection)\n253:         self._list_view.setItemDelegate(PresenceItemDelegate(self._list_view))\n254: \n255:         self._refresh_btn = QPushButton(self.tr(\"Refresh\"))\n256:         self._refresh_btn.setToolTip(self.tr(\"Force a manual presence refresh\"))\n257: \n258:         # Top bar\n259:         top_layout = QHBoxLayout()\n260:         top_layout.addWidget(QLabel(self.tr(\"Presence\")))\n261:         top_layout.addItem(QSpacerItem(0, 0, QSizePolicy.Expanding, QSizePolicy.Minimum))\n262:         top_layout.addWidget(self._refresh_btn)\n263: \n264:         # Main layout\n265:         layout = QVBoxLayout(self)\n266:         layout.addLayout(top_layout)\n267:         layout.addWidget(self._list_view)\n268:         self.setLayout(layout)\n269: \n270:         # Model\n271:         self._model = _PresenceListModel([])\n272:         self._list_view.setModel(self._model)\n273: \n274:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n275:     # Data Binding                                                       #\n276:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n277: \n278:     def _bind_view_model(self) -> None:\n279:         self._refresh_btn.clicked.connect(self._vm.refresh)\n280:         self._vm.presence_changed.connect(self._on_presence_changed)\n281: \n282:         # For initial data\n283:         self._on_presence_changed(self._vm.snapshot())\n284: \n285:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n286:     # Theme                                                              #\n287:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n288: \n289:     def _connect_theme_changes(self) -> None:\n290:         if hasattr(ThemeManager, \"theme_changed\"):\n291:             ThemeManager.theme_changed.connect(self._on_theme_changed)\n292: \n293:     @Slot(dict)\n294:     def _on_theme_changed(self, theme_meta: dict) -> None:\n295:         \"\"\"\n296:         Triggered when the global theme changed.\n297: \n298:         For the sake of demo we only update the list spacing/palette; more\n299:         thorough implementation would adapt colors in the delegate, etc.\n300:         \"\"\"\n301:         palette = self.palette()\n302:         palette.setColor(self.backgroundRole(), QColor(theme_meta.get(\"window\", \"#FFFFFF\")))\n303:         self.setPalette(palette)\n304: \n305:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n306:     # ViewModel slot                                                     #\n307:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n308: \n309:     @Slot(list)\n310:     def _on_presence_changed(self, colleagues: List[Colleague]) -> None:\n311:         logger.debug(\"PresenceWidget received %d colleagues.\", len(colleagues))\n312:         self._model.update_content(colleagues)\n313: \n314:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n315:     # Drag-and-drop                                                      #\n316:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n317: \n318:     def startDrag(self, supportedActions: Qt.DropActions) -> None:  # noqa: N802\n319:         \"\"\"Override QListView drag start behaviour.\"\"\"\n320:         index = self._list_view.currentIndex()\n321:         if not index.isValid():\n322:             return\n323: \n324:         uid = index.data(_PresenceListModel.UID_ROLE)\n325:         display_name = index.data(_PresenceListModel.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE)\n326:         avatar_path = index.data(_PresenceListModel.AVATAR_ROLE)\n327: \n328:         logger.debug(\"Start drag for user %s\", display_name)\n329:         drag = QDrag(self)\n330:         mime_data = self._vm.build_mime_data(uid)\n331:         drag.setMimeData(mime_data)\n332: \n333:         # Pixmap for drag feedback\n334:         pixmap = QPixmap(avatar_path).scaled(48, 48, Qt.KeepAspectRatio, Qt.SmoothTransformation)\n335:         drag.setPixmap(pixmap)\n336:         drag.setHotSpot(QPoint(pixmap.width() // 2, pixmap.height() // 2))\n337: \n338:         drag.exec_(Qt.MoveAction)\n339:         self.colleagueDragStarted.emit(uid)\n340: \n341: \n342: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n343: # ViewModel interface placeholder                                             #\n344: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n345: \n346: class PresenceViewModel(QObject):\n347:     \"\"\"\n348:     This interface is implemented elsewhere in the presence module.\n349: \n350:     PresenceWidget only relies on the signals described below.\n351:     \"\"\"\n352: \n353:     presence_changed = Signal(list)  # List[Colleague]\n354: \n355:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n356:     # API expected by the widget                                         #\n357:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n358: \n359:     def refresh(self) -> None: ...  # noqa: D400\n360: \n361:     def snapshot(self) -> List[Colleague]:\n362:         \"\"\"Return current presence list synchronously.\"\"\"\n363:         return []\n364: \n365:     def build_mime_data(self, uid: str) -> \"QMimeData\": ...  # noqa: D401\n366: \n367: \n368: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n369: # Debug entry-point (can be stripped for prod)                                #\n370: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n371: \n372: if __name__ == \"__main__\":  # pragma: no cover\n373:     # Minimal harness to showcase the widget in isolation.\n374:     logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)\n375: \n376:     class _DummyVM(PresenceViewModel):\n377: \n378:         def __init__(self) -> None:\n379:             super().__init__()\n380:             self._colleagues = [\n381:                 Colleague(\"1\", \"Alice\", \":/avatars/alice.png\", PresenceState.ONLINE),\n382:                 Colleague(\"2\", \"Bob\", \":/avatars/bob.png\", PresenceState.AWAY),\n383:                 Colleague(\"3\", \"Charlie\", \":/avatars/charlie.png\", PresenceState.OFFLINE),\n384:             ]\n385: \n386:         def refresh(self) -> None:\n387:             logger.info(\"Refresh called from UI\")\n388:             # In real case we might ask server; here we just toggle Bob\n389:             import random\n390:             self._colleagues[1].presence = random.choice(\n391:                 [PresenceState.ONLINE, PresenceState.AWAY, PresenceState.OFFLINE])\n392:             self.presence_changed.emit(self._colleagues)\n393: \n394:         def snapshot(self) -> List[Colleague]:\n395:             return self._colleagues\n396: \n397:         def build_mime_data(self, uid: str):\n398:             from PySide6.QtCore import QMimeData\n399:             md = QMimeData()\n400:             md.setText(uid)\n401:             return md\n402: \n403:     app = QApplication(sys.argv)\n404:     w = PresenceWidget(_DummyVM())\n405:     w.resize(240, 320)\n406:     w.show()\n407:     sys.exit(app.exec())\n408: ```",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021@16180b273d43410fcc114af7997ce2d2b28e392e/-/blob/flockdesk/modules/presence/view/presence_widget.py"
  }
}
16. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "flockdesk/shared/widgets/avatar_widget.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: ```python\\n2: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n3: flockdesk.shared.widgets.avatar_widget\\n4: --------------------------------------\\n5: \\n6: Reusable widget that displays (and manages) a user\u2019s avatar picture with\\n7: real-time presence indication, theme awareness, drag-and-drop support and an\\n8: extensible context-menu.\\n9: \\n10: The widget is intended to be used throughout the FlockDesk desktop client:\\n11: in chat lists, white-board collaborators, community dashboards, etc.\\n12: \\n13: Typical usage\\n14: ~~~~~~~~~~~~~\\n15: avatar = AvatarWidget(\\n16:     user_id=\\\"42\\\",\\n17:     event_bus=inject.instance(EventBus),          # optional\\n18:     theme_manager=inject.instance(ThemeManager),  # optional\\n19: )\\n20: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n21: \\n22: from __future__ import annotations\\n23: \\n24: import logging\\n25: import os\\n26: import sys\\n27: from collections import OrderedDict\\n28: from pathlib import Path\\n29: from typing import Optional\\n30: \\n31: from PySide6.QtCore import (QEvent, QPoint, QRunnable, QSize, Qt, QThreadPool,\\n32:                             QUrl, Signal, Slot)\\n33: from PySide6.QtGui import (QAction, QContextMenuEvent, QDragEnterEvent,\\n34:                            QDropEvent, QIcon, QImage, QMouseEvent, QPainter,\\n35:                            QPixmap)\\n36: from PySide6.QtWidgets import (QFileDialog, QMenu, QMessageBox, QWidget)\\n37: \\n38: __all__ = [\\\"AvatarWidget\\\"]\\n39: \\n40: log = logging.getLogger(__name__)\\n41: \\n42: # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n43: # (Optional) third-party FlockDesk services\\n44: # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n45: \\n46: \\n47: class _DummyBus:\\n48:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Stand-in for the real application-wide EventBus (if not imported).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n49: \\n50:     def subscribe(self, *_a, **_kw):\\n51:         pass\\n52: \\n53:     def publish(self, *_a, **_kw):\\n54:         pass\\n55: \\n56: \\n57: class _DummyTheme:\\n58:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Stand-in for the real ThemeManager (hot-swappable themes).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n59: \\n60:     def color(self, key: str, fallback: str = \\\"#CCCCCC\\\") -> str:\\n61:         return fallback\\n62: \\n63: \\n64: try:\\n65:     from flockdesk.core.event_bus import EventBus\\n66: except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\\n67:     EventBus = _DummyBus  # type: ignore\\n68: \\n69: \\n70: try:\\n71:     from flockdesk.core.theme import ThemeManager\\n72: except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\\n73:     ThemeManager = _DummyTheme  # type: ignore\\n74: \\n75: \\n76: # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n77: # Pixmap caching helpers\\n78: # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n79: \\n80: \\n81: class _PixmapCache:\\n82:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n83:     Very small LRU cache that stores pixmaps keyed by absolute path.\\n84:     Prevents re-decoding identical images when the avatar is shown in multiple\\n85:     locations at once.\\n86:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n87: \\n88:     _MAX_ENTRIES = 32\\n89:     _store: OrderedDict[str, QPixmap] = OrderedDict()\\n90: \\n91:     @classmethod\\n92:     def get(cls, path: str) -> Optional[QPixmap]:\\n93:         try:\\n94:             pixmap = cls._store.pop(path)\\n95:             cls._store[path] = pixmap  # move to end (most recently used)\\n96:             return pixmap\\n97:         except KeyError:\\n98:             return None\\n99: \\n100:     @classmethod\\n101:     def put(cls, path: str, pixmap: QPixmap) -> None:\\n102:         cls._store[path] = pixmap\\n103:         cls._store.move_to_end(path)\\n104:         # evict least recently used\\n105:         while len(cls._store) > cls._MAX_ENTRIES:\\n106:             cls._store.popitem(last=False)\\n107: \\n108: \\n109: # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n110: # Asynchronous loader used to decode / scale the avatar off the UI thread\\n111: # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n112: \\n113: \\n114: class _AvatarLoader(QRunnable):\\n115:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n116:     Decode and scale a QPixmap in a thread-pool to avoid UI jank when very\\n117:     large images are supplied.\\n118:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n119: \\n120:     def __init__(\\n121:         self,\\n122:         path: str,\\n123:         target_size: QSize,\\n124:         on_loaded: \\\"Signal[QPixmap]\\\",\\n125:     ) -> None:\\n126:         super().__init__()\\n127:         self._path = path\\n128:         self._target_size = target_size\\n129:         self._on_loaded = on_loaded\\n130: \\n131:     def run(self) -> None:  # noqa: D401\\n132:         try:\\n133:             image = QImage(self._path)\\n134:             if image.isNull():\\n135:                 raise ValueError(f\\\"Unable to load image: {self._path}\\\")\\n136: \\n137:             # Preserve aspect ratio, crop to square, then scale\\n138:             side = min(image.width(), image.height())\\n139:             cropped = image.copy(\\n140:                 (image.width() - side) // 2,\\n141:                 (image.height() - side) // 2,\\n142:                 side,\\n143:                 side,\\n144:             )\\n145:             scaled = cropped.scaled(\\n146:                 self._target_size,\\n147:                 Qt.KeepAspectRatio,\\n148:                 Qt.SmoothTransformation,\\n149:             )\\n150:             pixmap = QPixmap.fromImage(scaled)\\n151:             _PixmapCache.put(self._path, pixmap)\\n152:             self._on_loaded.emit(pixmap)\\n153:         except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\\n154:             log.exception(\\\"Failed to load avatar: %s\\\", self._path)\\n155: \\n156: \\n157: # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n158: # Avatar widget\\n159: # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n160: \\n161: \\n162: class AvatarWidget(QWidget):\\n163:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n164:     Displays a round avatar with an optional presence badge.\\n165: \\n166:     Signals\\n167:     -------\\n168:     clicked: Signal[None]\\n169:         Emitted on left mouse click.\\n170:     avatarChanged: Signal[str]\\n171:         Emitted when the user changes his / her avatar locally.\\n172:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n173: \\n174:     clicked = Signal()\\n175:     avatarChanged = Signal(str)\\n176: \\n177:     _DRAG_MIME_WHITELIST = {\\n178:         \\\"image/png\\\",\\n179:         \\\"image/jpeg\\\",\\n180:         \\\"image/webp\\\",\\n181:         \\\"image/gif\\\",\\n182:     }\\n183:     _DEFAULT_SIZE = 64\\n184: \\n185:     def __init__(\\n186:         self,\\n187:         user_id: str,\\n188:         *,\\n189:         size: int | None = None,\\n190:         event_bus: Optional[EventBus] = None,\\n191:         theme_manager: Optional[ThemeManager] = None,\\n192:         parent: Optional[QWidget] = None,\\n193:     ) -> None:\\n194:         super().__init__(parent)\\n195: \\n196:         self.setAcceptDrops(True)\\n197:         self.setCursor(Qt.PointingHandCursor)\\n198:         self.setFixedSize(QSize(size or self._DEFAULT_SIZE, size or self._DEFAULT_SIZE))\\n199: \\n200:         # dependencies\\n201:         self._user_id = user_id\\n202:         self._event_bus = event_bus or _DummyBus()\\n203:         self._theme = theme_manager or _DummyTheme()\\n204: \\n205:         # runtime state\\n206:         self._avatar_path: Optional[str] = None\\n207:         self._pixmap: Optional[QPixmap] = None\\n208:         self._presence: str = \\\"offline\\\"  # offline, online, busy, away \u2026\\n209:         self._thread_pool = QThreadPool.globalInstance()\\n210: \\n211:         self._install_event_listeners()\\n212: \\n213:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\\n214:     # Public API\\n215:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\\n216: \\n217:     def avatar(self) -> Optional[str]:\\n218:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Return absolute path of current avatar (may be None).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n219:         return self._avatar_path\\n220: \\n221:     def presence(self) -> str:\\n222:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Return the current presence key (online / busy / \u2026).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n223:         return self._presence\\n224: \\n225:     @Slot(str)\\n226:     def set_avatar(self, path: str) -> None:\\n227:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Update the avatar from the given image path.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n228:         path = os.path.abspath(path)\\n229:         if not os.path.exists(path):\\n230:             log.warning(\\\"Avatar path does not exist: %s\\\", path)\\n231:             return\\n232: \\n233:         self._avatar_path = path\\n234:         cached = _PixmapCache.get(path)\\n235:         if cached is not None:\\n236:             self._pixmap = cached\\n237:             self.update()\\n238:         else:\\n239:             loader = _AvatarLoader(\\n240:                 path=path,\\n241:                 target_size=self.size(),\\n242:                 on_loaded=self._on_async_loaded,\\n243:             )\\n244:             self._thread_pool.start(loader)\\n245: \\n246:     @Slot(str)\\n247:     def set_presence(self, state: str) -> None:\\n248:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Update presence overlay, e.g. from the event-bus.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n249:         if state == self._presence:\\n250:             return\\n251:         self._presence = state\\n252:         self.update()\\n253: \\n254:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\\n255:     # Event handling\\n256:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\\n257: \\n258:     def paintEvent(self, event):  # noqa: D401\\n259:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Paint circular avatar with presence badge.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n260:         painter = QPainter(self)\\n261:         painter.setRenderHint(QPainter.Antialiasing)\\n262: \\n263:         if self._pixmap and not self._pixmap.isNull():\\n264:             painter.setBrush(Qt.NoBrush)\\n265:             path = self._rounded_rect_path()\\n266:             painter.setClipPath(path)\\n267:             painter.drawPixmap(self.rect(), self._pixmap)\\n268:         else:\\n269:             # fallback placeholder\\n270:             painter.setBrush(\\n271:                 self._theme.color(\\\"avatar.background\\\", \\\"#8F8F8F\\\")\\n272:             )\\n273:             painter.setPen(Qt.NoPen)\\n274:             painter.drawEllipse(self.rect())\\n275: \\n276:         self._draw_presence_indicator(painter)\\n277: \\n278:     def mousePressEvent(self, event: QMouseEvent):  # noqa: D401\\n279:         if event.button() == Qt.LeftButton:\\n280:             self.clicked.emit()\\n281:         super().mousePressEvent(event)\\n282: \\n283:     def dragEnterEvent(self, event: QDragEnterEvent):  # noqa: D401\\n284:         if any(fmt in event.mimeData().formats() for fmt in self._DRAG_MIME_WHITELIST):\\n285:             event.acceptProposedAction()\\n286: \\n287:     def dropEvent(self, event: QDropEvent):  # noqa: D401\\n288:         url: QUrl\\n289:         for url in event.mimeData().urls():\\n290:             if url.isLocalFile():\\n291:                 self._handle_new_avatar(Path(url.toLocalFile()))\\n292:                 event.acceptProposedAction()\\n293:                 return\\n294:         QMessageBox.warning(self, \\\"Invalid file\\\", \\\"Please drop a local image file.\\\")\\n295: \\n296:     def contextMenuEvent(self, event: QContextMenuEvent):  # noqa: D401\\n297:         menu = QMenu(self)\\n298: \\n299:         choose_action = QAction(\\\"Change avatar \u2026\\\", self)\\n300:         choose_action.triggered.connect(self._choose_avatar)\\n301:         menu.addAction(choose_action)\\n302: \\n303:         menu.addSeparator()\\n304: \\n305:         if self._avatar_path:\\n306:             reveal = QAction(\\\"Reveal in Explorer\\\", self)\\n307:             reveal.triggered.connect(self._reveal_in_file_manager)\\n308:             menu.addAction(reveal)\\n309: \\n310:         menu.exec(event.globalPos())\\n311: \\n312:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\\n313:     # Internal helpers\\n314:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\\n315: \\n316:     def _install_event_listeners(self) -> None:\\n317:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n318:         Subscribe to application-wide events: theme change, presence updates\\n319:         for our user, etc.\\n320:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n321:         # presence update\\n322:         if isinstance(self._event_bus, EventBus):\\n323:             self._event_bus.subscribe(\\n324:                 \\\"presence.changed\\\",\\n325:                 handler=lambda e: (\\n326:                     self.set_presence(e.payload[\\\"state\\\"])\\n327:                     if e.payload.get(\\\"user_id\\\") == self._user_id\\n328:                     else None\\n329:                 ),\\n330:             )\\n331: \\n332:         # theme hot reload\\n333:         if isinstance(self._theme, ThemeManager):\\n334:             self._theme.themeChanged.connect(lambda: self.update())  # type: ignore\\n335: \\n336:     @Slot(QPixmap)\\n337:     def _on_async_loaded(self, pixmap: QPixmap) -> None:\\n338:         self._pixmap = pixmap\\n339:         self.update()\\n340: \\n341:     def _rounded_rect_path(self):\\n342:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Returns a QPainterPath of a circle matching widget size.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n343:         from PySide6.QtGui import QPainterPath\\n344: \\n345:         path = QPainterPath()\\n346:         path.addEllipse(self.rect())\\n347:         return path\\n348: \\n349:     def _draw_presence_indicator(self, painter: QPainter) -> None:\\n350:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Paint a small presence dot on bottom-right corner.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n351:         colors = {\\n352:             \\\"online\\\": \\\"#4CAF50\\\",\\n353:             \\\"busy\\\": \\\"#F44336\\\",\\n354:             \\\"away\\\": \\\"#FFB300\\\",\\n355:             \\\"offline\\\": \\\"#9E9E9E\\\",\\n356:         }\\n357:         radius = max(6, self.width() // 6)\\n358:         center = QPoint(self.width() - radius, self.height() - radius)\\n359: \\n360:         painter.setPen(Qt.NoPen)\\n361:         painter.setBrush(colors.get(self._presence, \\\"#9E9E9E\\\"))\\n362:         painter.drawEllipse(center, radius, radius)\\n363: \\n364:     # --------------------------------------------------------------\\n365:     # Context-menu actions\\n366:     # --------------------------------------------------------------\\n367: \\n368:     def _choose_avatar(self) -> None:\\n369:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Open file-dialog for user to pick a new avatar picture.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n370:         filename, _ = QFileDialog.getOpenFileName(\\n371:             self,\\n372:             \\\"Select Avatar Picture\\\",\\n373:             \\\"\\\",\\n374:             \\\"Images (*.png *.jpg *.jpeg *.webp *.gif)\\\",\\n375:         )\\n376:         if filename:\\n377:             self._handle_new_avatar(Path(filename))\\n378: \\n379:     def _handle_new_avatar(self, path: Path) -> None:\\n380:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Validate and persist the chosen avatar (drag-drop or file-dialog).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n381:         if not path.exists():\\n382:             return\\n383:         if not path.suffix.lower() in {\\\".png\\\", \\\".jpg\\\", \\\".jpeg\\\", \\\".webp\\\", \\\".gif\\\"}:\\n384:             QMessageBox.warning(self, \\\"Unsupported file\\\", \\\"File is not a supported image type.\\\")\\n385:             return\\n386: \\n387:         self.set_avatar(str(path))\\n388:         self.avatarChanged.emit(str(path))\\n389:         # Publish to event-bus so other processes may sync the change\\n390:         if isinstance(self._event_bus, EventBus):\\n391:             self._event_bus.publish(  # type: ignore\\n392:                 \\\"avatar.changed\\\",\\n393:                 user_id=self._user_id,\\n394:                 path=str(path),\\n395:             )\\n396: \\n397:     def _reveal_in_file_manager(self) -> None:\\n398:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Platform-specific file manager reveal of the avatar file.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n399:         if not self._avatar_path:\\n400:             return\\n401:         try:\\n402:             if sys.platform.startswith(\\\"darwin\\\"):\\n403:                 from subprocess import call\\n404: \\n405:                 call([\\\"open\\\", \\\"--\\\", self._avatar_path])\\n406:             elif os.name == \\\"nt\\\":\\n407:                 os.startfile(self._avatar_path)  # type: ignore  # pylint: disable=protected-access\\n408:             else:  # linux, etc.\\n409:                 from subprocess import call\\n410: \\n411:                 call([\\\"xdg-open\\\", self._avatar_path])\\n412:         except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\\n413:             log.exception(\\\"Could not open file manager for %s\\\", self._avatar_path)\\n414: ```\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021@16180b273d43410fcc114af7997ce2d2b28e392e/-/blob/flockdesk/shared/widgets/avatar_widget.py\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: ```python\n2: \"\"\"\n3: flockdesk.shared.widgets.avatar_widget\n4: --------------------------------------\n5: \n6: Reusable widget that displays (and manages) a user\u2019s avatar picture with\n7: real-time presence indication, theme awareness, drag-and-drop support and an\n8: extensible context-menu.\n9: \n10: The widget is intended to be used throughout the FlockDesk desktop client:\n11: in chat lists, white-board collaborators, community dashboards, etc.\n12: \n13: Typical usage\n14: ~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n15: avatar = AvatarWidget(\n16:     user_id=\"42\",\n17:     event_bus=inject.instance(EventBus),          # optional\n18:     theme_manager=inject.instance(ThemeManager),  # optional\n19: )\n20: \"\"\"\n21: \n22: from __future__ import annotations\n23: \n24: import logging\n25: import os\n26: import sys\n27: from collections import OrderedDict\n28: from pathlib import Path\n29: from typing import Optional\n30: \n31: from PySide6.QtCore import (QEvent, QPoint, QRunnable, QSize, Qt, QThreadPool,\n32:                             QUrl, Signal, Slot)\n33: from PySide6.QtGui import (QAction, QContextMenuEvent, QDragEnterEvent,\n34:                            QDropEvent, QIcon, QImage, QMouseEvent, QPainter,\n35:                            QPixmap)\n36: from PySide6.QtWidgets import (QFileDialog, QMenu, QMessageBox, QWidget)\n37: \n38: __all__ = [\"AvatarWidget\"]\n39: \n40: log = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n41: \n42: # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n43: # (Optional) third-party FlockDesk services\n44: # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n45: \n46: \n47: class _DummyBus:\n48:     \"\"\"Stand-in for the real application-wide EventBus (if not imported).\"\"\"\n49: \n50:     def subscribe(self, *_a, **_kw):\n51:         pass\n52: \n53:     def publish(self, *_a, **_kw):\n54:         pass\n55: \n56: \n57: class _DummyTheme:\n58:     \"\"\"Stand-in for the real ThemeManager (hot-swappable themes).\"\"\"\n59: \n60:     def color(self, key: str, fallback: str = \"#CCCCCC\") -> str:\n61:         return fallback\n62: \n63: \n64: try:\n65:     from flockdesk.core.event_bus import EventBus\n66: except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n67:     EventBus = _DummyBus  # type: ignore\n68: \n69: \n70: try:\n71:     from flockdesk.core.theme import ThemeManager\n72: except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n73:     ThemeManager = _DummyTheme  # type: ignore\n74: \n75: \n76: # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n77: # Pixmap caching helpers\n78: # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n79: \n80: \n81: class _PixmapCache:\n82:     \"\"\"\n83:     Very small LRU cache that stores pixmaps keyed by absolute path.\n84:     Prevents re-decoding identical images when the avatar is shown in multiple\n85:     locations at once.\n86:     \"\"\"\n87: \n88:     _MAX_ENTRIES = 32\n89:     _store: OrderedDict[str, QPixmap] = OrderedDict()\n90: \n91:     @classmethod\n92:     def get(cls, path: str) -> Optional[QPixmap]:\n93:         try:\n94:             pixmap = cls._store.pop(path)\n95:             cls._store[path] = pixmap  # move to end (most recently used)\n96:             return pixmap\n97:         except KeyError:\n98:             return None\n99: \n100:     @classmethod\n101:     def put(cls, path: str, pixmap: QPixmap) -> None:\n102:         cls._store[path] = pixmap\n103:         cls._store.move_to_end(path)\n104:         # evict least recently used\n105:         while len(cls._store) > cls._MAX_ENTRIES:\n106:             cls._store.popitem(last=False)\n107: \n108: \n109: # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n110: # Asynchronous loader used to decode / scale the avatar off the UI thread\n111: # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n112: \n113: \n114: class _AvatarLoader(QRunnable):\n115:     \"\"\"\n116:     Decode and scale a QPixmap in a thread-pool to avoid UI jank when very\n117:     large images are supplied.\n118:     \"\"\"\n119: \n120:     def __init__(\n121:         self,\n122:         path: str,\n123:         target_size: QSize,\n124:         on_loaded: \"Signal[QPixmap]\",\n125:     ) -> None:\n126:         super().__init__()\n127:         self._path = path\n128:         self._target_size = target_size\n129:         self._on_loaded = on_loaded\n130: \n131:     def run(self) -> None:  # noqa: D401\n132:         try:\n133:             image = QImage(self._path)\n134:             if image.isNull():\n135:                 raise ValueError(f\"Unable to load image: {self._path}\")\n136: \n137:             # Preserve aspect ratio, crop to square, then scale\n138:             side = min(image.width(), image.height())\n139:             cropped = image.copy(\n140:                 (image.width() - side) // 2,\n141:                 (image.height() - side) // 2,\n142:                 side,\n143:                 side,\n144:             )\n145:             scaled = cropped.scaled(\n146:                 self._target_size,\n147:                 Qt.KeepAspectRatio,\n148:                 Qt.SmoothTransformation,\n149:             )\n150:             pixmap = QPixmap.fromImage(scaled)\n151:             _PixmapCache.put(self._path, pixmap)\n152:             self._on_loaded.emit(pixmap)\n153:         except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n154:             log.exception(\"Failed to load avatar: %s\", self._path)\n155: \n156: \n157: # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n158: # Avatar widget\n159: # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n160: \n161: \n162: class AvatarWidget(QWidget):\n163:     \"\"\"\n164:     Displays a round avatar with an optional presence badge.\n165: \n166:     Signals\n167:     -------\n168:     clicked: Signal[None]\n169:         Emitted on left mouse click.\n170:     avatarChanged: Signal[str]\n171:         Emitted when the user changes his / her avatar locally.\n172:     \"\"\"\n173: \n174:     clicked = Signal()\n175:     avatarChanged = Signal(str)\n176: \n177:     _DRAG_MIME_WHITELIST = {\n178:         \"image/png\",\n179:         \"image/jpeg\",\n180:         \"image/webp\",\n181:         \"image/gif\",\n182:     }\n183:     _DEFAULT_SIZE = 64\n184: \n185:     def __init__(\n186:         self,\n187:         user_id: str,\n188:         *,\n189:         size: int | None = None,\n190:         event_bus: Optional[EventBus] = None,\n191:         theme_manager: Optional[ThemeManager] = None,\n192:         parent: Optional[QWidget] = None,\n193:     ) -> None:\n194:         super().__init__(parent)\n195: \n196:         self.setAcceptDrops(True)\n197:         self.setCursor(Qt.PointingHandCursor)\n198:         self.setFixedSize(QSize(size or self._DEFAULT_SIZE, size or self._DEFAULT_SIZE))\n199: \n200:         # dependencies\n201:         self._user_id = user_id\n202:         self._event_bus = event_bus or _DummyBus()\n203:         self._theme = theme_manager or _DummyTheme()\n204: \n205:         # runtime state\n206:         self._avatar_path: Optional[str] = None\n207:         self._pixmap: Optional[QPixmap] = None\n208:         self._presence: str = \"offline\"  # offline, online, busy, away \u2026\n209:         self._thread_pool = QThreadPool.globalInstance()\n210: \n211:         self._install_event_listeners()\n212: \n213:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\n214:     # Public API\n215:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\n216: \n217:     def avatar(self) -> Optional[str]:\n218:         \"\"\"Return absolute path of current avatar (may be None).\"\"\"\n219:         return self._avatar_path\n220: \n221:     def presence(self) -> str:\n222:         \"\"\"Return the current presence key (online / busy / \u2026).\"\"\"\n223:         return self._presence\n224: \n225:     @Slot(str)\n226:     def set_avatar(self, path: str) -> None:\n227:         \"\"\"Update the avatar from the given image path.\"\"\"\n228:         path = os.path.abspath(path)\n229:         if not os.path.exists(path):\n230:             log.warning(\"Avatar path does not exist: %s\", path)\n231:             return\n232: \n233:         self._avatar_path = path\n234:         cached = _PixmapCache.get(path)\n235:         if cached is not None:\n236:             self._pixmap = cached\n237:             self.update()\n238:         else:\n239:             loader = _AvatarLoader(\n240:                 path=path,\n241:                 target_size=self.size(),\n242:                 on_loaded=self._on_async_loaded,\n243:             )\n244:             self._thread_pool.start(loader)\n245: \n246:     @Slot(str)\n247:     def set_presence(self, state: str) -> None:\n248:         \"\"\"Update presence overlay, e.g. from the event-bus.\"\"\"\n249:         if state == self._presence:\n250:             return\n251:         self._presence = state\n252:         self.update()\n253: \n254:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\n255:     # Event handling\n256:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\n257: \n258:     def paintEvent(self, event):  # noqa: D401\n259:         \"\"\"Paint circular avatar with presence badge.\"\"\"\n260:         painter = QPainter(self)\n261:         painter.setRenderHint(QPainter.Antialiasing)\n262: \n263:         if self._pixmap and not self._pixmap.isNull():\n264:             painter.setBrush(Qt.NoBrush)\n265:             path = self._rounded_rect_path()\n266:             painter.setClipPath(path)\n267:             painter.drawPixmap(self.rect(), self._pixmap)\n268:         else:\n269:             # fallback placeholder\n270:             painter.setBrush(\n271:                 self._theme.color(\"avatar.background\", \"#8F8F8F\")\n272:             )\n273:             painter.setPen(Qt.NoPen)\n274:             painter.drawEllipse(self.rect())\n275: \n276:         self._draw_presence_indicator(painter)\n277: \n278:     def mousePressEvent(self, event: QMouseEvent):  # noqa: D401\n279:         if event.button() == Qt.LeftButton:\n280:             self.clicked.emit()\n281:         super().mousePressEvent(event)\n282: \n283:     def dragEnterEvent(self, event: QDragEnterEvent):  # noqa: D401\n284:         if any(fmt in event.mimeData().formats() for fmt in self._DRAG_MIME_WHITELIST):\n285:             event.acceptProposedAction()\n286: \n287:     def dropEvent(self, event: QDropEvent):  # noqa: D401\n288:         url: QUrl\n289:         for url in event.mimeData().urls():\n290:             if url.isLocalFile():\n291:                 self._handle_new_avatar(Path(url.toLocalFile()))\n292:                 event.acceptProposedAction()\n293:                 return\n294:         QMessageBox.warning(self, \"Invalid file\", \"Please drop a local image file.\")\n295: \n296:     def contextMenuEvent(self, event: QContextMenuEvent):  # noqa: D401\n297:         menu = QMenu(self)\n298: \n299:         choose_action = QAction(\"Change avatar \u2026\", self)\n300:         choose_action.triggered.connect(self._choose_avatar)\n301:         menu.addAction(choose_action)\n302: \n303:         menu.addSeparator()\n304: \n305:         if self._avatar_path:\n306:             reveal = QAction(\"Reveal in Explorer\", self)\n307:             reveal.triggered.connect(self._reveal_in_file_manager)\n308:             menu.addAction(reveal)\n309: \n310:         menu.exec(event.globalPos())\n311: \n312:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\n313:     # Internal helpers\n314:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------------\n315: \n316:     def _install_event_listeners(self) -> None:\n317:         \"\"\"\n318:         Subscribe to application-wide events: theme change, presence updates\n319:         for our user, etc.\n320:         \"\"\"\n321:         # presence update\n322:         if isinstance(self._event_bus, EventBus):\n323:             self._event_bus.subscribe(\n324:                 \"presence.changed\",\n325:                 handler=lambda e: (\n326:                     self.set_presence(e.payload[\"state\"])\n327:                     if e.payload.get(\"user_id\") == self._user_id\n328:                     else None\n329:                 ),\n330:             )\n331: \n332:         # theme hot reload\n333:         if isinstance(self._theme, ThemeManager):\n334:             self._theme.themeChanged.connect(lambda: self.update())  # type: ignore\n335: \n336:     @Slot(QPixmap)\n337:     def _on_async_loaded(self, pixmap: QPixmap) -> None:\n338:         self._pixmap = pixmap\n339:         self.update()\n340: \n341:     def _rounded_rect_path(self):\n342:         \"\"\"Returns a QPainterPath of a circle matching widget size.\"\"\"\n343:         from PySide6.QtGui import QPainterPath\n344: \n345:         path = QPainterPath()\n346:         path.addEllipse(self.rect())\n347:         return path\n348: \n349:     def _draw_presence_indicator(self, painter: QPainter) -> None:\n350:         \"\"\"Paint a small presence dot on bottom-right corner.\"\"\"\n351:         colors = {\n352:             \"online\": \"#4CAF50\",\n353:             \"busy\": \"#F44336\",\n354:             \"away\": \"#FFB300\",\n355:             \"offline\": \"#9E9E9E\",\n356:         }\n357:         radius = max(6, self.width() // 6)\n358:         center = QPoint(self.width() - radius, self.height() - radius)\n359: \n360:         painter.setPen(Qt.NoPen)\n361:         painter.setBrush(colors.get(self._presence, \"#9E9E9E\"))\n362:         painter.drawEllipse(center, radius, radius)\n363: \n364:     # --------------------------------------------------------------\n365:     # Context-menu actions\n366:     # --------------------------------------------------------------\n367: \n368:     def _choose_avatar(self) -> None:\n369:         \"\"\"Open file-dialog for user to pick a new avatar picture.\"\"\"\n370:         filename, _ = QFileDialog.getOpenFileName(\n371:             self,\n372:             \"Select Avatar Picture\",\n373:             \"\",\n374:             \"Images (*.png *.jpg *.jpeg *.webp *.gif)\",\n375:         )\n376:         if filename:\n377:             self._handle_new_avatar(Path(filename))\n378: \n379:     def _handle_new_avatar(self, path: Path) -> None:\n380:         \"\"\"Validate and persist the chosen avatar (drag-drop or file-dialog).\"\"\"\n381:         if not path.exists():\n382:             return\n383:         if not path.suffix.lower() in {\".png\", \".jpg\", \".jpeg\", \".webp\", \".gif\"}:\n384:             QMessageBox.warning(self, \"Unsupported file\", \"File is not a supported image type.\")\n385:             return\n386: \n387:         self.set_avatar(str(path))\n388:         self.avatarChanged.emit(str(path))\n389:         # Publish to event-bus so other processes may sync the change\n390:         if isinstance(self._event_bus, EventBus):\n391:             self._event_bus.publish(  # type: ignore\n392:                 \"avatar.changed\",\n393:                 user_id=self._user_id,\n394:                 path=str(path),\n395:             )\n396: \n397:     def _reveal_in_file_manager(self) -> None:\n398:         \"\"\"Platform-specific file manager reveal of the avatar file.\"\"\"\n399:         if not self._avatar_path:\n400:             return\n401:         try:\n402:             if sys.platform.startswith(\"darwin\"):\n403:                 from subprocess import call\n404: \n405:                 call([\"open\", \"--\", self._avatar_path])\n406:             elif os.name == \"nt\":\n407:                 os.startfile(self._avatar_path)  # type: ignore  # pylint: disable=protected-access\n408:             else:  # linux, etc.\n409:                 from subprocess import call\n410: \n411:                 call([\"xdg-open\", self._avatar_path])\n412:         except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n413:             log.exception(\"Could not open file manager for %s\", self._avatar_path)\n414: ```",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021@16180b273d43410fcc114af7997ce2d2b28e392e/-/blob/flockdesk/shared/widgets/avatar_widget.py"
  }
}
17. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "tests/integration/test_profile_sync.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: ```python\\n2: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n3: tests/integration/test_profile_sync.py\\n4: \\n5: End-to-end integration tests covering the profile-synchronisation layer that keeps\\n6: user preferences in-sync between micro-front-ends and the cloud profile service.\\n7: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n8: from __future__ import annotations\\n9: \\n10: import asyncio\\n11: import contextlib\\n12: import time\\n13: from dataclasses import dataclass, field\\n14: from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Optional\\n15: \\n16: import pytest\\n17: \\n18: \\n19: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n20: #                             \u2013\u2013\u2013 Test Doubles \u2013\u2013\u2013                            #\\n21: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n22: class NetworkError(ConnectionError):\\n23:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Raised when the remote profile gateway is unreachable.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n24: \\n25: \\n26: class FakeEventBus:\\n27:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n28:     *Extremely* lightweight pub/sub bus for integration testing.\\n29: \\n30:     It behaves like the in-process event bus the real application would use,\\n31:     but is limited to fan-out broadcasts with no routing / filtering logic.\\n32:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n33: \\n34:     def __init__(self) -> None:\\n35:         self._subscribers: Dict[str, List[asyncio.Queue]] = {}\\n36: \\n37:     def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Any) -> None:\\n38:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n39:         Dispatch an event to all current subscribers.  Publishing is synchronous\\n40:         but the individual consumers receive the events asynchronously via the\\n41:         returned queue from :py:meth:`listen`.\\n42:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n43:         if topic not in self._subscribers:  # pragma: no cover\\n44:             return  # No one cares.\\n45: \\n46:         for queue in self._subscribers[topic]:\\n47:             # Use put_nowait so a slow consumer does *not* block the publisher.\\n48:             queue.put_nowait(payload)\\n49: \\n50:     def listen(self, topic: str) -> \\\"asyncio.Queue[Any]\\\":\\n51:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n52:         Subscribe to a topic and receive a dedicated queue that will carry all\\n53:         future events for that topic.\\n54:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n55:         q: \\\"asyncio.Queue[Any]\\\" = asyncio.Queue()\\n56:         self._subscribers.setdefault(topic, []).append(q)\\n57:         return q\\n58: \\n59: \\n60: class FakeRemoteGateway:\\n61:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n62:     In-memory substitute of the cloud-backed profile service.\\n63: \\n64:     It emulates three main responsibilities:\\n65:       1. Keeping the canonical *remote* profile for each user.\\n66:       2. Detecting network availability and raising :class:`NetworkError`\\n67:          accordingly.\\n68:       3. Broadcasting updates from *any* client to *all* clients subscribed for\\n69:          that user.\\n70:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n71: \\n72:     def __init__(self) -> None:\\n73:         self._remote_profiles: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}\\n74:         self._channels: Dict[str, List[asyncio.Queue]] = {}\\n75:         self.offline: bool = False\\n76: \\n77:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n78:     #                        \u2013\u2013\u2013 Public API (client) \u2013\u2013\u2013                    #\\n79:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n80:     async def push_profile(self, user_id: str, profile: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\\n81:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Persist the profile to the 'cloud' and broadcast the change.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n82:         self._ensure_online()\\n83: \\n84:         # Use a *monotonic* timestamp as a version indicator (LWW conflict\\n85:         # resolution).\\n86:         merged = dict(profile)\\n87:         merged[\\\"_last_modified\\\"] = time.monotonic()\\n88:         self._remote_profiles[user_id] = merged\\n89: \\n90:         # Fan-out to listeners (non-blocking).\\n91:         for q in self._channels.get(user_id, []):\\n92:             q.put_nowait(merged)\\n93: \\n94:     def subscribe(self, user_id: str) -> \\\"asyncio.Queue[Dict[str, Any]]\\\":\\n95:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n96:         Subscribe to server-side changes for *user_id* and receive a queue that\\n97:         is fed whenever **another** client pushes a change.\\n98:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n99:         q: \\\"asyncio.Queue[Dict[str, Any]]\\\" = asyncio.Queue()\\n100:         self._channels.setdefault(user_id, []).append(q)\\n101:         return q\\n102: \\n103:     def snapshot(self, user_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n104:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Return the server-side profile for assertions.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n105:         return self._remote_profiles.get(user_id, {}).copy()\\n106: \\n107:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\\n108:     #                           \u2013\u2013\u2013 Internals \u2013\u2013\u2013                        #\\n109:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\\n110:     def _ensure_online(self) -> None:\\n111:         if self.offline:\\n112:             raise NetworkError(\\\"Remote gateway is offline\\\")\\n113: \\n114: \\n115: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n116: #                       \u2013\u2013\u2013 System-Under-Test: Sync Service \u2013\u2013\u2013               #\\n117: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n118: @dataclass(slots=True)\\n119: class ProfileSyncService:\\n120:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n121:     Minimal, yet realistic, profile-synchronisation service.\\n122: \\n123:     A real implementation would be heavily event-driven, but for the purpose of\\n124:     integration testing we drive the important mechanics manually.\\n125:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n126: \\n127:     user_id: str\\n128:     event_bus: FakeEventBus\\n129:     gateway: FakeRemoteGateway\\n130: \\n131:     local_profile: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\\n132: \\n133:     # Internal run-state\\n134:     _unsynced_changes: bool = field(default=False, init=False)\\n135:     _running: bool = field(default=False, init=False)\\n136:     _remote_listener_task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = field(default=None, init=False)\\n137: \\n138:     # ------------------------------- Life-Cycle ----------------------------- #\\n139:     async def start(self) -> None:\\n140:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Kick-off background tasks (remote listener).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n141:         if self._running:  # pragma: no cover\\n142:             return\\n143:         self._running = True\\n144:         self._remote_listener_task = asyncio.create_task(self._listen_remote())\\n145: \\n146:     async def stop(self) -> None:\\n147:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Gracefully shut down the background listener.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n148:         if not self._running:  # pragma: no cover\\n149:             return\\n150: \\n151:         self._running = False\\n152:         if self._remote_listener_task:\\n153:             self._remote_listener_task.cancel()\\n154:             with contextlib.suppress(asyncio.CancelledError):\\n155:                 await self._remote_listener_task\\n156: \\n157:     # ---------------------------- Public Interface ------------------------- #\\n158:     def update_local(self, changes: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\\n159:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n160:         Called by the UI layer whenever the user modifies their preferences.\\n161:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n162:         self.local_profile.update(changes)\\n163:         self._unsynced_changes = True\\n164: \\n165:         # Immediately broadcast to local components.\\n166:         self.event_bus.publish(\\n167:             f\\\"profile.{self.user_id}.updated\\\", dict(self.local_profile)\\n168:         )\\n169: \\n170:         # Fire-and-forget the sync attempt.\\n171:         asyncio.create_task(self._attempt_sync())\\n172: \\n173:     async def on_network_restored(self) -> None:\\n174:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n175:         The connectivity monitor can poke this method once the network becomes\\n176:         available again, so we can flush pending writes.\\n177:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n178:         await self._attempt_sync()\\n179: \\n180:     # ------------------------------- Internals ----------------------------- #\\n181:     async def _attempt_sync(self) -> None:\\n182:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Try to push local changes to the remote gateway.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n183:         if not self._unsynced_changes:\\n184:             return\\n185: \\n186:         try:\\n187:             await self.gateway.push_profile(self.user_id, dict(self.local_profile))\\n188:             self._unsynced_changes = False\\n189:         except NetworkError:\\n190:             # Silently keep the dirty flag so we know to retry later.\\n191:             pass\\n192: \\n193:     async def _listen_remote(self) -> None:\\n194:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n195:         Background coroutine that receives server-side updates that originated\\n196:         from *other* clients.\\n197:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n198:         q = self.gateway.subscribe(self.user_id)\\n199: \\n200:         while self._running:\\n201:             remote_profile = await q.get()\\n202: \\n203:             # We only care when the incoming update carries a newer timestamp.\\n204:             local_ts = self.local_profile.get(\\\"_last_modified\\\", -1)\\n205:             remote_ts = remote_profile[\\\"_last_modified\\\"]\\n206:             if remote_ts <= local_ts:\\n207:                 continue  # Local copy is newer or identical.\\n208: \\n209:             self.local_profile = dict(remote_profile)\\n210:             self.event_bus.publish(\\n211:                 f\\\"profile.{self.user_id}.updated\\\", dict(self.local_profile)\\n212:             )\\n213: \\n214: \\n215: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n216: #                                \u2013\u2013\u2013 Helpers \u2013\u2013\u2013                              #\\n217: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n218: async def wait_for(\\n219:     queue: \\\"asyncio.Queue[Any]\\\",\\n220:     *,\\n221:     predicate: Callable[[Any], bool] | None = None,\\n222:     timeout: float = 2.0,\\n223: ) -> Any:\\n224:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n225:     Utility that awaits until `predicate(item)` is *True* for an element read\\n226:     from *queue*.  If *predicate* is :pydata:`None`, the call returns the first\\n227:     element received.\\n228:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n229:     deadline = asyncio.get_running_loop().time() + timeout\\n230:     predicate = predicate or (lambda _x: True)\\n231: \\n232:     while True:\\n233:         remaining = deadline - asyncio.get_running_loop().time()\\n234:         if remaining <= 0:\\n235:             raise TimeoutError(\\\"Timeout while waiting for queue event\\\")\\n236: \\n237:         item = await asyncio.wait_for(queue.get(), timeout=remaining)\\n238:         if predicate(item):\\n239:             return item\\n240: \\n241: \\n242: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n243: #                            \u2013\u2013\u2013 Integration Tests \u2013\u2013\u2013                        #\\n244: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\\n245: @pytest.fixture()\\n246: def event_bus() -> FakeEventBus:\\n247:     return FakeEventBus()\\n248: \\n249: \\n250: @pytest.fixture()\\n251: def remote_gateway() -> FakeRemoteGateway:\\n252:     return FakeRemoteGateway()\\n253: \\n254: \\n255: @pytest.fixture()\\n256: async def services(event_bus: FakeEventBus, remote_gateway: FakeRemoteGateway):\\n257:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n258:     Spin-up *two* sync services to simulate two independent micro-front-ends\\n259:     running in different processes.\\n260:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n261:     svc_a = ProfileSyncService(\\\"alice\\\", event_bus, remote_gateway)\\n262:     svc_b = ProfileSyncService(\\\"alice\\\", event_bus, remote_gateway)\\n263: \\n264:     await asyncio.gather(svc_a.start(), svc_b.start())\\n265:     yield svc_a, svc_b\\n266: \\n267:     await asyncio.gather(svc_a.stop(), svc_b.stop())\\n268: \\n269: \\n270: @pytest.mark.asyncio\\n271: async def test_local_update_propagates_to_remote_and_peers(\\n272:     services, event_bus: FakeEventBus, remote_gateway: FakeRemoteGateway\\n273: ):\\n274:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n275:     When one service updates the local profile, the change should:\\n276: \\n277:       1. Appear on the remote gateway.\\n278:       2. Be broadcast on the local event bus.\\n279:       3. Update *other* micro-front-ends listening for that user.\\n280:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n281:     svc_a, svc_b = services\\n282:     bus_q = event_bus.listen(\\\"profile.alice.updated\\\")\\n283: \\n284:     # Act \u2013 change a preference on service A.\\n285:     svc_a.update_local({\\\"theme\\\": \\\"dark\\\"})\\n286: \\n287:     # 1) Wait for the bus broadcast and verify the payload.\\n288:     msg = await wait_for(bus_q, predicate=lambda m: m.get(\\\"theme\\\") == \\\"dark\\\")\\n289:     assert msg[\\\"theme\\\"] == \\\"dark\\\"\\n290: \\n291:     # 2) Eventually, remote profile should be in sync.\\n292:     await asyncio.sleep(0.1)  # Allow async push to complete.\\n293:     assert remote_gateway.snapshot(\\\"alice\\\")[\\\"theme\\\"] == \\\"dark\\\"\\n294: \\n295:     #
