3) The peer (service B) must have updated its local copy.\\n296:     assert svc_b.local_profile[\\\"theme\\\"] == \\\"dark\\\"\\n297: \\n298: \\n299: @pytest.mark.asyncio\\n300: async def test_last_write_wins_conflict_resolution(\\n301:     services, remote_gateway: FakeRemoteGateway\\n302: ):\\n303:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n304:     When concurrent updates occur, the service implementing a *later* change\\n305:     (higher `_last_modified`) must win the conflict.\\n306:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n307:     svc_a, svc_b = services\\n308: \\n309:     # Simulate near-simultaneous changes.\\n310:     svc_a.update_local({\\\"language\\\": \\\"en\\\"})\\n311:     await asyncio.sleep(0.05)\\n312:     svc_b.update_local({\\\"language\\\": \\\"fr\\\"})  # <- later write wins.\\n313: \\n314:     # Give the sync loop a moment to converge.\\n315:     await asyncio.sleep(0.2)\\n316: \\n317:     snap = remote_gateway.snapshot(\\\"alice\\\")\\n318:     assert snap[\\\"language\\\"] == \\\"fr\\\"\\n319:     assert svc_a.local_profile[\\\"language\\\"] == svc_b.local_profile[\\\"language\\\"] == \\\"fr\\\"\\n320: \\n321: \\n322: @pytest.mark.asyncio\\n323: async def test_offline_changes_are_synced_when_network_recovers(\\n324:     services, remote_gateway: FakeRemoteGateway\\n325: ):\\n326:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n327:     Offline edits must be queued locally and flushed to the server once\\n328:     connectivity is restored.\\n329:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n330:     svc_a, _ = services\\n331: \\n332:     # Simulate loss of connectivity *before* the update.\\n333:     remote_gateway.offline = True\\n334:     svc_a.update_local({\\\"showAvatars\\\": False})\\n335: \\n336:     # Wait briefly to ensure the failed attempt has happened.\\n337:     await asyncio.sleep(0.1)\\n338:     assert remote_gateway.snapshot(\\\"alice\\\") == {}  # Nothing on the server.\\n339: \\n340:     # Bring the network back and trigger a retry.\\n341:     remote_gateway.offline = False\\n342:     await svc_a.on_network_restored()\\n343: \\n344:     # The push should succeed shortly after.\\n345:     await asyncio.sleep(0.1)\\n346:     assert remote_gateway.snapshot(\\\"alice\\\")[\\\"showAvatars\\\"] is False\\n347:     assert svc_a._unsynced_changes is False\\n348: ```\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021@16180b273d43410fcc114af7997ce2d2b28e392e/-/blob/tests/integration/test_profile_sync.py\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: ```python\n2: \"\"\"\n3: tests/integration/test_profile_sync.py\n4: \n5: End-to-end integration tests covering the profile-synchronisation layer that keeps\n6: user preferences in-sync between micro-front-ends and the cloud profile service.\n7: \"\"\"\n8: from __future__ import annotations\n9: \n10: import asyncio\n11: import contextlib\n12: import time\n13: from dataclasses import dataclass, field\n14: from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Optional\n15: \n16: import pytest\n17: \n18: \n19: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n20: #                             \u2013\u2013\u2013 Test Doubles \u2013\u2013\u2013                            #\n21: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n22: class NetworkError(ConnectionError):\n23:     \"\"\"Raised when the remote profile gateway is unreachable.\"\"\"\n24: \n25: \n26: class FakeEventBus:\n27:     \"\"\"\n28:     *Extremely* lightweight pub/sub bus for integration testing.\n29: \n30:     It behaves like the in-process event bus the real application would use,\n31:     but is limited to fan-out broadcasts with no routing / filtering logic.\n32:     \"\"\"\n33: \n34:     def __init__(self) -> None:\n35:         self._subscribers: Dict[str, List[asyncio.Queue]] = {}\n36: \n37:     def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Any) -> None:\n38:         \"\"\"\n39:         Dispatch an event to all current subscribers.  Publishing is synchronous\n40:         but the individual consumers receive the events asynchronously via the\n41:         returned queue from :py:meth:`listen`.\n42:         \"\"\"\n43:         if topic not in self._subscribers:  # pragma: no cover\n44:             return  # No one cares.\n45: \n46:         for queue in self._subscribers[topic]:\n47:             # Use put_nowait so a slow consumer does *not* block the publisher.\n48:             queue.put_nowait(payload)\n49: \n50:     def listen(self, topic: str) -> \"asyncio.Queue[Any]\":\n51:         \"\"\"\n52:         Subscribe to a topic and receive a dedicated queue that will carry all\n53:         future events for that topic.\n54:         \"\"\"\n55:         q: \"asyncio.Queue[Any]\" = asyncio.Queue()\n56:         self._subscribers.setdefault(topic, []).append(q)\n57:         return q\n58: \n59: \n60: class FakeRemoteGateway:\n61:     \"\"\"\n62:     In-memory substitute of the cloud-backed profile service.\n63: \n64:     It emulates three main responsibilities:\n65:       1. Keeping the canonical *remote* profile for each user.\n66:       2. Detecting network availability and raising :class:`NetworkError`\n67:          accordingly.\n68:       3. Broadcasting updates from *any* client to *all* clients subscribed for\n69:          that user.\n70:     \"\"\"\n71: \n72:     def __init__(self) -> None:\n73:         self._remote_profiles: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}\n74:         self._channels: Dict[str, List[asyncio.Queue]] = {}\n75:         self.offline: bool = False\n76: \n77:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n78:     #                        \u2013\u2013\u2013 Public API (client) \u2013\u2013\u2013                    #\n79:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n80:     async def push_profile(self, user_id: str, profile: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n81:         \"\"\"Persist the profile to the 'cloud' and broadcast the change.\"\"\"\n82:         self._ensure_online()\n83: \n84:         # Use a *monotonic* timestamp as a version indicator (LWW conflict\n85:         # resolution).\n86:         merged = dict(profile)\n87:         merged[\"_last_modified\"] = time.monotonic()\n88:         self._remote_profiles[user_id] = merged\n89: \n90:         # Fan-out to listeners (non-blocking).\n91:         for q in self._channels.get(user_id, []):\n92:             q.put_nowait(merged)\n93: \n94:     def subscribe(self, user_id: str) -> \"asyncio.Queue[Dict[str, Any]]\":\n95:         \"\"\"\n96:         Subscribe to server-side changes for *user_id* and receive a queue that\n97:         is fed whenever **another** client pushes a change.\n98:         \"\"\"\n99:         q: \"asyncio.Queue[Dict[str, Any]]\" = asyncio.Queue()\n100:         self._channels.setdefault(user_id, []).append(q)\n101:         return q\n102: \n103:     def snapshot(self, user_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n104:         \"\"\"Return the server-side profile for assertions.\"\"\"\n105:         return self._remote_profiles.get(user_id, {}).copy()\n106: \n107:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n108:     #                           \u2013\u2013\u2013 Internals \u2013\u2013\u2013                        #\n109:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n110:     def _ensure_online(self) -> None:\n111:         if self.offline:\n112:             raise NetworkError(\"Remote gateway is offline\")\n113: \n114: \n115: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n116: #                       \u2013\u2013\u2013 System-Under-Test: Sync Service \u2013\u2013\u2013               #\n117: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n118: @dataclass(slots=True)\n119: class ProfileSyncService:\n120:     \"\"\"\n121:     Minimal, yet realistic, profile-synchronisation service.\n122: \n123:     A real implementation would be heavily event-driven, but for the purpose of\n124:     integration testing we drive the important mechanics manually.\n125:     \"\"\"\n126: \n127:     user_id: str\n128:     event_bus: FakeEventBus\n129:     gateway: FakeRemoteGateway\n130: \n131:     local_profile: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\n132: \n133:     # Internal run-state\n134:     _unsynced_changes: bool = field(default=False, init=False)\n135:     _running: bool = field(default=False, init=False)\n136:     _remote_listener_task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = field(default=None, init=False)\n137: \n138:     # ------------------------------- Life-Cycle ----------------------------- #\n139:     async def start(self) -> None:\n140:         \"\"\"Kick-off background tasks (remote listener).\"\"\"\n141:         if self._running:  # pragma: no cover\n142:             return\n143:         self._running = True\n144:         self._remote_listener_task = asyncio.create_task(self._listen_remote())\n145: \n146:     async def stop(self) -> None:\n147:         \"\"\"Gracefully shut down the background listener.\"\"\"\n148:         if not self._running:  # pragma: no cover\n149:             return\n150: \n151:         self._running = False\n152:         if self._remote_listener_task:\n153:             self._remote_listener_task.cancel()\n154:             with contextlib.suppress(asyncio.CancelledError):\n155:                 await self._remote_listener_task\n156: \n157:     # ---------------------------- Public Interface ------------------------- #\n158:     def update_local(self, changes: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n159:         \"\"\"\n160:         Called by the UI layer whenever the user modifies their preferences.\n161:         \"\"\"\n162:         self.local_profile.update(changes)\n163:         self._unsynced_changes = True\n164: \n165:         # Immediately broadcast to local components.\n166:         self.event_bus.publish(\n167:             f\"profile.{self.user_id}.updated\", dict(self.local_profile)\n168:         )\n169: \n170:         # Fire-and-forget the sync attempt.\n171:         asyncio.create_task(self._attempt_sync())\n172: \n173:     async def on_network_restored(self) -> None:\n174:         \"\"\"\n175:         The connectivity monitor can poke this method once the network becomes\n176:         available again, so we can flush pending writes.\n177:         \"\"\"\n178:         await self._attempt_sync()\n179: \n180:     # ------------------------------- Internals ----------------------------- #\n181:     async def _attempt_sync(self) -> None:\n182:         \"\"\"Try to push local changes to the remote gateway.\"\"\"\n183:         if not self._unsynced_changes:\n184:             return\n185: \n186:         try:\n187:             await self.gateway.push_profile(self.user_id, dict(self.local_profile))\n188:             self._unsynced_changes = False\n189:         except NetworkError:\n190:             # Silently keep the dirty flag so we know to retry later.\n191:             pass\n192: \n193:     async def _listen_remote(self) -> None:\n194:         \"\"\"\n195:         Background coroutine that receives server-side updates that originated\n196:         from *other* clients.\n197:         \"\"\"\n198:         q = self.gateway.subscribe(self.user_id)\n199: \n200:         while self._running:\n201:             remote_profile = await q.get()\n202: \n203:             # We only care when the incoming update carries a newer timestamp.\n204:             local_ts = self.local_profile.get(\"_last_modified\", -1)\n205:             remote_ts = remote_profile[\"_last_modified\"]\n206:             if remote_ts <= local_ts:\n207:                 continue  # Local copy is newer or identical.\n208: \n209:             self.local_profile = dict(remote_profile)\n210:             self.event_bus.publish(\n211:                 f\"profile.{self.user_id}.updated\", dict(self.local_profile)\n212:             )\n213: \n214: \n215: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n216: #                                \u2013\u2013\u2013 Helpers \u2013\u2013\u2013                              #\n217: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n218: async def wait_for(\n219:     queue: \"asyncio.Queue[Any]\",\n220:     *,\n221:     predicate: Callable[[Any], bool] | None = None,\n222:     timeout: float = 2.0,\n223: ) -> Any:\n224:     \"\"\"\n225:     Utility that awaits until `predicate(item)` is *True* for an element read\n226:     from *queue*.  If *predicate* is :pydata:`None`, the call returns the first\n227:     element received.\n228:     \"\"\"\n229:     deadline = asyncio.get_running_loop().time() + timeout\n230:     predicate = predicate or (lambda _x: True)\n231: \n232:     while True:\n233:         remaining = deadline - asyncio.get_running_loop().time()\n234:         if remaining <= 0:\n235:             raise TimeoutError(\"Timeout while waiting for queue event\")\n236: \n237:         item = await asyncio.wait_for(queue.get(), timeout=remaining)\n238:         if predicate(item):\n239:             return item\n240: \n241: \n242: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n243: #                            \u2013\u2013\u2013 Integration Tests \u2013\u2013\u2013                        #\n244: # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n245: @pytest.fixture()\n246: def event_bus() -> FakeEventBus:\n247:     return FakeEventBus()\n248: \n249: \n250: @pytest.fixture()\n251: def remote_gateway() -> FakeRemoteGateway:\n252:     return FakeRemoteGateway()\n253: \n254: \n255: @pytest.fixture()\n256: async def services(event_bus: FakeEventBus, remote_gateway: FakeRemoteGateway):\n257:     \"\"\"\n258:     Spin-up *two* sync services to simulate two independent micro-front-ends\n259:     running in different processes.\n260:     \"\"\"\n261:     svc_a = ProfileSyncService(\"alice\", event_bus, remote_gateway)\n262:     svc_b = ProfileSyncService(\"alice\", event_bus, remote_gateway)\n263: \n264:     await asyncio.gather(svc_a.start(), svc_b.start())\n265:     yield svc_a, svc_b\n266: \n267:     await asyncio.gather(svc_a.stop(), svc_b.stop())\n268: \n269: \n270: @pytest.mark.asyncio\n271: async def test_local_update_propagates_to_remote_and_peers(\n272:     services, event_bus: FakeEventBus, remote_gateway: FakeRemoteGateway\n273: ):\n274:     \"\"\"\n275:     When one service updates the local profile, the change should:\n276: \n277:       1. Appear on the remote gateway.\n278:       2. Be broadcast on the local event bus.\n279:       3. Update *other* micro-front-ends listening for that user.\n280:     \"\"\"\n281:     svc_a, svc_b = services\n282:     bus_q = event_bus.listen(\"profile.alice.updated\")\n283: \n284:     # Act \u2013 change a preference on service A.\n285:     svc_a.update_local({\"theme\": \"dark\"})\n286: \n287:     # 1) Wait for the bus broadcast and verify the payload.\n288:     msg = await wait_for(bus_q, predicate=lambda m: m.get(\"theme\") == \"dark\")\n289:     assert msg[\"theme\"] == \"dark\"\n290: \n291:     # 2) Eventually, remote profile should be in sync.\n292:     await asyncio.sleep(0.1)  # Allow async push to complete.\n293:     assert remote_gateway.snapshot(\"alice\")[\"theme\"] == \"dark\"\n294: \n295:     # 3) The peer (service B) must have updated its local copy.\n296:     assert svc_b.local_profile[\"theme\"] == \"dark\"\n297: \n298: \n299: @pytest.mark.asyncio\n300: async def test_last_write_wins_conflict_resolution(\n301:     services, remote_gateway: FakeRemoteGateway\n302: ):\n303:     \"\"\"\n304:     When concurrent updates occur, the service implementing a *later* change\n305:     (higher `_last_modified`) must win the conflict.\n306:     \"\"\"\n307:     svc_a, svc_b = services\n308: \n309:     # Simulate near-simultaneous changes.\n310:     svc_a.update_local({\"language\": \"en\"})\n311:     await asyncio.sleep(0.05)\n312:     svc_b.update_local({\"language\": \"fr\"})  # <- later write wins.\n313: \n314:     # Give the sync loop a moment to converge.\n315:     await asyncio.sleep(0.2)\n316: \n317:     snap = remote_gateway.snapshot(\"alice\")\n318:     assert snap[\"language\"] == \"fr\"\n319:     assert svc_a.local_profile[\"language\"] == svc_b.local_profile[\"language\"] == \"fr\"\n320: \n321: \n322: @pytest.mark.asyncio\n323: async def test_offline_changes_are_synced_when_network_recovers(\n324:     services, remote_gateway: FakeRemoteGateway\n325: ):\n326:     \"\"\"\n327:     Offline edits must be queued locally and flushed to the server once\n328:     connectivity is restored.\n329:     \"\"\"\n330:     svc_a, _ = services\n331: \n332:     # Simulate loss of connectivity *before* the update.\n333:     remote_gateway.offline = True\n334:     svc_a.update_local({\"showAvatars\": False})\n335: \n336:     # Wait briefly to ensure the failed attempt has happened.\n337:     await asyncio.sleep(0.1)\n338:     assert remote_gateway.snapshot(\"alice\") == {}  # Nothing on the server.\n339: \n340:     # Bring the network back and trigger a retry.\n341:     remote_gateway.offline = False\n342:     await svc_a.on_network_restored()\n343: \n344:     # The push should succeed shortly after.\n345:     await asyncio.sleep(0.1)\n346:     assert remote_gateway.snapshot(\"alice\")[\"showAvatars\"] is False\n347:     assert svc_a._unsynced_changes is False\n348: ```",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021@16180b273d43410fcc114af7997ce2d2b28e392e/-/blob/tests/integration/test_profile_sync.py"
  }
}
18. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "flockdesk/modules/presence/main.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: ```python\\n2: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n3: flockdesk.modules.presence.main\\n4: ================================\\n5: \\n6: Real-time presence micro-frontend for the FlockDesk desktop suite.  The module\\n7: subscribes to user-status events coming from the internal event-bus, maintains\\n8: a reactive in-memory model and renders the data through a small Qt/PySide6 UI\\n9: implemented with an MVVM flavour.\\n10: \\n11: The file can be started stand-alone (for local development) or imported by the\\n12: FlockDesk launcher which injects the shared ``EventBus`` implementation.\\n13: \\n14: Copyright:\\n15:     (c) 2024 FlockDesk Contributors \u2013 MIT License\\n16: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n17: from __future__ import annotations\\n18: \\n19: import asyncio\\n20: import importlib\\n21: import logging\\n22: import pkg_resources\\n23: import signal\\n24: import sys\\n25: from dataclasses import dataclass, field\\n26: from datetime import datetime, timedelta\\n27: from pathlib import Path\\n28: from typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Protocol\\n29: \\n30: from PySide6.QtCore import QMetaObject, QObject, Qt, QTimer, Signal, Slot\\n31: from PySide6.QtGui import QPixmap\\n32: from PySide6.QtWidgets import (\\n33:     QApplication,\\n34:     QLabel,\\n35:     QListWidget,\\n36:     QListWidgetItem,\\n37:     QVBoxLayout,\\n38:     QWidget,\\n39: )\\n40: \\n41: LOG = logging.getLogger(\\\"flockdesk.presence\\\")\\n42: LOG.setLevel(logging.INFO)\\n43: \\n44: \\n45: ################################################################################\\n46: # Event-Bus Abstractions\\n47: ################################################################################\\n48: class EventBus(Protocol):\\n49:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Very small subset of the FlockDesk event-bus contract we care about.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n50: \\n51:     async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None: ...\\n52: \\n53:     def subscribe(\\n54:         self,\\n55:         topic: str,\\n56:         handler: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]],\\n57:         *,\\n58:         buffered: bool = False,\\n59:     ) -> None: ...\\n60: \\n61: \\n62: class _InProcessBus:\\n63:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n64:     Development fallback event-bus that works completely in-process and\\n65:     single-threaded. **Never** used in production where the concrete bus will\\n66:     be injected by the launcher (e.g., NATS, ZeroMQ, or AMQP).\\n67:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n68: \\n69:     def __init__(self) -> None:\\n70:         self._topics: Dict[str, List[Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]]]] = {}\\n71: \\n72:     async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\\n73:         LOG.debug(\\\"Publish: %s -> %s\\\", topic, payload)\\n74:         for handler in self._topics.get(topic, []):\\n75:             try:\\n76:                 await handler(payload)\\n77:             except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\\n78:                 LOG.exception(\\\"Handler error for topic %s\\\", topic)\\n79: \\n80:     def subscribe(\\n81:         self,\\n82:         topic: str,\\n83:         handler: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]],\\n84:         *,\\n85:         buffered: bool = False,\\n86:     ) -> None:\\n87:         self._topics.setdefault(topic, []).append(handler)\\n88:         LOG.debug(\\\"Subscribed %s to %s\\\", handler, topic)\\n89: \\n90: \\n91: ################################################################################\\n92: # Model\\n93: ################################################################################\\n94: @dataclass(slots=True)\\n95: class UserPresence:\\n96:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Immutable snapshot of a user's current presence.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n97: \\n98:     user_id: str\\n99:     display_name: str\\n100:     status: str  # e.g. \\\"online\\\", \\\"away\\\", \\\"dnd\\\", \\\"offline\\\"\\n101:     last_active: datetime\\n102:     avatar_url: Optional[str] = None\\n103: \\n104:     def is_online(self) -> bool:\\n105:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Return ``True`` if the user is considered online.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n106:         if self.status in {\\\"offline\\\", \\\"unknown\\\"}:\\n107:             return False\\n108:         delta = datetime.utcnow() - self.last_active\\n109:         return delta < timedelta(minutes=5)\\n110: \\n111: \\n112: ################################################################################\\n113: # View-Model\\n114: ################################################################################\\n115: class PresenceViewModel(QObject):\\n116:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n117:     Wraps the presence collection and notifies Qt when something changes.\\n118:     The VM lives on the Qt thread and receives signals forwarded from asyncio.\\n119:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n120: \\n121:     presence_changed = Signal()\\n122: \\n123:     def __init__(self) -> None:\\n124:         super().__init__()\\n125:         self._users: Dict[str, UserPresence] = {}\\n126: \\n127:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- API\\n128:     def all_users(self) -> List[UserPresence]:\\n129:         return sorted(\\n130:             self._users.values(), key=lambda u: (not u.is_online(), u.display_name)\\n131:         )\\n132: \\n133:     @Slot(dict)\\n134:     def on_presence_event(self, evt: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\\n135:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n136:         Slot invoked by the asyncio runner; marshals JSON into our dataclass and\\n137:         emits a notification to the UI controls.\\n138:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n139:         user = UserPresence(\\n140:             user_id=evt[\\\"user_id\\\"],\\n141:             display_name=evt.get(\\\"display_name\\\", evt[\\\"user_id\\\"]),\\n142:             status=evt.get(\\\"status\\\", \\\"unknown\\\"),\\n143:             last_active=datetime.fromisoformat(evt[\\\"last_active\\\"]),\\n144:             avatar_url=evt.get(\\\"avatar_url\\\"),\\n145:         )\\n146:         LOG.debug(\\\"Presence updated: %s\\\", user)\\n147:         self._users[user.user_id] = user\\n148:         self.presence_changed.emit()\\n149: \\n150:     # ----------------------------------------------------------- Diagnostics\\n151:     def __repr__(self) -> str:  # pragma: no cover\\n152:         return f\\\"<PresenceViewModel users={len(self._users)}>\\\"\\n153: \\n154: \\n155: ################################################################################\\n156: # Qt View\\n157: ################################################################################\\n158: class PresenceListWidget(QWidget):\\n159:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n160:     Very small widget that shows presence information in a vertical list.\\n161:     It is intentionally minimal \u2013 complex styling is handled by FlockDesk's\\n162:     global stylesheet that is applied by the shell.\\n163:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n164: \\n165:     def __init__(self, vm: PresenceViewModel, *, parent: Optional[QWidget] = None):\\n166:         super().__init__(parent)\\n167:         self._vm = vm\\n168:         self._setup_ui()\\n169:         vm.presence_changed.connect(self._refresh)\\n170: \\n171:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------- UI\\n172:     def _setup_ui(self) -> None:\\n173:         self.setWindowTitle(\\\"Presence\\\")\\n174:         layout = QVBoxLayout(self)\\n175:         self._list = QListWidget(self)\\n176:         self._list.setUniformItemSizes(True)\\n177:         layout.addWidget(self._list)\\n178: \\n179:     @Slot()\\n180:     def _refresh(self) -> None:\\n181:         LOG.debug(\\\"Refresh UI\\\")\\n182:         self._list.clear()\\n183:         for user in self._vm.all_users():\\n184:             item = QListWidgetItem()\\n185:             prefix = \\\"\ud83d\udfe2\\\" if user.is_online() else \\\"\u26ab\\\"\\n186:             item.setText(f\\\"{prefix}  {user.display_name}\\\")\\n187:             if user.avatar_url:\\n188:                 pixmap = QPixmap(user.avatar_url)\\n189:                 if not pixmap.isNull():\\n190:                     item.setIcon(pixmap.scaled(24, 24, Qt.KeepAspectRatio))\\n191:             self._list.addItem(item)\\n192: \\n193: \\n194: ################################################################################\\n195: # Presence Service (async)\\n196: ################################################################################\\n197: class PresenceService:\\n198:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n199:     Background asyncio component responsible for:\\n200: \\n201:         \u2022 Subscribing to the presence topic\\n202:         \u2022 Forwarding updates into the Qt world (thread-safe)\\n203:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n204: \\n205:     _TOPIC = \\\"users.presence\\\"\\n206: \\n207:     def __init__(self, bus: EventBus, vm: PresenceViewModel, qt_obj: QObject) -> None:\\n208:         self._bus = bus\\n209:         self._vm = vm\\n210:         self._qt_obj = qt_obj\\n211: \\n212:     async def start(self) -> None:\\n213:         # Subscribe for push updates coming from remote users\\n214:         self._bus.subscribe(self._TOPIC, self._handle_presence)\\n215: \\n216:         # Send an initial handshake for the local user (if desired)\\n217:         await self._publish_local_presence(\\\"online\\\")\\n218: \\n219:         # Emit a heartbeat every 30 s so others know we're still alive\\n220:         asyncio.create_task(self._heartbeat())\\n221: \\n222:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------- internals\\n223:     async def _handle_presence(self, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\\n224:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n225:         Receives JSON dictionaries from the bus and routes them into the Qt\\n226:         thread where the view-model lives.\\n227:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n228:         # Qt objects must only be touched from their own thread, therefore we\\n229:         # use `QMetaObject.invokeMethod` to schedule the slot.\\n230:         LOG.debug(\\\"Event-bus -> Qt: %s\\\", payload)\\n231:         QMetaObject.invokeMethod(\\n232:             self._qt_obj,\\n233:             lambda: self._vm.on_presence_event(payload),\\n234:             Qt.QueuedConnection,\\n235:         )\\n236: \\n237:     async def _publish_local_presence(self, status: str) -> None:\\n238:         payload = {\\n239:             \\\"user_id\\\": self._local_user(),\\n240:             \\\"display_name\\\": self._local_user(),  # demo\\n241:             \\\"status\\\": status,\\n242:             \\\"last_active\\\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\\n243:             \\\"avatar_url\\\": None,\\n244:         }\\n245:         await self._bus.publish(self._TOPIC, payload)\\n246: \\n247:     async def _heartbeat(self) -> None:\\n248:         while True:\\n249:             await asyncio.sleep(30)\\n250:             await self._publish_local_presence(\\\"online\\\")\\n251: \\n252:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------- util\\n253:     @staticmethod\\n254:     def _local_user() -> str:\\n255:         # In real life we would ask the identity service\\n256:         return Path.home().name\\n257: \\n258: \\n259: ################################################################################\\n260: # Plugin System (Providers, Rich-Presence, etc.)\\n261: ################################################################################\\n262: class PresenceProvider(Protocol):\\n263:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n264:     Extendable hook for vendors to feed additional presence information into\\n265:     the bus (think of Slack or Teams gateways).  Providers are optional Python\\n266:     packages that expose an ``flockdesk_presence_provider = mypkg:make_provider``\\n267:     entry-point.\\n268:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n269: \\n270:     async def start(self, bus: EventBus) -> None: ...\\n271: \\n272: \\n273: def _load_providers() -> List[PresenceProvider]:\\n274:     providers: List[PresenceProvider] = []\\n275:     for entry_point in pkg_resources.iter_entry_points(\\\"flockdesk_presence_provider\\\"):\\n276:         try:\\n277:             factory = entry_point.load()\\n278:             providers.append(factory())  # type: ignore[call-arg]\\n279:             LOG.info(\\\"Loaded presence provider %s\\\", entry_point.name)\\n280:         except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\\n281:             LOG.exception(\\\"Failed to load presence provider %s\\\", entry_point.name)\\n282:     return providers\\n283: \\n284: \\n285: ################################################################################\\n286: # Bootstrap\\n287: ################################################################################\\n288: async def _async_main(bus: EventBus) -> None:\\n289:     app = QApplication.instance() or QApplication(sys.argv)\\n290: \\n291:     vm = PresenceViewModel()\\n292:     widget = PresenceListWidget(vm)\\n293:     widget.resize(240, 400)\\n294:     widget.show()\\n295: \\n296:     # Async service & plugins\\n297:     service = PresenceService(bus, vm, widget)\\n298:     await service.start()\\n299: \\n300:     for prov in _load_providers():\\n301:         asyncio.create_task(prov.start(bus))\\n302: \\n303:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------- Run loop\\n304:     # Integrate Qt + asyncio.  We manually pump the event-loop every few ms so\\n305:     # we don't depend on *qasync* which would add an additional runtime dep.\\n306:     loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()\\n307:     qtimer = QTimer()\\n308:     qtimer.setInterval(5)  # ms\\n309:     qtimer.timeout.connect(lambda: None)  # NOOP, but keeps Qt awake\\n310:     qtimer.start()\\n311: \\n312:     # Handle ^C gracefully on *nix\\n313:     for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):\\n314:         loop.add_signal_handler(sig, app.quit)\\n315: \\n316:     await loop.run_in_executor(None, app.exec)\\n317: \\n318: \\n319: def main() -> None:  # pragma: no cover\\n320:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n321:     Entry-point used by the micro-frontend launcher **or** when the file is\\n322:     executed directly.\\n323:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n324:     logging.basicConfig(\\n325:         level=logging.INFO,\\n326:         format=\\\"%(asctime)s | %(levelname)-8s | %(name)s -> %(message)s\\\",\\n327:     )\\n328:     bus: EventBus = _InProcessBus()  # dev fallback\\n329:     try:\\n330:         asyncio.run(_async_main(bus))\\n331:     except KeyboardInterrupt:\\n332:         LOG.info(\\\"Shutting down.\\\")\\n333: \\n334: \\n335: if __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":  # pragma: no cover\\n336:     main()\\n337: ```\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021@16180b273d43410fcc114af7997ce2d2b28e392e/-/blob/flockdesk/modules/presence/main.py\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: ```python\n2: \"\"\"\n3: flockdesk.modules.presence.main\n4: ================================\n5: \n6: Real-time presence micro-frontend for the FlockDesk desktop suite.  The module\n7: subscribes to user-status events coming from the internal event-bus, maintains\n8: a reactive in-memory model and renders the data through a small Qt/PySide6 UI\n9: implemented with an MVVM flavour.\n10: \n11: The file can be started stand-alone (for local development) or imported by the\n12: FlockDesk launcher which injects the shared ``EventBus`` implementation.\n13: \n14: Copyright:\n15:     (c) 2024 FlockDesk Contributors \u2013 MIT License\n16: \"\"\"\n17: from __future__ import annotations\n18: \n19: import asyncio\n20: import importlib\n21: import logging\n22: import pkg_resources\n23: import signal\n24: import sys\n25: from dataclasses import dataclass, field\n26: from datetime import datetime, timedelta\n27: from pathlib import Path\n28: from typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Protocol\n29: \n30: from PySide6.QtCore import QMetaObject, QObject, Qt, QTimer, Signal, Slot\n31: from PySide6.QtGui import QPixmap\n32: from PySide6.QtWidgets import (\n33:     QApplication,\n34:     QLabel,\n35:     QListWidget,\n36:     QListWidgetItem,\n37:     QVBoxLayout,\n38:     QWidget,\n39: )\n40: \n41: LOG = logging.getLogger(\"flockdesk.presence\")\n42: LOG.setLevel(logging.INFO)\n43: \n44: \n45: ################################################################################\n46: # Event-Bus Abstractions\n47: ################################################################################\n48: class EventBus(Protocol):\n49:     \"\"\"Very small subset of the FlockDesk event-bus contract we care about.\"\"\"\n50: \n51:     async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None: ...\n52: \n53:     def subscribe(\n54:         self,\n55:         topic: str,\n56:         handler: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]],\n57:         *,\n58:         buffered: bool = False,\n59:     ) -> None: ...\n60: \n61: \n62: class _InProcessBus:\n63:     \"\"\"\n64:     Development fallback event-bus that works completely in-process and\n65:     single-threaded. **Never** used in production where the concrete bus will\n66:     be injected by the launcher (e.g., NATS, ZeroMQ, or AMQP).\n67:     \"\"\"\n68: \n69:     def __init__(self) -> None:\n70:         self._topics: Dict[str, List[Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]]]] = {}\n71: \n72:     async def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n73:         LOG.debug(\"Publish: %s -> %s\", topic, payload)\n74:         for handler in self._topics.get(topic, []):\n75:             try:\n76:                 await handler(payload)\n77:             except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n78:                 LOG.exception(\"Handler error for topic %s\", topic)\n79: \n80:     def subscribe(\n81:         self,\n82:         topic: str,\n83:         handler: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Awaitable[None]],\n84:         *,\n85:         buffered: bool = False,\n86:     ) -> None:\n87:         self._topics.setdefault(topic, []).append(handler)\n88:         LOG.debug(\"Subscribed %s to %s\", handler, topic)\n89: \n90: \n91: ################################################################################\n92: # Model\n93: ################################################################################\n94: @dataclass(slots=True)\n95: class UserPresence:\n96:     \"\"\"Immutable snapshot of a user's current presence.\"\"\"\n97: \n98:     user_id: str\n99:     display_name: str\n100:     status: str  # e.g. \"online\", \"away\", \"dnd\", \"offline\"\n101:     last_active: datetime\n102:     avatar_url: Optional[str] = None\n103: \n104:     def is_online(self) -> bool:\n105:         \"\"\"Return ``True`` if the user is considered online.\"\"\"\n106:         if self.status in {\"offline\", \"unknown\"}:\n107:             return False\n108:         delta = datetime.utcnow() - self.last_active\n109:         return delta < timedelta(minutes=5)\n110: \n111: \n112: ################################################################################\n113: # View-Model\n114: ################################################################################\n115: class PresenceViewModel(QObject):\n116:     \"\"\"\n117:     Wraps the presence collection and notifies Qt when something changes.\n118:     The VM lives on the Qt thread and receives signals forwarded from asyncio.\n119:     \"\"\"\n120: \n121:     presence_changed = Signal()\n122: \n123:     def __init__(self) -> None:\n124:         super().__init__()\n125:         self._users: Dict[str, UserPresence] = {}\n126: \n127:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- API\n128:     def all_users(self) -> List[UserPresence]:\n129:         return sorted(\n130:             self._users.values(), key=lambda u: (not u.is_online(), u.display_name)\n131:         )\n132: \n133:     @Slot(dict)\n134:     def on_presence_event(self, evt: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n135:         \"\"\"\n136:         Slot invoked by the asyncio runner; marshals JSON into our dataclass and\n137:         emits a notification to the UI controls.\n138:         \"\"\"\n139:         user = UserPresence(\n140:             user_id=evt[\"user_id\"],\n141:             display_name=evt.get(\"display_name\", evt[\"user_id\"]),\n142:             status=evt.get(\"status\", \"unknown\"),\n143:             last_active=datetime.fromisoformat(evt[\"last_active\"]),\n144:             avatar_url=evt.get(\"avatar_url\"),\n145:         )\n146:         LOG.debug(\"Presence updated: %s\", user)\n147:         self._users[user.user_id] = user\n148:         self.presence_changed.emit()\n149: \n150:     # ----------------------------------------------------------- Diagnostics\n151:     def __repr__(self) -> str:  # pragma: no cover\n152:         return f\"<PresenceViewModel users={len(self._users)}>\"\n153: \n154: \n155: ################################################################################\n156: # Qt View\n157: ################################################################################\n158: class PresenceListWidget(QWidget):\n159:     \"\"\"\n160:     Very small widget that shows presence information in a vertical list.\n161:     It is intentionally minimal \u2013 complex styling is handled by FlockDesk's\n162:     global stylesheet that is applied by the shell.\n163:     \"\"\"\n164: \n165:     def __init__(self, vm: PresenceViewModel, *, parent: Optional[QWidget] = None):\n166:         super().__init__(parent)\n167:         self._vm = vm\n168:         self._setup_ui()\n169:         vm.presence_changed.connect(self._refresh)\n170: \n171:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------- UI\n172:     def _setup_ui(self) -> None:\n173:         self.setWindowTitle(\"Presence\")\n174:         layout = QVBoxLayout(self)\n175:         self._list = QListWidget(self)\n176:         self._list.setUniformItemSizes(True)\n177:         layout.addWidget(self._list)\n178: \n179:     @Slot()\n180:     def _refresh(self) -> None:\n181:         LOG.debug(\"Refresh UI\")\n182:         self._list.clear()\n183:         for user in self._vm.all_users():\n184:             item = QListWidgetItem()\n185:             prefix = \"\ud83d\udfe2\" if user.is_online() else \"\u26ab\"\n186:             item.setText(f\"{prefix}  {user.display_name}\")\n187:             if user.avatar_url:\n188:                 pixmap = QPixmap(user.avatar_url)\n189:                 if not pixmap.isNull():\n190:                     item.setIcon(pixmap.scaled(24, 24, Qt.KeepAspectRatio))\n191:             self._list.addItem(item)\n192: \n193: \n194: ################################################################################\n195: # Presence Service (async)\n196: ################################################################################\n197: class PresenceService:\n198:     \"\"\"\n199:     Background asyncio component responsible for:\n200: \n201:         \u2022 Subscribing to the presence topic\n202:         \u2022 Forwarding updates into the Qt world (thread-safe)\n203:     \"\"\"\n204: \n205:     _TOPIC = \"users.presence\"\n206: \n207:     def __init__(self, bus: EventBus, vm: PresenceViewModel, qt_obj: QObject) -> None:\n208:         self._bus = bus\n209:         self._vm = vm\n210:         self._qt_obj = qt_obj\n211: \n212:     async def start(self) -> None:\n213:         # Subscribe for push updates coming from remote users\n214:         self._bus.subscribe(self._TOPIC, self._handle_presence)\n215: \n216:         # Send an initial handshake for the local user (if desired)\n217:         await self._publish_local_presence(\"online\")\n218: \n219:         # Emit a heartbeat every 30 s so others know we're still alive\n220:         asyncio.create_task(self._heartbeat())\n221: \n222:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------- internals\n223:     async def _handle_presence(self, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n224:         \"\"\"\n225:         Receives JSON dictionaries from the bus and routes them into the Qt\n226:         thread where the view-model lives.\n227:         \"\"\"\n228:         # Qt objects must only be touched from their own thread, therefore we\n229:         # use `QMetaObject.invokeMethod` to schedule the slot.\n230:         LOG.debug(\"Event-bus -> Qt: %s\", payload)\n231:         QMetaObject.invokeMethod(\n232:             self._qt_obj,\n233:             lambda: self._vm.on_presence_event(payload),\n234:             Qt.QueuedConnection,\n235:         )\n236: \n237:     async def _publish_local_presence(self, status: str) -> None:\n238:         payload = {\n239:             \"user_id\": self._local_user(),\n240:             \"display_name\": self._local_user(),  # demo\n241:             \"status\": status,\n242:             \"last_active\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n243:             \"avatar_url\": None,\n244:         }\n245:         await self._bus.publish(self._TOPIC, payload)\n246: \n247:     async def _heartbeat(self) -> None:\n248:         while True:\n249:             await asyncio.sleep(30)\n250:             await self._publish_local_presence(\"online\")\n251: \n252:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------- util\n253:     @staticmethod\n254:     def _local_user() -> str:\n255:         # In real life we would ask the identity service\n256:         return Path.home().name\n257: \n258: \n259: ################################################################################\n260: # Plugin System (Providers, Rich-Presence, etc.)\n261: ################################################################################\n262: class PresenceProvider(Protocol):\n263:     \"\"\"\n264:     Extendable hook for vendors to feed additional presence information into\n265:     the bus (think of Slack or Teams gateways).  Providers are optional Python\n266:     packages that expose an ``flockdesk_presence_provider = mypkg:make_provider``\n267:     entry-point.\n268:     \"\"\"\n269: \n270:     async def start(self, bus: EventBus) -> None: ...\n271: \n272: \n273: def _load_providers() -> List[PresenceProvider]:\n274:     providers: List[PresenceProvider] = []\n275:     for entry_point in pkg_resources.iter_entry_points(\"flockdesk_presence_provider\"):\n276:         try:\n277:             factory = entry_point.load()\n278:             providers.append(factory())  # type: ignore[call-arg]\n279:             LOG.info(\"Loaded presence provider %s\", entry_point.name)\n280:         except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n281:             LOG.exception(\"Failed to load presence provider %s\", entry_point.name)\n282:     return providers\n283: \n284: \n285: ################################################################################\n286: # Bootstrap\n287: ################################################################################\n288: async def _async_main(bus: EventBus) -> None:\n289:     app = QApplication.instance() or QApplication(sys.argv)\n290: \n291:     vm = PresenceViewModel()\n292:     widget = PresenceListWidget(vm)\n293:     widget.resize(240, 400)\n294:     widget.show()\n295: \n296:     # Async service & plugins\n297:     service = PresenceService(bus, vm, widget)\n298:     await service.start()\n299: \n300:     for prov in _load_providers():\n301:         asyncio.create_task(prov.start(bus))\n302: \n303:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------- Run loop\n304:     # Integrate Qt + asyncio.  We manually pump the event-loop every few ms so\n305:     # we don't depend on *qasync* which would add an additional runtime dep.\n306:     loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()\n307:     qtimer = QTimer()\n308:     qtimer.setInterval(5)  # ms\n309:     qtimer.timeout.connect(lambda: None)  # NOOP, but keeps Qt awake\n310:     qtimer.start()\n311: \n312:     # Handle ^C gracefully on *nix\n313:     for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):\n314:         loop.add_signal_handler(sig, app.quit)\n315: \n316:     await loop.run_in_executor(None, app.exec)\n317: \n318: \n319: def main() -> None:  # pragma: no cover\n320:     \"\"\"\n321:     Entry-point used by the micro-frontend launcher **or** when the file is\n322:     executed directly.\n323:     \"\"\"\n324:     logging.basicConfig(\n325:         level=logging.INFO,\n326:         format=\"%(asctime)s | %(levelname)-8s | %(name)s -> %(message)s\",\n327:     )\n328:     bus: EventBus = _InProcessBus()  # dev fallback\n329:     try:\n330:         asyncio.run(_async_main(bus))\n331:     except KeyboardInterrupt:\n332:         LOG.info(\"Shutting down.\")\n333: \n334: \n335: if __name__ == \"__main__\":  # pragma: no cover\n336:     main()\n337: ```",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021@16180b273d43410fcc114af7997ce2d2b28e392e/-/blob/flockdesk/modules/presence/main.py"
  }
}
19. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "flockdesk/shared/schemas/user_profile.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: ```python\\n2: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n3: flockdesk.shared.schemas.user_profile\\n4: ====================================\\n5: \\n6: Strongly-typed, versioned schema for persisting a user\u2019s profile in FlockDesk.\\n7: The schema is expressed as nested Pydantic models to guarantee integrity,\\n8: provide automatic (de)serialization, and enable painless migrations when the\\n9: structure evolves.\\n10: \\n11: The profile encapsulates:\\n12:     \u2022 Identity   \u2013 id, e-mail address, display name, avatar path\\n13:     \u2022 Preferences \u2013 theme, shortcuts, window layout, enabled plug-ins\\n14:     \u2022 Timestamps  \u2013 creation, last update, schema revision\\n15: \\n16: The helper methods `save()` and `load()` make it trivial to persist and restore\\n17: profiles as UTF-8 JSON files.  When older documents are encountered, an\\n18: internal migration pipeline `_migrate()` upgrades them on-the-fly so the rest\\n19: of the application can rely on the latest shape.\\n20: \\n21: This module has *no* external runtime dependencies outside of `pydantic`,\\n22: `uuid`, and the Python standard library.\\n23: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n24: \\n25: from __future__ import annotations\\n26: \\n27: import json\\n28: from datetime import datetime\\n29: from pathlib import Path\\n30: from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional\\n31: from uuid import UUID, uuid4\\n32: \\n33: from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, ValidationError, root_validator, validator\\n34: \\n35: __all__ = [\\n36:     \\\"ThemeSettings\\\",\\n37:     \\\"ShortcutBinding\\\",\\n38:     \\\"KeyboardPreferences\\\",\\n39:     \\\"WindowGeometry\\\",\\n40:     \\\"WindowLayout\\\",\\n41:     \\\"PluginConfig\\\",\\n42:     \\\"UserPreferences\\\",\\n43:     \\\"UserProfile\\\",\\n44: ]\\n45: \\n46: ###############################################################################\\n47: # Low-level building blocks\\n48: ###############################################################################\\n49: \\n50: \\n51: class ThemeSettings(BaseModel):\\n52:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n53:     Visual styling preferences.\\n54: \\n55:     Attributes\\n56:     ----------\\n57:     name\\n58:         Human-readable name of the theme.\\n59:     accent_color\\n60:         Hex triplet to use for accent highlights (validated).\\n61:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n62: \\n63:     name: str = Field(\\\"FlockDark\\\", description=\\\"Name of the currently used theme.\\\")\\n64:     accent_color: str = Field(\\n65:         \\\"#1abc9c\\\",\\n66:         regex=r\\\"^#(?:[0-9a-fA-F]{3}){1,2}$\\\",\\n67:         description=\\\"HEX RGB color (e.g. #RRGGBB) used for accent highlights.\\\",\\n68:     )\\n69: \\n70: \\n71: class ShortcutBinding(BaseModel):\\n72:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n73:     Represents one keyboard shortcut mapping.\\n74: \\n75:     The `keys` field accepts multi-stroke definitions, e.g. `['Ctrl+Shift+P']`.\\n76:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n77: \\n78:     action: str = Field(..., description=\\\"Canonical action identifier.\\\")\\n79:     keys: List[str] = Field(\\n80:         ...,\\n81:         min_items=1,\\n82:         description=\\\"Key sequence(s) that trigger the action.\\\",\\n83:     )\\n84: \\n85: \\n86: class KeyboardPreferences(BaseModel):\\n87:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Collection of user-defined keyboard shortcuts.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n88: \\n89:     bindings: List[ShortcutBinding] = Field(\\n90:         default_factory=list, description=\\\"All custom keyboard bindings.\\\"\\n91:     )\\n92: \\n93:     def get_binding_for_action(self, action: str) -> Optional[ShortcutBinding]:\\n94:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n95:         Return the shortcut binding for a given action, if present.\\n96:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n97:         return next((b for b in self.bindings if b.action == action), None)\\n98: \\n99: \\n100: class WindowGeometry(BaseModel):\\n101:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n102:     Geometry description for a single window or dock widget.\\n103:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n104: \\n105:     x: int\\n106:     y: int\\n107:     width: int\\n108:     height: int\\n109:     is_maximized: bool = Field(\\n110:         False, description=\\\"Whether the window was maximised when saved.\\\"\\n111:     )\\n112: \\n113:     @validator(\\\"*\\\", pre=True)\\n114:     def _ensure_int(cls, v: Any) -> int:\\n115:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n116:         Pydantic sometimes passes `None` for missing fields, we make sure the\\n117:         geometry is always an integer \u2265 0 to avoid Qt warnings later.\\n118:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n119:         if not isinstance(v, int) or v < 0:\\n120:             raise ValueError(\\\"Window geometry values must be non-negative integers.\\\")\\n121:         return v\\n122: \\n123: \\n124: class WindowLayout(BaseModel):\\n125:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n126:     Persisted layout for main and dock windows.\\n127:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n128: \\n129:     main_window: WindowGeometry\\n130:     dock_widgets: Dict[str, WindowGeometry] = Field(\\n131:         default_factory=dict,\\n132:         description=\\\"Mapping dock-widget-id -> geometry.\\\",\\n133:     )\\n134: \\n135: \\n136: class PluginConfig(BaseModel):\\n137:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n138:     Per-plug-in runtime state (enabled, custom settings, \u2026).\\n139:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n140: \\n141:     id: str = Field(..., description=\\\"Fully-qualified plug-in identifier.\\\")\\n142:     enabled: bool = Field(True, description=\\\"Is the plug-in enabled?\\\")\\n143:     state: Dict[str, Any] = Field(\\n144:         default_factory=dict,\\n145:         description=\\\"Opaque blob saved/restored by the plug-in itself.\\\",\\n146:     )\\n147: \\n148: \\n149: ###############################################################################\\n150: # High-level aggregated preferences\\n151: ###############################################################################\\n152: \\n153: \\n154: class UserPreferences(BaseModel):\\n155:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n156:     Root container for everything customisable by the user.\\n157:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n158: \\n159:     theme: ThemeSettings = Field(default_factory=ThemeSettings)\\n160:     keyboard: KeyboardPreferences = Field(default_factory=KeyboardPreferences)\\n161:     layout: WindowLayout\\n162:     plugins: List[PluginConfig] = Field(default_factory=list)\\n163:     last_updated: datetime = Field(\\n164:         default_factory=datetime.utcnow,\\n165:         description=\\\"UTC timestamp when preferences changed last.\\\",\\n166:     )\\n167: \\n168:     # Convenience helpers -----------------------------------------------------\\n169: \\n170:     def plugin(self, plugin_id: str) -> Optional[PluginConfig]:\\n171:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n172:         Fetch a plug-in configuration by id.\\n173:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n174:         return next((p for p in self.plugins if p.id == plugin_id), None)\\n175: \\n176:     def enable_plugin(self, plugin_id: str) -> None:\\n177:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n178:         Enable (or add) a plug-in.\\n179:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n180:         cfg = self.plugin(plugin_id)\\n181:         if cfg:\\n182:             cfg.enabled = True\\n183:         else:\\n184:             self.plugins.append(PluginConfig(id=plugin_id, enabled=True))\\n185: \\n186:     def disable_plugin(self, plugin_id: str) -> None:\\n187:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n188:         Disable a plug-in if it exists.\\n189:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n190:         cfg = self.plugin(plugin_id)\\n191:         if cfg:\\n192:             cfg.enabled = False\\n193: \\n194: \\n195: ###############################################################################\\n196: # Top-level profile\\n197: ###############################################################################\\n198: \\n199: \\n200: class UserProfile(BaseModel):\\n201:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n202:     Complete persisted user profile.\\n203: \\n204:     The model is intentionally *not* immutable \u2013 live components mutate it, and\\n205:     we rely on Pydantic\u2019s `validate_assignment` to keep things consistent.\\n206:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n207: \\n208:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- Meta\\n209:     schema_version: int = Field(\\n210:         1,\\n211:         const=True,\\n212:         description=\\\"Monotonically increasing schema revision.\\\",\\n213:     )\\n214: \\n215:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------- Identity\\n216:     user_id: UUID = Field(default_factory=uuid4)\\n217:     email: str\\n218:     display_name: str\\n219:     avatar_path: Optional[str] = Field(\\n220:         None,\\n221:         description=\\\"File path or HTTPS URL to the avatar image.\\\",\\n222:     )\\n223: \\n224:     # -------------------------------------------------------------- Preferences\\n225:     preferences: UserPreferences = Field(\\n226:         default_factory=lambda: UserPreferences(\\n227:             layout=WindowLayout(\\n228:                 main_window=WindowGeometry(\\n229:                     x=50,\\n230:                     y=50,\\n231:                     width=1280,\\n232:                     height=800,\\n233:                     is_maximized=False,\\n234:                 )\\n235:             )\\n236:         )\\n237:     )\\n238: \\n239:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------- Audit\\n240:     created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)\\n241:     updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)\\n242: \\n243:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ Config\\n244:     class Config:\\n245:         validate_assignment = True\\n246:         json_encoders = {datetime: lambda dt: dt.replace(tzinfo=None).isoformat()}\\n247: \\n248:     # ----------------------------------------------------------------- Hooks\\n249:     @validator(\\\"email\\\")\\n250:     def _email_must_be_valid(cls, v: str) -> str:\\n251:         if \\\"@\\\" not in v:\\n252:             raise ValueError(\\\"Invalid e-mail address.\\\")\\n253:         return v.strip().lower()\\n254: \\n255:     @root_validator\\n256:     def _touch_updated_at(cls, values: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n257:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n258:         Whenever the model is (re-)validated we update the timestamp so callers\\n259:         don\u2019t have to remember doing it themselves.\\n260:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n261:         values[\\\"updated_at\\\"] = datetime.utcnow()\\n262:         return values\\n263: \\n264:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------- Public\\n265:     # Persistence helpers .....................................................\\n266: \\n267:     def save(self, path: str | Path) -> None:\\n268:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n269:         Flush the profile to *path* as pretty-printed UTF-8 JSON.\\n270:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n271:         path = Path(path)\\n272:         try:\\n273:             path.write_text(self.json(indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n274:         except OSError as exc:  # pragma: no cover\\n275:             raise IOError(f\\\"Unable to write user profile to {path}: {exc}\\\") from exc\\n276: \\n277:     @classmethod\\n278:     def load(cls, path: str | Path) -> \\\"UserProfile\\\":\\n279:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n280:         Load a profile from *path*, migrating older versions automatically.\\n281:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n282:         path = Path(path)\\n283:         if not path.exists():\\n284:             raise FileNotFoundError(f\\\"User profile not found: {path}\\\")\\n285: \\n286:         raw_json = path.read_text(encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n287:         data = json.loads(raw_json)\\n288: \\n289:         current_version = cls.__fields__[\\\"schema_version\\\"].default\\n290:         stored_version = data.get(\\\"schema_version\\\", 0)\\n291:         if stored_version != current_version:\\n292:             data = cls._migrate(data, from_version=stored_version)\\n293: \\n294:         try:\\n295:             return cls.parse_obj(data)\\n296:         except ValidationError as exc:  # pragma: no cover\\n297:             raise ValueError(\\n298:                 f\\\"Corrupted profile '{path}':\\\\n{exc}\\\"\\n299:             ) from exc\\n300: \\n301:     # Migration pipeline ......................................................\\n302: \\n303:     @staticmethod\\n304:     def _migrate(payload: Dict[str, Any], *, from_version: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n305:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n306:         Upgrade *payload* to the latest schema version.\\n307: \\n308:         Every `if` block is responsible for migrating *from* a specific older\\n309:         revision *to* the immediate next one.\\n310:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n311:         migrated = dict(payload)  # shallow copy\\n312: \\n313:         if from_version < 1:\\n314:             # \u2013\u2013\u2013 v0 \u279c v1 \u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\\n315:             #  \u2022 Ensure `display_name` exists (fallback to local-part of e-mail)\\n316:             #\\n317:             migrated.setdefault(\\n318:                 \\\"display_name\\\",\\n319:                 migrated.get(\\\"email\\\", \\\"User\\\").split(\\\"@\\\")[0].title(),\\n320:             )\\n321:             migrated[\\\"schema_version\\\"] = 1\\n322: \\n323:         return migrated\\n324: \\n325:     # Convenience API .........................................................\\n326: \\n327:     def update_preferences(self, **changes: Any) -> None:\\n328:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n329:         Patch top-level keys of :class:`UserPreferences`.\\n330: \\n331:         Examples\\n332:         --------\\n333:         >>> user.update_preferences(theme=ThemeSettings(name=\\\"Solarized\\\"))\\n334:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n335:         for key, value in changes.items():\\n336:             if not hasattr(self.preferences, key):\\n337:                 raise AttributeError(f\\\"Unknown preference section: {key!r}\\\")\\n338:             setattr(self.preferences, key, value)\\n339: \\n340:         # Re-validate to bump `updated_at`\\n341:         self.__dict__[\\\"updated_at\\\"] = datetime.utcnow()\\n342:         self.preferences.last_updated = self.updated_at\\n343: ```\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021@16180b273d43410fcc114af7997ce2d2b28e392e/-/blob/flockdesk/shared/schemas/user_profile.py\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: ```python\n2: \"\"\"\n3: flockdesk.shared.schemas.user_profile\n4: ====================================\n5: \n6: Strongly-typed, versioned schema for persisting a user\u2019s profile in FlockDesk.\n7: The schema is expressed as nested Pydantic models to guarantee integrity,\n8: provide automatic (de)serialization, and enable painless migrations when the\n9: structure evolves.\n10: \n11: The profile encapsulates:\n12:     \u2022 Identity   \u2013 id, e-mail address, display name, avatar path\n13:     \u2022 Preferences \u2013 theme, shortcuts, window layout, enabled plug-ins\n14:     \u2022 Timestamps  \u2013 creation, last update, schema revision\n15: \n16: The helper methods `save()` and `load()` make it trivial to persist and restore\n17: profiles as UTF-8 JSON files.  When older documents are encountered, an\n18: internal migration pipeline `_migrate()` upgrades them on-the-fly so the rest\n19: of the application can rely on the latest shape.\n20: \n21: This module has *no* external runtime dependencies outside of `pydantic`,\n22: `uuid`, and the Python standard library.\n23: \"\"\"\n24: \n25: from __future__ import annotations\n26: \n27: import json\n28: from datetime import datetime\n29: from pathlib import Path\n30: from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional\n31: from uuid import UUID, uuid4\n32: \n33: from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, ValidationError, root_validator, validator\n34: \n35: __all__ = [\n36:     \"ThemeSettings\",\n37:     \"ShortcutBinding\",\n38:     \"KeyboardPreferences\",\n39:     \"WindowGeometry\",\n40:     \"WindowLayout\",\n41:     \"PluginConfig\",\n42:     \"UserPreferences\",\n43:     \"UserProfile\",\n44: ]\n45: \n46: ###############################################################################\n47: # Low-level building blocks\n48: ###############################################################################\n49: \n50: \n51: class ThemeSettings(BaseModel):\n52:     \"\"\"\n53:     Visual styling preferences.\n54: \n55:     Attributes\n56:     ----------\n57:     name\n58:         Human-readable name of the theme.\n59:     accent_color\n60:         Hex triplet to use for accent highlights (validated).\n61:     \"\"\"\n62: \n63:     name: str = Field(\"FlockDark\", description=\"Name of the currently used theme.\")\n64:     accent_color: str = Field(\n65:         \"#1abc9c\",\n66:         regex=r\"^#(?:[0-9a-fA-F]{3}){1,2}$\",\n67:         description=\"HEX RGB color (e.g. #RRGGBB) used for accent highlights.\",\n68:     )\n69: \n70: \n71: class ShortcutBinding(BaseModel):\n72:     \"\"\"\n73:     Represents one keyboard shortcut mapping.\n74: \n75:     The `keys` field accepts multi-stroke definitions, e.g. `['Ctrl+Shift+P']`.\n76:     \"\"\"\n77: \n78:     action: str = Field(..., description=\"Canonical action identifier.\")\n79:     keys: List[str] = Field(\n80:         ...,\n81:         min_items=1,\n82:         description=\"Key sequence(s) that trigger the action.\",\n83:     )\n84: \n85: \n86: class KeyboardPreferences(BaseModel):\n87:     \"\"\"Collection of user-defined keyboard shortcuts.\"\"\"\n88: \n89:     bindings: List[ShortcutBinding] = Field(\n90:         default_factory=list, description=\"All custom keyboard bindings.\"\n91:     )\n92: \n93:     def get_binding_for_action(self, action: str) -> Optional[ShortcutBinding]:\n94:         \"\"\"\n95:         Return the shortcut binding for a given action, if present.\n96:         \"\"\"\n97:         return next((b for b in self.bindings if b.action == action), None)\n98: \n99: \n100: class WindowGeometry(BaseModel):\n101:     \"\"\"\n102:     Geometry description for a single window or dock widget.\n103:     \"\"\"\n104: \n105:     x: int\n106:     y: int\n107:     width: int\n108:     height: int\n109:     is_maximized: bool = Field(\n110:         False, description=\"Whether the window was maximised when saved.\"\n111:     )\n112: \n113:     @validator(\"*\", pre=True)\n114:     def _ensure_int(cls, v: Any) -> int:\n115:         \"\"\"\n116:         Pydantic sometimes passes `None` for missing fields, we make sure the\n117:         geometry is always an integer \u2265 0 to avoid Qt warnings later.\n118:         \"\"\"\n119:         if not isinstance(v, int) or v < 0:\n120:             raise ValueError(\"Window geometry values must be non-negative integers.\")\n121:         return v\n122: \n123: \n124: class WindowLayout(BaseModel):\n125:     \"\"\"\n126:     Persisted layout for main and dock windows.\n127:     \"\"\"\n128: \n129:     main_window: WindowGeometry\n130:     dock_widgets: Dict[str, WindowGeometry] = Field(\n131:         default_factory=dict,\n132:         description=\"Mapping dock-widget-id -> geometry.\",\n133:     )\n134: \n135: \n136: class PluginConfig(BaseModel):\n137:     \"\"\"\n138:     Per-plug-in runtime state (enabled, custom settings, \u2026).\n139:     \"\"\"\n140: \n141:     id: str = Field(..., description=\"Fully-qualified plug-in identifier.\")\n142:     enabled: bool = Field(True, description=\"Is the plug-in enabled?\")\n143:     state: Dict[str, Any] = Field(\n144:         default_factory=dict,\n145:         description=\"Opaque blob saved/restored by the plug-in itself.\",\n146:     )\n147: \n148: \n149: ###############################################################################\n150: # High-level aggregated preferences\n151: ###############################################################################\n152: \n153: \n154: class UserPreferences(BaseModel):\n155:     \"\"\"\n156:     Root container for everything customisable by the user.\n157:     \"\"\"\n158: \n159:     theme: ThemeSettings = Field(default_factory=ThemeSettings)\n160:     keyboard: KeyboardPreferences = Field(default_factory=KeyboardPreferences)\n161:     layout: WindowLayout\n162:     plugins: List[PluginConfig] = Field(default_factory=list)\n163:     last_updated: datetime = Field(\n164:         default_factory=datetime.utcnow,\n165:         description=\"UTC timestamp when preferences changed last.\",\n166:     )\n167: \n168:     # Convenience helpers -----------------------------------------------------\n169: \n170:     def plugin(self, plugin_id: str) -> Optional[PluginConfig]:\n171:         \"\"\"\n172:         Fetch a plug-in configuration by id.\n173:         \"\"\"\n174:         return next((p for p in self.plugins if p.id == plugin_id), None)\n175: \n176:     def enable_plugin(self, plugin_id: str) -> None:\n177:         \"\"\"\n178:         Enable (or add) a plug-in.\n179:         \"\"\"\n180:         cfg = self.plugin(plugin_id)\n181:         if cfg:\n182:             cfg.enabled = True\n183:         else:\n184:             self.plugins.append(PluginConfig(id=plugin_id, enabled=True))\n185: \n186:     def disable_plugin(self, plugin_id: str) -> None:\n187:         \"\"\"\n188:         Disable a plug-in if it exists.\n189:         \"\"\"\n190:         cfg = self.plugin(plugin_id)\n191:         if cfg:\n192:             cfg.enabled = False\n193: \n194: \n195: ###############################################################################\n196: # Top-level profile\n197: ###############################################################################\n198: \n199: \n200: class UserProfile(BaseModel):\n201:     \"\"\"\n202:     Complete persisted user profile.\n203: \n204:     The model is intentionally *not* immutable \u2013 live components mutate it, and\n205:     we rely on Pydantic\u2019s `validate_assignment` to keep things consistent.\n206:     \"\"\"\n207: \n208:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------- Meta\n209:     schema_version: int = Field(\n210:         1,\n211:         const=True,\n212:         description=\"Monotonically increasing schema revision.\",\n213:     )\n214: \n215:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------- Identity\n216:     user_id: UUID = Field(default_factory=uuid4)\n217:     email: str\n218:     display_name: str\n219:     avatar_path: Optional[str] = Field(\n220:         None,\n221:         description=\"File path or HTTPS URL to the avatar image.\",\n222:     )\n223: \n224:     # -------------------------------------------------------------- Preferences\n225:     preferences: UserPreferences = Field(\n226:         default_factory=lambda: UserPreferences(\n227:             layout=WindowLayout(\n228:                 main_window=WindowGeometry(\n229:                     x=50,\n230:                     y=50,\n231:                     width=1280,\n232:                     height=800,\n233:                     is_maximized=False,\n234:                 )\n235:             )\n236:         )\n237:     )\n238: \n239:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------- Audit\n240:     created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)\n241:     updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)\n242: \n243:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------ Config\n244:     class Config:\n245:         validate_assignment = True\n246:         json_encoders = {datetime: lambda dt: dt.replace(tzinfo=None).isoformat()}\n247: \n248:     # ----------------------------------------------------------------- Hooks\n249:     @validator(\"email\")\n250:     def _email_must_be_valid(cls, v: str) -> str:\n251:         if \"@\" not in v:\n252:             raise ValueError(\"Invalid e-mail address.\")\n253:         return v.strip().lower()\n254: \n255:     @root_validator\n256:     def _touch_updated_at(cls, values: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n257:         \"\"\"\n258:         Whenever the model is (re-)validated we update the timestamp so callers\n259:         don\u2019t have to remember doing it themselves.\n260:         \"\"\"\n261:         values[\"updated_at\"] = datetime.utcnow()\n262:         return values\n263: \n264:     # ---------------------------------------------------------------- Public\n265:     # Persistence helpers .....................................................\n266: \n267:     def save(self, path: str | Path) -> None:\n268:         \"\"\"\n269:         Flush the profile to *path* as pretty-printed UTF-8 JSON.\n270:         \"\"\"\n271:         path = Path(path)\n272:         try:\n273:             path.write_text(self.json(indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n274:         except OSError as exc:  # pragma: no cover\n275:             raise IOError(f\"Unable to write user profile to {path}: {exc}\") from exc\n276: \n277:     @classmethod\n278:     def load(cls, path: str | Path) -> \"UserProfile\":\n279:         \"\"\"\n280:         Load a profile from *path*, migrating older versions automatically.\n281:         \"\"\"\n282:         path = Path(path)\n283:         if not path.exists():\n284:             raise FileNotFoundError(f\"User profile not found: {path}\")\n285: \n286:         raw_json = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n287:         data = json.loads(raw_json)\n288: \n289:         current_version = cls.__fields__[\"schema_version\"].default\n290:         stored_version = data.get(\"schema_version\", 0)\n291:         if stored_version != current_version:\n292:             data = cls._migrate(data, from_version=stored_version)\n293: \n294:         try:\n295:             return cls.parse_obj(data)\n296:         except ValidationError as exc:  # pragma: no cover\n297:             raise ValueError(\n298:                 f\"Corrupted profile '{path}':\\n{exc}\"\n299:             ) from exc\n300: \n301:     # Migration pipeline ......................................................\n302: \n303:     @staticmethod\n304:     def _migrate(payload: Dict[str, Any], *, from_version: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n305:         \"\"\"\n306:         Upgrade *payload* to the latest schema version.\n307: \n308:         Every `if` block is responsible for migrating *from* a specific older\n309:         revision *to* the immediate next one.\n310:         \"\"\"\n311:         migrated = dict(payload)  # shallow copy\n312: \n313:         if from_version < 1:\n314:             # \u2013\u2013\u2013 v0 \u279c v1 \u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\u2013\n315:             #  \u2022 Ensure `display_name` exists (fallback to local-part of e-mail)\n316:             #\n317:             migrated.setdefault(\n318:                 \"display_name\",\n319:                 migrated.get(\"email\", \"User\").split(\"@\")[0].title(),\n320:             )\n321:             migrated[\"schema_version\"] = 1\n322: \n323:         return migrated\n324: \n325:     # Convenience API .........................................................\n326: \n327:     def update_preferences(self, **changes: Any) -> None:\n328:         \"\"\"\n329:         Patch top-level keys of :class:`UserPreferences`.\n330: \n331:         Examples\n332:         --------\n333:         >>> user.update_preferences(theme=ThemeSettings(name=\"Solarized\"))\n334:         \"\"\"\n335:         for key, value in changes.items():\n336:             if not hasattr(self.preferences, key):\n337:                 raise AttributeError(f\"Unknown preference section: {key!r}\")\n338:             setattr(self.preferences, key, value)\n339: \n340:         # Re-validate to bump `updated_at`\n341:         self.__dict__[\"updated_at\"] = datetime.utcnow()\n342:         self.preferences.last_updated = self.updated_at\n343: ```",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-benchmarks/locobench-python_desktop_development_expert_021@16180b273d43410fcc114af7997ce2d2b28e392e/-/blob/flockdesk/shared/schemas/user_profile.py"
  }
}

21. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input

  "path": "flockdesk/modules/presence/model/user_status.py",
