34. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/flockdesk/core/app.py"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```python\n\"\"\"\nflockdesk.core.app\n==================\n\nThe central orchestration runtime for the FlockDesk desktop suite.\nResponsible for:\n\n* Application-wide lifecycle & graceful shutdown\n* Global event-bus for intra-service communication\n* Plugin discovery, dependency wiring and hot-reload hooks\n* Persisted user settings & dynamic preference updates\n* Crash-reporting funnel to Sentry ( sandboxed per-service )\n* Asyncio <-> Qt/GUI message-pump coherency\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport asyncio\nimport importlib\nimport importlib.util\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport os\nimport signal\nimport sys\nimport traceback\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom types import ModuleType\nfrom typing import (\n    Any,\n    Awaitable,\n    Callable,\n    Coroutine,\n    Dict,\n    Iterable,\n    List,\n    Optional,\n    Protocol,\n    Set,\n    Tuple,\n    Union,\n)\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Optional / soft dependencies                                               #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\ntry:\n    from PySide6.QtCore import QCoreApplication, QObject, QTimer  # type: ignore\nexcept ModuleNotFoundError:  # Headless / non-GUI environments\n    QCoreApplication = None  # type: ignore\n    QObject = object  # type: ignore\n    QTimer = None  # type: ignore\n\ntry:\n    import appdirs  # type: ignore\nexcept ModuleNotFoundError:\n    appdirs = None\n\ntry:\n    import sentry_sdk  # type: ignore\nexcept ModuleNotFoundError:\n    sentry_sdk = None  # noqa: N816\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Logging setup                                                              #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nlogger = logging.getLogger(\"flockdesk.core\")\nlogger.setLevel(logging.INFO)\n\n_handler = logging.StreamHandler(stream=sys.stdout)\n_handler.setFormatter(\n    logging.Formatter(\"%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(name)s: %(message)s\")\n)\nlogger.addHandler(_handler)\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Typing                                                                     #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nJSON_T = Union[str, int, float, bool, None, Dict[str, \"JSON_T\"], List[\"JSON_T\"]]\n\n\nclass Event:\n    \"\"\"\n    A lightweight event envelope exchanged over the FlockDesk event bus.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    __slots__ = (\"topic\", \"payload\", \"meta\")\n\n    def __init__(self, topic: str, payload: Any = None, **meta: Any) -> None:\n        self.topic: str = topic\n        self.payload: Any = payload\n        self.meta: Dict[str, Any] = meta\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Convenience helpers                                                   #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def __repr__(self) -> str:  # pragma: no cover\n        return f\"Event({self.topic!r}, payload={self.payload!r}, meta={self.meta!r})\"\n\n\nListener = Callable[[Event], Union[None, Awaitable[None]]]\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Event Bus Implementation                                                   #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nclass EventBus:\n    \"\"\"\n    A minimal in-process publish/subscribe mechanism.\n\n    The bus is intentionally *na\u00efve* but good enough for inter-plugin traffic.\n    More advanced requirements (wildcards, QoS, type routing) can be layered\n    on top by dedicated broker services.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self._subscribers: Dict[str, Set[Listener]] = {}\n        self._loop: asyncio.AbstractEventLoop = asyncio.get_event_loop()\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Subscription                                                          #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def subscribe(self, topic: str, listener: Listener) -> None:\n        listeners = self._subscribers.setdefault(topic, set())\n        listeners.add(listener)\n        logger.debug(\"Listener %s subscribed to topic %s\", listener, topic)\n\n    def unsubscribe(self, topic: str, listener: Listener) -> None:\n        try:\n            self._subscribers[topic].discard(listener)\n            logger.debug(\"Listener %s unsubscribed from topic %s\", listener, topic)\n        except KeyError:\n            logger.warning(\"Attempted to unsubscribe unknown topic %s\", topic)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Publishing                                                            #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def publish(self, topic: str, payload: Any = None, **meta: Any) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Schedule the dispatch of an event to all interested listeners.\n        \"\"\"\n        event = Event(topic, payload, **meta)\n        logger.debug(\"Publishing event %s\", event)\n        listeners = self._subscribers.get(topic, set()).copy()\n\n        for listener in listeners:\n            try:\n                result = listener(event)\n                if asyncio.iscoroutine(result):\n                    # Fire-and-forget, but capture exceptions.\n                    task = self._loop.create_task(result)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n                    task.add_done_callback(self._capture_async_exception)\n            except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n                logger.exception(\n                    \"Synchronous listener %s crashed while handling %s: %s\",\n                    listener,\n                    topic,\n                    exc,\n                )\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Helpers                                                               #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def _capture_async_exception(self, task: asyncio.Task[Any]) -> None:  # noqa: D401\n        \"\"\"\n        Funnel unhandled exceptions from background tasks into the logger.\n        \"\"\"\n        if task.cancelled():\n            return\n        if exc := task.exception():\n            logger.exception(\"Async listener raised: %s\", exc)\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Plugin System                                                              #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nclass Plugin(Protocol):\n    \"\"\"\n    Public interface that all plugins must comply with.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    name: str\n    version: str\n\n    def setup(self, bus: EventBus) -> None: ...\n    async def start(self) -> None: ...\n    async def shutdown(self) -> None: ...\n\n\nclass PluginLoader:\n    \"\"\"\n    Discover & instantiate plugins.\n\n    *User* plugins are loaded from the following search order:\n\n    1. `$FLOCKDESK_PLUGINS` (semicolon separated list of paths)\n    2. `~/.flockdesk/plugins`\n    3. `<install-root>/flockdesk/plugins`\n    \"\"\"\n\n    SEARCH_LOCATIONS: Tuple[Path, ...] = (\n        Path(os.getenv(\"FLOCKDESK_PLUGINS\", \"\")),\n        Path.home() / \".flockdesk\" / \"plugins\",\n        Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / \"plugins\",\n    )\n\n    def __init__(self, bus: EventBus) -> None:\n        self._bus = bus\n        self._plugins: List[Plugin] = []\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Discovery                                                             #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def discover(self) -> List[Plugin]:\n        logger.info(\"Discovering plugins \u2026\")\n        for location in self.SEARCH_LOCATIONS:\n            if not location or not location.exists():\n                continue\n            for path in location.glob(\"*/__init__.py\"):\n                try:\n                    plugin = self._load_module(path)\n                    self._plugins.append(plugin)\n                    logger.info(\"Loaded plugin: %s %s\", plugin.name, plugin.version)\n                except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n                    logger.error(\n                        \"Failed to load plugin at %s:\\n%s\",\n                        path,\n                        traceback.format_exc(),\n                    )\n        return self._plugins\n\n    def _load_module(self, init_file: Path) -> Plugin:\n        module_name = f\"flockdesk.plugin.{init_file.parent.name}\"\n        spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(module_name, init_file)\n        if not spec or not spec.loader:\n            raise ImportError(f\"Cannot build spec for {init_file}\")\n        module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)\n        sys.modules[module_name] = module\n        spec.loader.exec_module(module)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n\n        if not hasattr(module, \"Plugin\"):\n            raise AttributeError(\n                f\"{module_name} does not expose a 'Plugin' class\"\n            )  # noqa: TRY003\n\n        plugin_cls = getattr(module, \"Plugin\")\n        plugin: Plugin = plugin_cls()  # type: ignore[call-arg]\n\n        plugin.setup(self._bus)\n        return plugin\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Lifecycle                                                             #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    async def start_all(self) -> None:\n        for plugin in self._plugins:\n            try:\n                await plugin.start()\n                logger.info(\"Plugin started: %s\", plugin.name)\n            except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n                logger.exception(\"Plugin %s failed to start\", plugin.name)\n\n    async def shutdown_all(self) -> None:\n        for plugin in reversed(self._plugins):\n            try:\n                await plugin.shutdown()\n                logger.info(\"Plugin stopped: %s\", plugin.name)\n            except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n                logger.exception(\"Plugin %s failed to shutdown\", plugin.name)\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Settings / Configuration                                                   #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nclass SettingsManager:\n    \"\"\"\n    Persisted key/value store backed by a JSON blob in the user's config dir.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    FILE_NAME = \"settings.json\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self._data: Dict[str, JSON_T] = {}\n        self._path = self._resolve_path()\n        self._load()\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Public API                                                            #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def get(self, key: str, default: JSON_T | None = None) -> JSON_T | None:\n        return self._data.get(key, default)\n\n    def set(self, key: str, value: JSON_T) -> None:\n        self._data[key] = value\n        self._save_async()\n\n    def all(self) -> Dict[str, JSON_T]:\n        return dict(self._data)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Persistence                                                           #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def _load(self) -> None:\n        try:\n            if self._path.exists():\n                self._data = json.loads(self._path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n                logger.debug(\"Settings loaded from %s\", self._path)\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError) as exc:\n            logger.warning(\"Failed to load settings (%s), starting fresh\", exc)\n            self._data = {}\n\n    def _save_async(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Off-load writing to disk to avoid blocking the GUI / event-loop.\n        \"\"\"\n\n        async def _writer() -> None:\n            try:\n                self._path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n                self._path.write_text(json.dumps(self._data, indent=2), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n                logger.debug(\"Settings persisted to %s\", self._path)\n            except OSError as exc:\n                logger.error(\"Could not persist settings: %s\", exc)\n\n        asyncio.get_event_loop().create_task(_writer())\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Helpers                                                               #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    @staticmethod\n    def _resolve_path() -> Path:\n        if appdirs:\n            cfg_dir = Path(appdirs.user_config_dir(\"FlockDesk\", \"Flockware\"))\n        else:\n            cfg_dir = Path.home() / \".config\" / \"flockdesk\"\n        return cfg_dir / SettingsManager.FILE_NAME\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Crash / Diagnostics                                                        #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nclass CrashReporter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Thin wrapper around Sentry so we can easily replace/disable later on.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, dsn: Optional[str] = None) -> None:\n        self._enabled: bool = False\n        if dsn and sentry_sdk:\n            sentry_sdk.init(dsn=dsn, release=\"flockdesk@1.0.0\")\n            self._enabled = True\n            logger.info(\"Crash reporting enabled\")\n        elif dsn:\n            logger.warning(\"sentry-sdk not installed, crash reporting disabled\")\n\n    async def capture_exception(self, exc: BaseException) -> None:\n        if self._enabled:\n            sentry_sdk.capture_exception(exc)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Core Application                                                           #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\nclass FlockDeskCoreApp(QObject if QObject is not object else object):  # type: ignore[misc]\n    \"\"\"\n    Central bootstrap for FlockDesk.\n\n    Spins up the event-bus, plugin runtime, setting manager, and bridges\n    between the asyncio reactor and Qt's message-pump if available.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, *, headless: bool = False) -> None:\n        if not headless and QCoreApplication is None:\n            raise RuntimeError(\n                \"PySide6 not installed \u2011 cannot run in GUI mode. \"\n                \"Pass `headless=True` to run without Qt.\"\n            )\n\n        super().__init__()  # type: ignore[misc]\n        self._headless = headless\n        self._loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()\n        asyncio.set_event_loop(self._loop)\n\n        self.bus = EventBus()\n        self.settings = SettingsManager()\n        self.crash = CrashReporter(\n            dsn=self.settings.get(\"sentry_dsn\")  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n        )\n        self._plugin_loader = PluginLoader(self.bus)\n\n        self._closing = asyncio.Event()\n\n        # Connect SIGINT / SIGTERM\n        for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):\n            signal.signal(sig, self._signal_handler)\n\n        if not self._headless:\n            self._qt_app: QCoreApplication = QCoreApplication(sys.argv)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n            # Pump asyncio via Qt's event loop every 15ms\n            QTimer.singleShot(0, self._qt_asyncio_integration_tick)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Bootstrap                                                             #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def run(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Synchronous entry-point; block until shutdown completes.\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.info(\"Starting FlockDesk \u2026\")\n        try:\n            self._loop.run_until_complete(self._async_main())\n        finally:\n            self._loop.run_until_complete(self._loop.shutdown_asyncgens())\n            self._loop.close()\n            logger.info(\"FlockDesk stopped\")\n\n    async def _async_main(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Coroutine that manages the asynchronous lifecycle.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Plugin discovery/startup\n        self._plugin_loader.discover()\n        await self._plugin_loader.start_all()\n\n        # Run until a shutdown signal is received\n        await self._closing.wait()\n\n        # Tear-down\n        await self._plugin_loader.shutdown_all()\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Shutdown & Signals                                                    #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def _signal_handler(self, signum: int, _frame: Any | None = None) -> None:\n        logger.info(\"Received signal %s, shutting down \u2026\", signum)\n        self._loop.call_soon_threadsafe(self._closing.set)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Qt / Async Integration                                                #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def _qt_asyncio_integration_tick(self) -> None:  # pragma: no cover\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute all asyncio tasks waiting on the loop & re-schedule next tick.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            self._loop.call_soon(self._loop.stop)\n            self._loop.run_forever()\n        except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n            logger.exception(\"Error during asyncio-Qt integration tick: %s\", exc)\n            self._loop.create_task(self.crash.capture_exception(exc))\n        finally:\n            # Re-schedule as long as the application is alive\n            if not self._closing.is_set():\n                QTimer.singleShot(15, self._qt_asyncio_integration_tick)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Convenience helpers                                                   #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    def quit(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Can be called by plugins/UI to request a graceful shutdown.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._closing.set()\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Entrypoint                                                                 #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\ndef _main() -> None:  # pragma: no cover\n    \"\"\"\n    CLI bootstrap mainly for development/testing purposes.\n    \"\"\"\n    import argparse\n\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Launch FlockDesk core service\")\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--headless\",\n        action=\"store_true\",\n        help=\"Run without Qt (no GUI) \u2013 suitable for CI or server mode.\",\n    )\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    app = FlockDeskCoreApp(headless=args.headless)\n    app.run()\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    _main()\n```",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/flockdesk/core/app.py",
    "numLines": 490,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 490
  },
  "type": "text"
}
35. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/flockdesk/modules/chat/viewmodel/chat_vm.py"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```python\n\"\"\"\nFlockDesk \u2013 Chat Module\nView-Model (MVVM) for the Chat micro-front-end.\n\nThis module owns the presentation-ready state of a single chat channel.\nIt keeps the Qt View in sync with the domain layer through an internal\nevent-bus, handles optimistic UI updates, provides command objects for\nthe Controller, and persists transient UI data such as unsent drafts.\n\nAuthor: FlockDesk Core Team\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport threading\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional\n\nfrom PySide6 import QtCore\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# External / Project-wide Dependencies\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\ntry:\n    from flockdesk.core.events import EventBus, Event  # type: ignore\n    from flockdesk.core.settings import settings  # type: ignore\n    from flockdesk.core.exceptions import ChatSendError  # type: ignore\nexcept ModuleNotFoundError:  # Fallbacks for static type checkers / docs\n    EventBus = Any  # pragma: no cover\n    Event = Any  # pragma: no cover\n    settings = {}  # pragma: no cover\n    ChatSendError = Exception  # pragma: no cover\n\n\n_log = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# Data Models\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n@dataclass(slots=True, frozen=True)\nclass ChatMessage:\n    \"\"\"Immutably represents a message entity in the chat session.\"\"\"\n\n    msg_id: str\n    channel_id: str\n    sender_id: str\n    sender_display: str\n    content: str\n    timestamp: datetime\n    edited: bool = False\n    is_outgoing: bool = False\n    attachments: List[Path] = field(default_factory=list)\n\n    def serialize(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        return {\n            \"msg_id\": self.msg_id,\n            \"channel_id\": self.channel_id,\n            \"sender_id\": self.sender_id,\n            \"sender_display\": self.sender_display,\n            \"content\": self.content,\n            \"timestamp\": self.timestamp.isoformat(),\n            \"edited\": self.edited,\n            \"is_outgoing\": self.is_outgoing,\n            \"attachments\": [str(p) for p in self.attachments],\n        }\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# Chat View-Model\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\nclass ChatViewModel(QtCore.QObject):\n    \"\"\"\n    Acts as the View-Model for a single chat channel.\n\n    Responsibilities\n    ----------------\n    \u2022 Keep a local in-memory list of ChatMessage objects\n    \u2022 Subscribe to the domain event-bus for incoming/outgoing messages\n    \u2022 Expose Qt signals so Views can bind via Qt\u2019s signal/slot system\n    \u2022 Persist unsent drafts & restore them on reboot\n    \"\"\"\n\n    # -------- Qt Signals ----------------------------------------------------#\n    messageAdded = QtCore.Signal(ChatMessage)  # new message appended\n    messageReplaced = QtCore.Signal(ChatMessage)  # edited or updated\n    typingStatusChanged = QtCore.Signal(str, bool)  # user_id, is_typing\n    errorOccurred = QtCore.Signal(str)  # human-readable error string\n    historyLoaded = QtCore.Signal(list)  # list[ChatMessage]\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    def __init__(self, channel_id: str, event_bus: EventBus, parent=None) -> None:\n        super().__init__(parent)\n        self._channel_id: str = channel_id\n        self._event_bus: EventBus = event_bus\n        self._messages: list[ChatMessage] = []\n\n        # drafts/<user>/<channel>.json\n        self._draft_path: Path = (\n            Path(settings.get(\"user_data_dir\", \"~/.flockdesk\")).expanduser()\n            / \"drafts\"\n            / f\"{settings.get('user_id', 'anonymous')}\"\n            / f\"{channel_id}.json\"\n        )\n\n        self._typing_users: Dict[str, QtCore.QTimer] = {}\n        self._lock = threading.RLock()\n\n        self._register_event_subscriptions()\n        self._restore_draft_async()\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # API surface for Controllers\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    def history(self) -> list[ChatMessage]:\n        \"\"\"Return a copy of the current message history.\"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            return list(self._messages)\n\n    def send_message(self, content: str, attachments: Optional[List[Path]] = None) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Send a message to the current channel.\n\n        The actual network interaction is delegated to the domain layer\n        via the event-bus. We optimistically append the message locally\n        and mark it as `is_outgoing=True`; once the server acknowledges\n        (or rejects) the message we will replace/update it.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not content and not attachments:\n            _log.debug(\"Empty message suppressed\")\n            return\n\n        msg_id = f\"tmp-{datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc).timestamp()}\"\n        attachments = attachments or []\n        outgoing = ChatMessage(\n            msg_id=msg_id,\n            channel_id=self._channel_id,\n            sender_id=settings.get(\"user_id\", \"anonymous\"),\n            sender_display=settings.get(\"user_display\", \"Me\"),\n            content=content,\n            timestamp=datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc),\n            is_outgoing=True,\n            attachments=attachments,\n        )\n\n        self._append_message(outgoing)\n\n        # Publish to domain event-bus (fire-and-forget)\n        _log.debug(\"Publishing OutgoingMessage event: %s\", outgoing.msg_id)\n        self._event_bus.publish(\n            Event(\n                topic=\"chat.outgoing\",\n                payload=outgoing.serialize(),\n                replayable=False,\n            )\n        )\n\n        # Clear persisted draft for this channel\n        self._clear_draft_file()\n\n    def start_typing(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Publish typing indicator event when user starts typing.\"\"\"\n        self._event_bus.publish(\n            Event(\n                topic=\"chat.typing\",\n                payload={\"channel_id\": self._channel_id, \"user_id\": settings.get(\"user_id\")},\n            )\n        )\n\n    def persist_draft(self, draft_text: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Persist unsent draft on disk.\n\n        Called from the Controller on text-changed events.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not draft_text.strip():\n            self._clear_draft_file()\n            return\n\n        self._draft_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        try:\n            self._draft_path.write_text(\n                json.dumps({\"draft\": draft_text, \"timestamp\": datetime.now().isoformat()}),\n                encoding=\"utf-8\",\n            )\n            _log.debug(\"Draft persisted to %s\", self._draft_path)\n        except OSError as exc:\n            _log.warning(\"Failed to write draft file: %s\", exc)\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Internal helpers\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    def _register_event_subscriptions(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Subscribe to the global EventBus for chat-related events.\n\n        We keep the subscription tokens so we can unsubscribe\n        when the View-Model is deleted / the window is closed.\n        \"\"\"\n\n        # Incoming messages\n        self._event_bus.subscribe(\n            \"chat.incoming\",\n            self._on_incoming_message,\n            group=f\"vm_{self._channel_id}\",\n        )\n\n        # Delivery acknowledgements for optimistic updates\n        self._event_bus.subscribe(\n            \"chat.outgoing.confirmation\",\n            self._on_delivery_confirmation,\n            group=f\"vm_{self._channel_id}\",\n        )\n\n        # Typing indicators\n        self._event_bus.subscribe(\n            \"chat.typing\",\n            self._on_typing_indicator,\n            group=f\"vm_{self._channel_id}\",\n        )\n\n        # Synchronised edits / deletions\n        self._event_bus.subscribe(\n            \"chat.message.updated\",\n            self._on_remote_update,\n            group=f\"vm_{self._channel_id}\",\n        )\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    def _append_message(self, message: ChatMessage) -> None:\n        with self._lock:\n            self._messages.append(message)\n        self.messageAdded.emit(message)\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Event-bus callback handlers\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    def _on_incoming_message(self, event: Event) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Handle new message events from other users.\"\"\"\n        payload = event.payload\n        if payload[\"channel_id\"] != self._channel_id:\n            return\n\n        msg = ChatMessage(\n            msg_id=payload[\"msg_id\"],\n            channel_id=payload[\"channel_id\"],\n            sender_id=payload[\"sender_id\"],\n            sender_display=payload[\"sender_display\"],\n            content=payload[\"content\"],\n            timestamp=datetime.fromisoformat(payload[\"timestamp\"]),\n            attachments=[Path(p) for p in payload.get(\"attachments\", [])],\n        )\n        self._append_message(msg)\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    def _on_delivery_confirmation(self, event: Event) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Replace optimistic message with definitive server copy.\"\"\"\n        payload = event.payload\n        if payload[\"channel_id\"] != self._channel_id:\n            return\n\n        tmp_id = payload[\"tmp_id\"]\n        real_id = payload[\"msg_id\"]\n\n        with self._lock:\n            for idx, m in enumerate(self._messages):\n                if m.msg_id == tmp_id:\n                    updated = ChatMessage(\n                        msg_id=real_id,\n                        channel_id=m.channel_id,\n                        sender_id=m.sender_id,\n                        sender_display=m.sender_display,\n                        content=m.content,\n                        timestamp=datetime.fromisoformat(payload[\"timestamp\"]),\n                        is_outgoing=False,\n                        attachments=m.attachments,\n                    )\n                    self._messages[idx] = updated\n                    _log.debug(\"Optimistic message %s replaced by %s\", tmp_id, real_id)\n                    self.messageReplaced.emit(updated)\n                    break\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    def _on_typing_indicator(self, event: Event) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update typing indicator map & emit signal.\"\"\"\n        payload = event.payload\n        if payload[\"channel_id\"] != self._channel_id:\n            return\n\n        user_id = payload[\"user_id\"]\n        if user_id == settings.get(\"user_id\"):\n            return  # ignore self typing\n\n        # Reset/Start debounce timer for this user\n        timer = self._typing_users.get(user_id)\n        if timer is None:\n            timer = QtCore.QTimer(self)\n            timer.setSingleShot(True)\n            timer.timeout.connect(lambda uid=user_id: self._emit_typing_end(uid))\n            self._typing_users[user_id] = timer\n            self.typingStatusChanged.emit(user_id, True)\n\n        timer.start(3000)  # 3s window\n\n    def _emit_typing_end(self, user_id: str) -> None:\n        self.typingStatusChanged.emit(user_id, False)\n        self._typing_users.pop(user_id, None)\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    def _on_remote_update(self, event: Event) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Handle remote edits or deletions.\"\"\"\n        payload = event.payload\n        if payload[\"channel_id\"] != self._channel_id:\n            return\n\n        with self._lock:\n            for idx, m in enumerate(self._messages):\n                if m.msg_id == payload[\"msg_id\"]:\n                    updated = ChatMessage(\n                        msg_id=m.msg_id,\n                        channel_id=m.channel_id,\n                        sender_id=m.sender_id,\n                        sender_display=m.sender_display,\n                        content=payload.get(\"content\", m.content),\n                        timestamp=m.timestamp,\n                        edited=True,\n                        attachments=[Path(p) for p in payload.get(\"attachments\", [])],\n                    )\n                    self._messages[idx] = updated\n                    self.messageReplaced.emit(updated)\n                    _log.debug(\"Message %s updated from remote\", m.msg_id)\n                    break\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Draft persistence\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    def _restore_draft_async(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Restore draft in a background thread to keep UI responsive.\"\"\"\n\n        def _worker(vm: \"ChatViewModel\") -> None:\n            if vm._draft_path.exists():\n                try:\n                    data = json.loads(vm._draft_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n                    draft = data.get(\"draft\", \"\")\n                    _log.debug(\"Draft restored for channel %s\", vm._channel_id)\n                    # Emit via Qt to avoid cross-thread issues\n                    QtCore.QMetaObject.invokeMethod(\n                        vm,\n                        \"_emit_draft_restored\",\n                        QtCore.Qt.QueuedConnection,\n                        QtCore.Q_ARG(str, draft),\n                    )\n                except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:\n                    _log.warning(\"Failed to restore draft: %s\", exc)\n\n        threading.Thread(target=_worker, args=(self,), daemon=True).start()\n\n    @QtCore.Slot(str)\n    def _emit_draft_restored(self, text: str) -> None:\n        self.historyLoaded.emit([])  # just to notify view to attach draft text\n\n    def _clear_draft_file(self) -> None:\n        try:\n            if self._draft_path.exists():\n                self._draft_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)\n                _log.debug(\"Draft cleared for channel %s\", self._channel_id)\n        except OSError as exc:\n            _log.warning(\"Failed to clear draft file: %s\", exc)\n\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Qt Object Life-Cycle\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    def __del__(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Ensure we cleanly unsubscribe from the EventBus.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            self._event_bus.unsubscribe_group(f\"vm_{self._channel_id}\")\n        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n            pass  # During interpreter shutdown objects may be gone\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# Factory (Dependency Injection ready)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\ndef create_chat_viewmodel(channel_id: str, event_bus: EventBus) -> ChatViewModel:\n    \"\"\"\n    Factory helper so the app\u2019s DI container can easily inject dependencies.\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    ChatViewModel\n        Ready-to-use instance with subscriptions in place.\n    \"\"\"\n    vm = ChatViewModel(channel_id=channel_id, event_bus=event_bus)\n    return vm\n```",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/flockdesk/modules/chat/viewmodel/chat_vm.py",
    "numLines": 400,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 400
  },
  "type": "text"
}
36. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/flockdesk/modules/presence/viewmodel/presence_vm.py"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```python\n\"\"\"\nFlockDesk \u2013 Presence Module: View-Model Layer\n============================================\n\nThis file holds the *view-model* that mediates between the presence\nbackend-service (listening on the internal event-bus) and the Qt views /\nQML components that render the roster.\n\nThe module is 100 % standalone and can be imported by tests without the\nfull desktop runtime.  It exposes one public symbol:\n\n    PresenceViewModel  \u2013 A Qt QObject that wraps a\n                         QAbstractListModel with presence data and\n                         exposes commands for the UI.\n\nAuthor  : FlockDesk Core Team\nLicense : MIT (see project root)\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport asyncio\nimport datetime as _dt\nimport logging\nimport types\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom enum import Enum, auto\nfrom typing import Dict, List, Optional\n\nfrom PySide6 import QtCore\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# 3rd-party / infrastructure stubs\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# NOTE:\n# The real application injects an actual event-bus implementation that\n# resembles the minimal API below.  We ship a tiny duck-type fallback so\n# the module does not crash when imported in isolation.\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n\nclass _DummySubscription:  # pragma: no cover \u2013 testing helper\n    def __init__(self, topic: str, cb):\n        self._topic, self._cb = topic, cb\n\n    def dispose(self):\n        \"\"\"Mimic an un-subscribe operation.\"\"\"\n        logging.getLogger(__name__).debug(\"Unsubscribed from topic %s\", self._topic)\n\n\nclass EventBus:  # pragma: no cover \u2013 placeholder\n    \"\"\"Very small subset of the real bus used in production.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self._topics: Dict[str, List] = {}\n\n    def publish(self, topic: str, payload: object) -> None:\n        for cb in self._topics.get(topic, []):\n            try:\n                cb(payload)  # noqa: B023 \u2013 sync callback in fake bus\n            except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n                logging.exception(\"Unhandled error in bus subscriber for topic %s\", topic)\n\n    def subscribe(self, topic: str, cb) -> _DummySubscription:\n        self._topics.setdefault(topic, []).append(cb)\n        return _DummySubscription(topic, cb)\n\n\n# When FlockDesk boots, the DI-container injects a **shared** bus\n# instance.  Until then, we fall back to a local private one.\n_default_bus = EventBus()\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# Domain model\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n\nclass PresenceStatus(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Canonical presence states understood by the desktop suite.\"\"\"\n\n    ONLINE = auto()\n    AWAY = auto()\n    BUSY = auto()\n    OFFLINE = auto()\n\n    def __str__(self) -> str:  # Readable for QML\n        return self.name.lower()\n\n\n@dataclass(slots=True)\nclass PresenceEntry:\n    user_id: str\n    display_name: str\n    status: PresenceStatus = PresenceStatus.OFFLINE\n    last_active: _dt.datetime = field(default_factory=_dt.datetime.utcnow)\n\n    def to_qvariant_map(self) -> Dict[str, object]:\n        \"\"\"Return values convertible by Qt for use in QML/JS.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"userId\": self.user_id,\n            \"displayName\": self.display_name,\n            \"status\": str(self.status),\n            \"lastActive\": self.last_active.isoformat(),\n        }\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# QAbstractListModel representing the roster\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n\nclass PresenceListModel(QtCore.QAbstractListModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Thin wrapper between a list[PresenceEntry] and Qt's model/view API.\n    Emits the usual model signals, hence updates appear instantaneously\n    on bound QML ListViews or QtWidgets views.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    # Qt Role numeric IDs\n    USER_ID_ROLE = QtCore.Qt.UserRole + 1\n    DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE = USER_ID_ROLE + 1\n    STATUS_ROLE = DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE + 1\n    LAST_ACTIVE_ROLE = STATUS_ROLE + 1\n\n    _ROLE_NAMES = {\n        USER_ID_ROLE: b\"userId\",\n        DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE: b\"displayName\",\n        STATUS_ROLE: b\"status\",\n        LAST_ACTIVE_ROLE: b\"lastActive\",\n    }\n\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Qt mandatory overrides\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n    def __init__(self, parent: Optional[QtCore.QObject] = None):\n        super().__init__(parent)\n        self._roster: List[PresenceEntry] = []\n\n    # Qt interface \u2013 meta\n    def roleNames(self) -> Dict[int, bytes]:  # noqa: N802 \u2013 Qt API\n        return self._ROLE_NAMES\n\n    # Qt interface \u2013 data\n    def rowCount(self, parent=QtCore.QModelIndex()) -> int:  # noqa: N802\n        return 0 if parent.isValid() else len(self._roster)\n\n    def data(self, index: QtCore.QModelIndex, role: int = QtCore.Qt.DisplayRole):  # noqa: N802\n        if not index.isValid() or not (0 <= index.row() < len(self._roster)):\n            return None\n\n        entry = self._roster[index.row()]\n\n        if role == self.USER_ID_ROLE:\n            return entry.user_id\n        if role == self.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE:\n            return entry.display_name\n        if role == self.STATUS_ROLE:\n            return str(entry.status)\n        if role == self.LAST_ACTIVE_ROLE:\n            return entry.last_active.isoformat()\n\n        return None\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Mutating helpers exposed to the VM\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n    def upsert_entries(self, entries: List[PresenceEntry]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Insert or update one or many presence rows.\n\n        The algorithm is O(N*M) but roster lists are typically short\n        (max ~200 members).  Optimise later if needed.\n        \"\"\"\n        for entry in entries:\n            try:\n                idx = next(i for i, e in enumerate(self._roster) if e.user_id == entry.user_id)\n                if self._roster[idx] != entry:\n                    self._roster[idx] = entry\n                    top_left = self.index(idx, 0)\n                    self.dataChanged.emit(top_left, top_left, self._ROLE_NAMES.keys())\n            except StopIteration:\n                # New participant \u2013 append\n                self.beginInsertRows(QtCore.QModelIndex(), len(self._roster), len(self._roster))\n                self._roster.append(entry)\n                self.endInsertRows()\n\n    def remove_user(self, user_id: str) -> None:\n        try:\n            idx = next(i for i, e in enumerate(self._roster) if e.user_id == user_id)\n        except StopIteration:\n            return  # nothing to do\n        self.beginRemoveRows(QtCore.QModelIndex(), idx, idx)\n        del self._roster[idx]\n        self.endRemoveRows()\n\n    # convenience for QML\n    @QtCore.Slot(str, result=int)\n    def row_for_user(self, user_id: str) -> int:  # noqa: D401\n        \"\"\"Return row index for a given user or \u20111.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return next(i for i, entry in enumerate(self._roster) if entry.user_id == user_id)\n        except StopIteration:\n            return -1\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# Presence View-Model\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n\nclass PresenceViewModel(QtCore.QObject):\n    \"\"\"\n    Qt QObject acting as the binding layer between presence model\n    changes and the GUI.  It subscribes to an *EventBus*, receives\n    events like ``presence.updated`` or ``presence.left``, and updates\n    an internal QAbstractListModel exposed to QML.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    presenceListChanged = QtCore.Signal()  # emitted when the model pointer itself changes\n    errorOccurred = QtCore.Signal(str)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Construction / DI\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        bus: EventBus | None = None,\n        *,\n        current_user_id: Optional[str] = None,\n        parent: Optional[QtCore.QObject] = None,\n    ):\n        super().__init__(parent)\n\n        self._log = logging.getLogger(self.__class__.__name__)\n        self._bus: EventBus = bus or _default_bus\n        self._current_user_id = current_user_id\n\n        # Our roster list-model\n        self._model = PresenceListModel(self)\n\n        # Keep references to bus subscriptions so we can dispose later.\n        self._subscriptions: List[_DummySubscription] = []\n\n        # Async tasks we spawn (cancel on destruction)\n        self._tasks: List[asyncio.Task] = []\n\n        self._wire_bus()\n        self._bootstrap_roster()\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Qt property for QML\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n    @QtCore.Property(QtCore.QObject, notify=presenceListChanged)\n    def presenceModel(self) -> PresenceListModel:  # noqa: D401\n        \"\"\"Return the QAbstractListModel consumed by the view.\"\"\"\n        return self._model\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Public API\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n    @QtCore.Slot(result=str)\n    def currentUserId(self) -> str:  # noqa: D401, N802 \u2013 match Qt naming\n        \"\"\"Expose the logged-in user's ID to the view.\"\"\"\n        return self._current_user_id or \"\"\n\n    @QtCore.Slot(str, str)\n    def setStatus(self, user_id: str, raw_status: str) -> None:  # noqa: N802\n        \"\"\"\n        Command from the UI to update somebody's presence manually.\n        Usually only called for *self*.  Publishes an event so the\n        backend broadcasts to everybody else.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            status = PresenceStatus[raw_status.upper()]\n        except KeyError as exc:\n            self.errorOccurred.emit(f\"Invalid status: {raw_status}\")\n            self._log.warning(\"Rejected invalid presence status %s\", raw_status)\n            return\n\n        payload = {\n            \"user_id\": user_id,\n            \"status\": status.name,\n            \"timestamp\": _dt.datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n        }\n        self._bus.publish(\"presence.set_status\", payload)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Private helpers \u2013 bus / async\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n    def _wire_bus(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Subscribe to presence events on the internal event-bus.\"\"\"\n        self._subscriptions.append(self._bus.subscribe(\"presence.snapshot\", self._on_snapshot))\n        self._subscriptions.append(self._bus.subscribe(\"presence.updated\", self._on_presence_updated))\n        self._subscriptions.append(self._bus.subscribe(\"presence.left\", self._on_presence_left))\n\n    def _bootstrap_roster(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Ask the backend for the initial roster list via RPC-style\n        request/response.  We use asyncio so as not to block the Qt loop.\n        \"\"\"\n\n        async def request_snapshot():\n            try:\n                # fire-and-wait pattern: publish request, listen for snapshot\n                # The backend replies on the same `presence.snapshot` topic\n                self._bus.publish(\"presence.request_snapshot\", {\"requester\": self._current_user_id})\n                # DONE: nothing, listener will receive snapshot asynchronously\n            except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except\n                self._log.exception(\"Failed to request presence snapshot\")\n                self.errorOccurred.emit(\"Presence initialisation failed\")\n\n        task = asyncio.create_task(request_snapshot(), name=\"presence.bootstrap\")\n        self._tasks.append(task)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Bus event callbacks \u2013 sync context (fake bus).\n    # In production they run on a background thread; we therefore use\n    # Qt's `invokeMethod` to marshal into the main thread.\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n    # The real bus dispatches events on worker threads.  Qt objects are\n    # not thread-safe; we hop back to the main thread via single-shot\n    # queued connections.  `QtCore.QMetaObject.invokeMethod` is used\n    # instead of Signals for performance / testability.\n    def _sync_to_qt(self, fn: types.FunctionType, /, *args):\n        QtCore.QMetaObject.invokeMethod(\n            self,\n            lambda: fn(*args),  # type: ignore[arg-type]  # pyqt signature magic\n            QtCore.Qt.QueuedConnection,\n        )\n\n    # Snapshot \u2013 full presence list\n    def _on_snapshot(self, payload):\n        self._log.debug(\"Received presence.snapshot %s\", payload)\n        entries = [\n            PresenceEntry(\n                user_id=e[\"user_id\"],\n                display_name=e[\"display_name\"],\n                status=PresenceStatus[e[\"status\"].upper()],\n                last_active=_dt.datetime.fromisoformat(e[\"last_active\"]),\n            )\n            for e in payload.get(\"members\", [])\n            if \"user_id\" in e\n        ]\n        self._sync_to_qt(self._model.upsert_entries, entries)\n\n    # Incremental updates\n    def _on_presence_updated(self, payload):\n        self._log.debug(\"presence.updated %s\", payload)\n        entry = PresenceEntry(\n            user_id=payload[\"user_id\"],\n            display_name=payload.get(\"display_name\", payload[\"user_id\"]),\n            status=PresenceStatus[payload[\"status\"].upper()],\n            last_active=_dt.datetime.utcnow(),\n        )\n        self._sync_to_qt(self._model.upsert_entries, [entry])\n\n    # User left \u2013 remove from list\n    def _on_presence_left(self, payload):\n        self._log.debug(\"presence.left %s\", payload)\n        self._sync_to_qt(self._model.remove_user, payload[\"user_id\"])\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Cleanup\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n\n    def __del__(self):\n        for sub in self._subscriptions:\n            try:\n                sub.dispose()\n            except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n                self._log.warning(\"Failed to dispose subscription %s\", sub, exc_info=True)\n        for task in self._tasks:\n            task.cancel()\n```",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/flockdesk/modules/presence/viewmodel/presence_vm.py",
    "numLines": 381,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 381
  },
  "type": "text"
}
37. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/project/flockdesk/modules/presence/view/presence_widget.py"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "```python\n\"\"\"\nflockdesk.modules.presence.view.presence_widget\n------------------------------------------------\nQt / PySide6 visual component that renders presence information for teammates\ninside the FlockDesk desktop suite.\n\nThis widget plays the \uf0b7V (View) part in the MVVM triad.  It is intentionally\n*thin*: all business-logic lives in the ViewModel and/or EventBus handlers,\nallowing us to hot-swap or unit-test the UI without touching domain code.\n\nThe widget is:\n\n    \u2022 Subscribing to the global EventBus for presence/theme updates.\n    \u2022 Displaying colleagues in a QListView with custom item-delegates so we\n      can paint avatars, names and activity indicators.\n    \u2022 Drag-and-drop aware \u2013 users can drag a colleague into another micro-UI\n      (e.g. chat or whiteboard) to open a contextual collaboration pane.\n    \u2022 Lightweight \u2013 starts instantly and survives theme changes without leaks.\n\nAuthor:  The FlockDesk team\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nimport sys\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom typing import List, Optional\n\nfrom PySide6.QtCore import (QAbstractListModel, QByteArray, QModelIndex,\n                            QObject, QPoint, Qt, Signal, Slot)\nfrom PySide6.QtGui import (QColor, QDrag, QIcon, QPainter, QPixmap,\n                           QStandardItemModel, QTextOption)\nfrom PySide6.QtWidgets import (QApplication, QHBoxLayout, QLabel, QListView,\n                               QPushButton, QSpacerItem, QStyle, QStyleOptionViewItem,\n                               QStyledItemDelegate, QVBoxLayout, QWidget)\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Optional project-local imports                                              #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\ntry:\n    from flockdesk.core.event_bus import EventBus, Topic  # type: ignore\n    from flockdesk.modules.presence.model import PresenceState  # type: ignore\n    from flockdesk.shared.theming import ThemeManager  # type: ignore\nexcept (ModuleNotFoundError, ImportError):\n    # Fallback stubs to keep linters and RT preview alive\n    class EventBus:  # noqa: D401\n        \"\"\"Very small stub when running in isolation.\"\"\"\n        @staticmethod\n        def subscribe(topic: str, listener: QObject, slot: str) -> None: ...\n        @staticmethod\n        def publish(topic: str, payload) -> None: ...\n\n    class PresenceState:  # noqa: D401\n        \"\"\"Enum stub for online/offline/etc.\"\"\"\n        ONLINE = \"online\"\n        AWAY = \"away\"\n        OFFLINE = \"offline\"\n\n    class ThemeManager:  # noqa: D401\n        \"\"\"Simplistic theme stub.\"\"\"\n        theme_changed = Signal(dict)\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Dataclasses & Models                                                        #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n@dataclass(slots=True)\nclass Colleague:\n    \"\"\"Lightweight data container for a colleague row.\"\"\"\n    uid: str\n    display_name: str\n    avatar_path: str\n    presence: PresenceState\n\n\nclass _PresenceListModel(QAbstractListModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    A trivial QAbstractListModel that exposes colleagues to the QListView.\n\n    This model is intentionally 100 % dumb: all mutations come from the\n    PresenceViewModel which will feed new lists in one go.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    # Roles used by the delegate for painting\n    UID_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 1\n    DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 2\n    AVATAR_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 3\n    PRESENCE_ROLE = Qt.UserRole + 4\n\n    _ROLE_NAMES = {\n        UID_ROLE: QByteArray(b\"uid\"),\n        DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE: QByteArray(b\"display_name\"),\n        AVATAR_ROLE: QByteArray(b\"avatar\"),\n        PRESENCE_ROLE: QByteArray(b\"presence\"),\n    }\n\n    def __init__(self, colleagues: Optional[List[Colleague]] = None, parent: QObject | None = None) -> None:\n        super().__init__(parent)\n        self._colleagues: List[Colleague] = colleagues or []\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Required overrides                                                    #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    def rowCount(self, parent: QModelIndex = QModelIndex()) -> int:  # noqa: N802\n        return 0 if parent.isValid() else len(self._colleagues)\n\n    def data(self, index: QModelIndex, role: int = Qt.DisplayRole):  # noqa: N802\n        if not index.isValid():\n            return None\n\n        try:\n            colleague = self._colleagues[index.row()]\n        except IndexError:\n            return None\n\n        if role == Qt.DisplayRole:\n            return colleague.display_name\n        if role == self.UID_ROLE:\n            return colleague.uid\n        if role == self.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE:\n            return colleague.display_name\n        if role == self.AVATAR_ROLE:\n            return colleague.avatar_path\n        if role == self.PRESENCE_ROLE:\n            return colleague.presence\n        return None\n\n    def roleNames(self) -> dict[int, QByteArray]:  # noqa: N802\n        return self._ROLE_NAMES\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Public API                                                            #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    def update_content(self, colleagues: List[Colleague]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Replace the entire model dataset in one shot.\"\"\"\n        self.beginResetModel()\n        self._colleagues = colleagues\n        self.endResetModel()\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Custom Delegate                                                             #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\nclass PresenceItemDelegate(QStyledItemDelegate):\n    \"\"\"Paints each list item with avatar, name and presence indicator icon.\"\"\"\n\n    AVATAR_SIZE = 32\n    PADDING = 6\n\n    _PRESENCE_ICONS = {\n        PresenceState.ONLINE: QIcon(\":/presence/online.svg\"),\n        PresenceState.AWAY: QIcon(\":/presence/away.svg\"),\n        PresenceState.OFFLINE: QIcon(\":/presence/offline.svg\"),\n    }\n\n    def paint(self, painter: QPainter, option: QStyleOptionViewItem, index: QModelIndex) -> None:\n        painter.save()\n        try:\n            # Draw selection highlight\n            if option.state & QStyle.State_Selected:\n                painter.fillRect(option.rect, option.palette.highlight())\n\n            # Fetch data\n            name: str = index.data(_PresenceListModel.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE)\n            avatar_path: str = index.data(_PresenceListModel.AVATAR_ROLE)\n            presence: PresenceState = index.data(_PresenceListModel.PRESENCE_ROLE)\n\n            # Avatar\n            avatar_rect = option.rect.adjusted(self.PADDING, self.PADDING,\n                                               -(option.rect.width() - self.AVATAR_SIZE - self.PADDING),\n                                               -self.PADDING)\n            avatar_pix = QPixmap(avatar_path).scaled(self.AVATAR_SIZE, self.AVATAR_SIZE,\n                                                     Qt.KeepAspectRatio, Qt.SmoothTransformation)\n            painter.drawPixmap(avatar_rect.topLeft(), avatar_pix)\n\n            # Presence indicator bottom-right of avatar\n            presence_icon: QIcon = self._PRESENCE_ICONS.get(presence, QIcon())\n            indicator_sz = 10\n            indicator_rect = avatar_rect.adjusted(self.AVATAR_SIZE - indicator_sz,\n                                                  self.AVATAR_SIZE - indicator_sz,\n                                                  0, 0)\n            presence_pix = presence_icon.pixmap(indicator_sz, indicator_sz)\n            painter.drawPixmap(indicator_rect.topLeft(), presence_pix)\n\n            # User name\n            text_rect = option.rect.adjusted(self.AVATAR_SIZE + 2 * self.PADDING,\n                                             0, -self.PADDING, 0)\n            painter.setPen(option.palette.text().color())\n            text_option = QTextOption()\n            text_option.setAlignment(Qt.AlignVCenter | Qt.AlignLeft)\n            painter.drawText(text_rect, name, text_option)\n\n        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover\n            logger.exception(\"Failed to paint presence delegate.\")\n        finally:\n            painter.restore()\n\n    def sizeHint(self, option: QStyleOptionViewItem, index: QModelIndex):  # noqa: N802\n        base = super().sizeHint(option, index)\n        return base.expandedTo(QSize(0, self.AVATAR_SIZE + 2 * self.PADDING))\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# PresenceWidget                                                              #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\nclass PresenceWidget(QWidget):\n    \"\"\"\n    QWidget exposing team presence.\n\n    Responsibilities\n    ----------------\n    1. Build UI (list-view, refresh button)\n    2. Bind ViewModel (subscribe to signals / event-bus)\n    3. Bridge drag-and-drop to external micro-front-ends\n    \"\"\"\n\n    # Emitted when the user drags a colleague over somewhere else\n    colleagueDragStarted = Signal(str)  # uid\n\n    def __init__(self, view_model: \"PresenceViewModel\", parent: QWidget | None = None) -> None:\n        super().__init__(parent)\n        self._vm = view_model\n\n        self._build_ui()\n        self._bind_view_model()\n        self._connect_theme_changes()\n\n        # Enable drag source\n        self._list_view.setDragEnabled(True)\n        self._list_view.viewport().setAcceptDrops(False)\n        self._list_view.setDefaultDropAction(Qt.IgnoreAction)\n\n        logger.debug(\"PresenceWidget initialised.\")\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    # UI                                                                  #\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n\n    def _build_ui(self) -> None:\n        self._list_view = QListView(self, objectName=\"presenceListView\")\n        self._list_view.setSpacing(4)\n        self._list_view.setUniformItemSizes(True)\n        self._list_view.setSelectionMode(QListView.NoSelection)\n        self._list_view.setItemDelegate(PresenceItemDelegate(self._list_view))\n\n        self._refresh_btn = QPushButton(self.tr(\"Refresh\"))\n        self._refresh_btn.setToolTip(self.tr(\"Force a manual presence refresh\"))\n\n        # Top bar\n        top_layout = QHBoxLayout()\n        top_layout.addWidget(QLabel(self.tr(\"Presence\")))\n        top_layout.addItem(QSpacerItem(0, 0, QSizePolicy.Expanding, QSizePolicy.Minimum))\n        top_layout.addWidget(self._refresh_btn)\n\n        # Main layout\n        layout = QVBoxLayout(self)\n        layout.addLayout(top_layout)\n        layout.addWidget(self._list_view)\n        self.setLayout(layout)\n\n        # Model\n        self._model = _PresenceListModel([])\n        self._list_view.setModel(self._model)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    # Data Binding                                                       #\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n\n    def _bind_view_model(self) -> None:\n        self._refresh_btn.clicked.connect(self._vm.refresh)\n        self._vm.presence_changed.connect(self._on_presence_changed)\n\n        # For initial data\n        self._on_presence_changed(self._vm.snapshot())\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    # Theme                                                              #\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n\n    def _connect_theme_changes(self) -> None:\n        if hasattr(ThemeManager, \"theme_changed\"):\n            ThemeManager.theme_changed.connect(self._on_theme_changed)\n\n    @Slot(dict)\n    def _on_theme_changed(self, theme_meta: dict) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Triggered when the global theme changed.\n\n        For the sake of demo we only update the list spacing/palette; more\n        thorough implementation would adapt colors in the delegate, etc.\n        \"\"\"\n        palette = self.palette()\n        palette.setColor(self.backgroundRole(), QColor(theme_meta.get(\"window\", \"#FFFFFF\")))\n        self.setPalette(palette)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    # ViewModel slot                                                     #\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n\n    @Slot(list)\n    def _on_presence_changed(self, colleagues: List[Colleague]) -> None:\n        logger.debug(\"PresenceWidget received %d colleagues.\", len(colleagues))\n        self._model.update_content(colleagues)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    # Drag-and-drop                                                      #\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n\n    def startDrag(self, supportedActions: Qt.DropActions) -> None:  # noqa: N802\n        \"\"\"Override QListView drag start behaviour.\"\"\"\n        index = self._list_view.currentIndex()\n        if not index.isValid():\n            return\n\n        uid = index.data(_PresenceListModel.UID_ROLE)\n        display_name = index.data(_PresenceListModel.DISPLAY_NAME_ROLE)\n        avatar_path = index.data(_PresenceListModel.AVATAR_ROLE)\n\n        logger.debug(\"Start drag for user %s\", display_name)\n        drag = QDrag(self)\n        mime_data = self._vm.build_mime_data(uid)\n        drag.setMimeData(mime_data)\n\n        # Pixmap for drag feedback\n        pixmap = QPixmap(avatar_path).scaled(48, 48, Qt.KeepAspectRatio, Qt.SmoothTransformation)\n        drag.setPixmap(pixmap)\n        drag.setHotSpot(QPoint(pixmap.width() // 2, pixmap.height() // 2))\n\n        drag.exec_(Qt.MoveAction)\n        self.colleagueDragStarted.emit(uid)\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# ViewModel interface placeholder                                             #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\nclass PresenceViewModel(QObject):\n    \"\"\"\n    This interface is implemented elsewhere in the presence module.\n\n    PresenceWidget only relies on the signals described below.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    presence_changed = Signal(list)  # List[Colleague]\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n    # API expected by the widget                                         #\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #\n\n    def refresh(self) -> None: ...  # noqa: D400\n\n    def snapshot(self) -> List[Colleague]:\n        \"\"\"Return current presence list synchronously.\"\"\"\n        return []\n\n    def build_mime_data(self, uid: str) -> \"QMimeData\": ...  # noqa: D401\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Debug entry-point (can be stripped for prod)                                #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":  # pragma: no cover\n    # Minimal harness to showcase the widget in isolation.\n    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)\n\n    class _DummyVM(PresenceViewModel):\n\n        def __init__(self) -> None:\n            super().__init__()\n            self._colleagues = [\n                Colleague(\"1\", \"Alice\", \":/avatars/alice.png\", PresenceState.ONLINE),\n                Colleague(\"2\", \"Bob\", \":/avatars/bob.png\", PresenceState.AWAY),\n                Colleague(\"3\", \"Charlie\", \":/avatars/charlie.png\", PresenceState.OFFLINE),\n            ]\n\n        def refresh(self) -> None:\n            logger.info(\"Refresh called from UI\")\n            # In real case we might ask server; here we just toggle Bob\n            import random\n            self._colleagues[1].presence = random.choice(\n                [PresenceState.ONLINE, PresenceState.AWAY, PresenceState.OFFLINE])\n            self.presence_changed.emit(self._colleagues)\n\n        def snapshot(self) -> List[Colleague]:\n            return self._colleagues\n\n        def build_mime_data(self, uid: str):\n            from PySide6.QtCore import QMimeData\n            md = QMimeData()\n            md.setText(uid)\n            return md\n\n    app = QApplication(sys.argv)\n    w = PresenceWidget(_DummyVM())\n    w.resize(240, 320)\n    w.show()\n    sys.exit(app.exec())\n```",
    "filePath": "/workspace/project/flockdesk/modules/presence/view/presence_widget.py",
    "numLines": 408,
    "startLine": 1,

  },
